sections.nesting:
### Laminator Pass Count

Both the 7.25" width and 10.00" height dimensions are well within the 13.5" polyester laminator. Single lamination pass in either orientation. No laminator complications.

### Print Layout and Nesting

Label: 7.25" × 10.00". Roland SG3-300 max print width: 28".

- **Orientation A** (7.25" across feed, 10.00" in feed direction): 7.25" across the 28" print bed → up to 3 labels across (3 × 7.25" = 21.75" ≤ 28").
- **Orientation B** (10.00" across feed, 7.25" in feed direction): 10.00" across the 28" bed → up to 2 labels across (2 × 10.00" = 20" ≤ 28").

Nesting is identical to 1082570 — orientation A (3-across) is the optimal layout at higher quantities; both orientations fit cleanly at low quantities.

### Material Cost Breakdown per Label

> Material cost version date: 2026-06-09. Unit costs from `governance/PRODUCTION.md` (verified 2026-04-22; laminate verified 2026-05-28). Full bleed ink rule per PRICING_RULES.md §25.

| Component | Calculation | Per-Label Cost |
|-----------|-------------|---------------|
| Orajet 3951 cast vinyl | 0.503 sq ft × $1.21/sq ft | $0.609 |
| 1-mil polyester overlaminate | 0.503 sq ft × $0.2389/sq ft | $0.120 |
| Eco-solvent ink (full bleed per §25) | 0.503 sq ft × $0.50/sq ft | $0.252 |
| Calculated total | | **$0.981** |
| Incidental buffer (conservative round-up) | | + $0.019 |
| **Canonical `material_cost_per_unit`** | | **$1.00** |

Material cost matches P/N 1082570 ($1.00/label) by direct parity — identical sq ft, identical material family, identical full bleed ink treatment under PRICING_RULES.md §25.

> **Costing-era note (2026-06-09, Session I / audit D2):** The prior filed cost ($1.33) reproduced 1082570's legacy Safety Yellow flood-coat number via an oversized ~$0.35 buffer. Rebuilt to the §25 canonical formula with the standard modest buffer, in lockstep with the 1082570 normalization. Documentation-only: no sell price, tier value, or band anchor changed.

---

sections.pricing_derivation:
> **DIRECT PARITY EXEMPTION APPLIED — NO MULTI-ROUND AI VALIDATION REQUIRED.**

>

None of these differences affect material cost, nesting, production process, or per-label labor.

**Direct parity exemption applies per PRICING_VALIDATION.md.** Tier table and material cost cloned exactly from 1082570 with no modification.

## Step 3 — Pricing Profile Band Check (Rule 15 — still applies)

The category singles band at qty 20 is **$15.43–$15.91/sq ft** (calibrated at the 0.503–1.296 sq ft range, founded by 1230820 and confirmed by 1082570).

1068270 at qty 20: $8.00 / 0.503 = **$15.91/sq ft** — exact match to 1082570; lands cleanly within the singles band.

Rule 15 satisfied. No band adjustment required.

## Step 4 — Why No Multi-Round AI Validation Was Run

Per PRICING_VALIDATION.md: "Items dimensionally and materially identical to something already quoted. Match the existing structure and move on. Note: this exempts multi-round AI validation only — Rule 15 (validate against category Pricing Profile band) still applies to every new item regardless."

1068270 meets every dimensional and material parity criterion against 1082570. There is no new pricing question to validate, no new size class, no new material, no new process, and no new tier structure. Cloning the validated 1082570 tier table is the procedurally correct action.

The 1082570 tier table was itself the product of a full 4-round, 6-model AI validation (24 model runs) — that validation is inherited by every direct parity clone of 1082570 going forward, including 1068270.

---

## Step 5 — Order Context and Quantity Framing

No current PO. No quantity specified. Quote should reference the standard tier table directly; no current-order job-economics framing is required at this time (unlike 1082570, which has an open one-off qty 2 order at $42 flat tied to SO 20125600). If Sean later requests a one-off small-run order on 1068270, apply the same job-economics framework documented on 1082570 — but that is not in scope for this quote.

sections.notes:
> ## DIRECT PARITY CLONE OF 1082570
>
> P/N 1068270 is documented as a direct parity clone of P/N 1082570. All pricing, material cost, nesting math, production process, and tier structure are inherited unchanged. The two items differ only in part number, drawing content (G85 basket jib 500# load chart vs I70 EZR Mount 3.6K load chart), and the orientation of the dimensional pair (W/H swapped — sq ft is identical at 0.503).
>
> If any material cost, process, or band-anchor parameter changes on 1082570 in a future session, 1068270 should be updated in lockstep — the parity link is a permanent structural connection, not a one-time copy.

---

> ## DIMENSIONAL ORIENTATION NOTE
>
> 1082570 is documented as 10.00" W × 7.25" H. 1068270 is documented as 7.25" W × 10.00" H. The W/H pair is flipped, but the label area is identical at 0.503 sq ft. The flip has no impact on:
>
> - Material cost — vinyl, laminate, and ink are area-based
> - Lamination — both dimensions fit the 13.5" laminator either way (6.25" or 3.5" clearance)
> - Nesting — 28" Roland print bed accommodates both orientations (3-across at 7.25" or 2-across at 10.00")
> - Per-label production labor — single print, single lam pass, single cut run in either orientation
>
> The frontmatter `width_in: 7.25` / `height_in: 10.00` reflects the orientation as drawn on the 1068270 spec sheet. The validate.py math (7.25 × 10.00 / 144 = 0.5035 → 0.503) checks out cleanly.

---

> ## NO MULTI-ROUND AI VALIDATION
>
> Per PRICING_VALIDATION.md direct parity exemption. The 4-round, 6-model AI validation that established the 1082570 tier table (24 model runs) is inherited by this item.

---

> ## ARTWORK PREP — $0
>
> Elliott Equipment Company provides production-ready DWG files for every item on this account, without exception. File prep, artwork preparation, design time, and pre-press labor are zero-cost, zero-time inputs on every Elliott job. Step 1 (file import/setup) is a ~5-minute mechanical operation. This is a permanent account-level rule. See `PRICING_RULES.md` §22-24.

---

> ## FULL BLEED INK STANDARD
>
> Material cost includes full bleed ink coverage at $0.50/sq ft per the account-wide rule established 2026-06-01 (PRICING_RULES.md §25). No partial, medium, or low coverage assumptions are permitted on any Elliott printed/laminated item. The $1.00/label material cost reflects the buffered total under the canonical formula:
>
> `(Orajet 0.503 × $1.21) + (lam 0.503 × $0.2389) + (ink 0.503 × $0.50) + incidental buffer = $0.981 + $0.019 buffer = $1.00`
>
> Ink cost is locked at the §25 full bleed rate regardless of artwork color — no recosting is triggered by any color decision on this item or its benchmark (1082570).

---

> ## NO MOQ
>
> No minimum order quantity on this account, this item, or any future Elliott item. Permanent account-level rule.

---

sections.pricing_derivation:
> **4-ROUND AI VALIDATION: COMPLETE.** 6 models × 4 rounds = 24 total model runs. Validation modified the tier table and current-order price. See Step 4 below for full documentation.

---

## Step 1 — Spec Summary and Band Classification

- Dimensions: 10" × 7.25" = 0.503 sq ft. Single label. Orajet 3951 + 1-mil polyester lam. 1 production pass.
- At the low end of the singles band scope (~0.5–2.0 sq ft). Above the tiny-label threshold (<0.1 sq ft). The singles per-sq-ft band applies.
- Material cost: $1.00/label (§25 canonical full-bleed formula — color-independent; see color conflict below for the production-setup question).

---

## Step 2 — Singles Band Rate

The singles Pricing Profile has 1 confirmed data point:

| Item | Sq Ft | Price (qty 20) | Rate |
|------|-------|----------------|------|
| 1230820 (Load Chart, D105) | 1.296 | $20.00 | $15.43/sq ft |

**Band width:** Wide (1 data point). Future items in the 0.5–2.0 sq ft range should land within ±15% of $15.43/sq ft ($13.12–$17.74).

Scaling 1082570 at 0.503 sq ft:

`0.503 sq ft × $15.43/sq ft = $7.76` → rounded to **$8.00 at qty 20**

Per-sq-ft check: $8.00 / 0.503 = **$15.91/sq ft** — within ±15% of band center ✓

---

## Step 3 — Full Tier Table Construction

Tier structure proportioned from P/N 1230820's accepted tier ratios relative to the qty 20 anchor:

| Tier | 1230820 Ratio | 1082570 Raw | Quoted |
|------|--------------|-------------|--------|
| 1-9 | $30/$20 = 1.50× | $8 × 1.50 = $12.00 | **$12** |
| 10-19 | $24/$20 = 1.20× | $8 × 1.20 = $9.60 | **$10** |
| 20-49 | 1.00× (anchor) | $8.00 | **$8** |
| 50-99 | $17/$20 = 0.85× | $8 × 0.85 = $6.80 | **$7** |
| 100-199 | $14/$20 = 0.70× | $8 × 0.70 = $5.60 | **$6** |
| 200+ | $11/$20 = 0.55× | $8 × 0.55 = $4.40 | **$5** (rounded up — see note) |

**200+ tier note (pre-validation):** $4.40 proportionally rounds to $4. However, at material cost $1.70, a $4 tier yields ~58% margin — below the category minimum-worthwhile floor (~64%). Preliminary: rounded up to $5. See Step 4 — AI validation replaced this tier table with final validated figures.

**This is the pre-validation preliminary tier table.** AI validation (Step 4) modified all tiers — particularly 1-9 (raised from $12 to $16.50), 50-99 (reduced from $7 to $6.25), 100-199 (reduced from $6 to $5.25), and 200+ (reduced from $5 to $4.25 as volume reward for strategic account). The qty 20-49 anchor ($8.00) was confirmed unchanged.

---

## Step 4 — Current Order Pricing: Option A vs Option B + AI Validation (4 Rounds, 6 Models)

### The Core Question

At qty 2, what price does this order receive?

**Option A:** Apply the $55 account minimum-worthwhile-charge floor ($55.00 total, $27.50/label implied).

**Option B:** Price on job economics with setup recovery — a flat rate anchored to what the job warrants at this label size and order quantity.

**Option A argument:** Consistent with prior one-off small-run orders on this account (P/N 1277970-1278000 program, P/N 3017583, P/N 3017584).

**Option B argument:** Those precedents are tiny-label one-offs (0.008–0.026 sq ft) where the per-sq-ft band is inapplicable. At 0.503 sq ft, 1082570 is a full singles-band label — Sean can make a direct D105 comparison. A $55 total ($27.50/label implied) = $54.67/sq ft, vs D105 at $15.43/sq ft. That is 3.5× the benchmark rate and is indefensible if Sean runs the math.

---

### Round 1 — Build (6 Models)

- Qty 2 consensus: **$38–$45 cluster**
- Qty 20 consensus: **$8.50–$11.00 cluster**
- **All 6 rejected the $55 floor** for a 0.503 sq ft label vs D105 comparison
- All 6 agreed setup-recovery logic applies to the current order, not sq ft band alone
- All 6 confirmed the 20-49 anchor at approximately $8.00–$9.00/label

---

### Round 2 — Destruction (6 Models)

- **All 6 found the $55 floor indefensible** at this label size — D105 comparison available and damaging
- All 6 identified the 20-49 tier as most competitively exposed vs D105
- **All 6 recommended Option B** (job economics) over Option A ($55 floor)
- Consensus qty 2 total: **$40–$42**
- Consensus qty 20 tier: **$7.25–$8.50/label**
- Consensus 1-9 tier: **$14–$18/label**
  - One model specifically recommended $16.50 to widen the gap between the flat current-order price and the low-end catalog tier — noted for Round 4

---

### Round 3 — Buyer Simulation (6 Models)

- **5 of 6 models approved $42 for qty 2 without question** — instant approval below $50–$100 threshold
- D105 comparison at qty 20: $15.91/sq ft vs $15.43/sq ft = **3% delta** — every model called it a non-issue
- Job economics explanation (setup recovery on a 2-label run) was the **single most cited approval driver** across all 6 models
- Mental model Sean builds: ~$15.50/sq ft at qty 20, $40–$55 short-run floor — healthy framework for future quotes
- 1 outlier recommended $24–$26 total — **rejected**; ignores setup recovery and undercuts the account's own value structure

---

### Round 4 — Final Synthesis (6 Models)

- **5 of 6 unanimous: send as shown, no changes**
- 1 model recommended bumping the 1-9 tier from $16.00 to **$16.50** to widen the gap at low quantities — **adopted**
- No tier flagged as wrong by majority
- Long-term precedent: holds cleanly for 20+ items on this account without creating pricing friction
- No discomfort flags on any tier or on the $42 flat current-order price
- **No MOQ.** One model raised the possibility of a minimum order quantity. Nick's decision: **no MOQ on this account**. Elliott is a strategic account with major growth potential — access at any quantity is intentional and permanent.

---

### Final Validated Tier Table

| Tier | Price/Label | Per Sq Ft | Margin | Notes |
|------|------------|-----------|--------|-------|
| 1-9 | $16.50 | $32.80 | ~94% | Widened from preliminary $12 |
| 10-19 | $10.50 | $20.88 | ~90% | |
| 20-49 | $8.00 | $15.91 | ~88% | Band anchor confirmed — D105 delta +3%, non-issue |
| 50-99 | $6.25 | $12.43 | ~84% | |
| 100-199 | $5.25 | $10.44 | ~81% | |
| 200+ | $4.25 | $8.45 | ~76% | Intentionally steepest volume discount — reward for strategic account |

**200+ tier:** $8.45/sq ft is intentionally below the D105 anchor ($15.43/sq ft). Volume reward for a strategic account — Nick's decision. The margin floor reflects the steepest tier discount by design. Not a margin error; documented as a strategic override. (Margins updated 2026-06-09 — material cost normalized to the §25 canonical formula, $1.00 vs the prior $1.33 Safety Yellow flood-coat assumption; tier prices unchanged.)

**No MOQ:** No minimum order quantity has been set, and none should ever be set on this account, this item, or any future Elliott item. This is a permanent account-level rule — do not add, suggest, or imply MOQ language anywhere.

**The $42.00 current-order price does NOT enter the singles Pricing Profile band.** The band tracks production-volume acceptance. One-off tied-ship orders are excluded from band calculations.

---

## Step 5 — Color Version and the §25 Ink Standard

The open color conflict (Safety Yellow flood coat per drawing note 6 vs black-on-white per artwork) is a **production-setup question only**. Under the account-wide §25 full-bleed standard (established 2026-06-01, applied to this item 2026-06-09), ink is priced at $0.50/sq ft × full label sq ft regardless of color. Material cost ($1.00/label) and every tier price are identical under either version. No recosting or reprice is triggered by Sean's selection.

Details in Nesting and Material Cost section.

---

## Step 6 — Job Economics for Current Order ($42.00 Flat)

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Quantity | 2 labels |
| Material cost | 2 × $1.00 = $2.00 |
| File prep / artwork prep / design | $0 — Elliott provides production-ready DWG (account rule, `PRICING_RULES.md` §22) |
| Estimated production time | ~20-25 min |
| Quote price (job economics / setup recovery) | $42.00 |
| Revenue per production minute | ~$1.68–$2.10/min (~$101–$126/hr) |
| Contribution margin on this order | ($42 − $2.00) / $42 = **~95%** |

The ~95% contribution margin is structural to job-economics pricing on a small-material-cost 2-label run. It reflects contribution to setup recovery and fixed overhead, not excess profit. Effective job-level margin is lower when all fixed costs are allocated. The $42.00 is intentionally above the 1-9 tier math ($16.50 × 2 = $33.00) to reflect the setup-recovery component on this specific tied ship order.

>

>
> **Artwork as provided:** Black linework and text on a white background — NO Safety Yellow flood coat present in the artwork.
>

>
> **Current status — samples delivered:**
> - Nick sent Sean physical samples of both versions (black on white per artwork; Safety Yellow per drawing note 6).
> - Samples were delivered within **~26 hours** of the request.

> - Production is **blocked** pending Sean's color selection via PO.
>

> - Material cost: $1.00/label (§25 canonical formula) — identical under either color version.
> - Whichever version Sean selects: pricing holds exactly as quoted. No reprice required.
> - **Under NO circumstances produce this label without Sean's explicit color version selection.**
>
> **Pricing is color-independent per §25.** The full-bleed formula covers cost under either outcome.

---

> ## ⚠ CURRENT ORDER — $42.00 FLAT — JOB ECONOMICS FRAMING
>
> Sean Finn ordered qty 2 for SO 20125600 — a specific I70 unit shipping. PO not yet received (internal reference only).
>
> **This order is quoted at $42.00 flat.** Framing: job economics — setup recovery on a 2-label run. NOT a per-label rate. NOT a floor-anchored price. NOT a band data point. NOT a benchmark for future orders.
>

>
> **No minimum order quantity** on this account. Do not add, suggest, or imply MOQ language.
>
> The $42.00 price does NOT enter the singles Pricing Profile band. At reorder (any quantity): apply the tier table directly.

>
> No dimensions appear on the drawing (dated 08-25-04). Width 10" and Height 7.25" were confirmed by Derrik Walton, P.E., Engineering Manager at Elliott Equipment Company, via email on 2026-05-28. Walton referenced the I50 chart as the size standard for the I70 model.
>
> These dimensions are engineering-manager confirmed, not estimated. If a future drawing revision surfaces different dimensions, contact Nick before any reprice or reorder.

---

> ## MYLAR TERMINOLOGY
>
> Drawing calls out "1 mil clear Mylar overlay." Mylar is a DuPont-originated trade name for biaxially oriented polyester (BoPET) film. It maps directly to the standard 1-mil clear polyester overlaminate used across the entire Orajet 3951 material family on this account. No material substitution. No ambiguity.
>
> Consistent with the same terminology note documented on P/N 3017584 (same "Mylar overlay" wording). Nick is actively working with Elliott to standardize spec-sheet verbiage toward "1-mil polyester overlaminate."

---

> ## ARTWORK PREP — $0
>
> Elliott Equipment Company provides production-ready DWG files for every item on this account, without exception. File prep, artwork preparation, design time, and pre-press labor are zero-cost, zero-time inputs on every Elliott job. Step 1 (file import/setup) is a ~5-minute mechanical operation. This is a permanent account-level rule. See `PRICING_RULES.md` §22-24.

---

> ## AI VALIDATION — COMPLETE
>
> 4-round, 6-model AI validation (24 total model runs per `governance/PRICING_VALIDATION.md`) was completed following the initial quote session. Full documentation in Pricing Derivation, Step 4.
>
> **Key outcomes:** Per-label catalog rate at qty 2 rejected by all 6 models — $42 flat adopted for job economics / setup recovery. Tier table modified from preliminary: $16.50 / $10.50 / $8.00 / $6.25 / $5.25 / $4.25. No MOQ — Nick's decision, permanent account rule.

---

> ## STRATEGIC ACCOUNT DEVELOPMENT — ENGINEERING/PROCUREMENT STANDARD PROJECT
>
> Sean's response to the physical sample delivery went beyond color selection. He asked Nick to suggest what specs to call out on a drawing for label/decal procurement.
>
> More significantly: **Sean and his manager are initiating a project to develop an engineering and procurement standard for labels and decals at Elliott Equipment Company.** They will be reaching out to Nick for input on the standard.
>
> **This is not a pricing input.** It is documented here as strategic account context for anyone working this account in the future:

> - Nick's involvement at the engineering/specification level positions Pro Label as the expert vendor of record, not a commodity supplier.
> - This initiative is separate from the current color conflict resolution. It is a long-term relationship signal.
> - Any future Claude Code session working this account should be aware of this when framing quotes, communications, or strategic recommendations.

> **No action required on pricing or production.** Update this note when Sean's outreach occurs and the scope of the standard project becomes clearer.

sections.production:
## Process Steps

1. **File import/setup** — open the Elliott-supplied production-ready DWG, send to cutter. ~5 min mechanical. NOT billable labor (see `PRICING_RULES.md` §22).
2. **Cut** — Roland SG3-300 on 24" Cardinal Red stock. 2 labels across, cut in rows.
3. **Weed** — remove excess vinyl around "E160" block letters. 4 large characters, no fine interior detail, open geometry.
4. **Mask** — apply TransferRite 582U transfer tape over weeded characters.
5. **Inspect** — verify cut accuracy, no lifted edges, character legibility.
6. **Package** — flat, masked with transfer tape carrier, ready for hand-application.

## Production Notes

- **Lamination passes:** 0 (cut vinyl, no print)
- **Cut runs:** 1
- **Equipment:** Roland SG3-300 (24" Cardinal Red stock)
- **Weeding complexity:** Low. "E160" is 4 large block characters at generous scale. Direct analog to P/N 1205720 ("E190") — same dimensions, same color, same character count, same letter forms (E, digit, digit, digit).

---
sections.pricing_derivation:
**Methodology:** Direct sq ft parity with P/N 1205720 (cut vinyl baseline, FA Accepted).

**Sq ft comparison:**

| Item | Dimensions | Sq Ft | Price (qty 20) | $/sq ft |
|------|-----------|-------|----------------|---------|
| P/N 1205720 (benchmark) | 33-9/16" × 11" | 2.56 | $35 | $13.67 |
| P/N 3017435 (parity) | 43.91" × 8.38" | 2.56 | $35 | $13.67 |
| P/N 3018378 (parity) | 32.88" × 11.00" | 2.512 | $35 | $13.94 |
| **P/N 1186310 (this item)** | **33-9/16" × 11"** | **2.564** | **$35** | **$13.65** |

Dimensions are identical to P/N 1205720 to the 1/16". Material (3M 180mC-53 Cardinal Red), process (Cut/Weed/Mask), and weeding profile are also identical — only the model number content differs ("E160" vs "E190"). Same tier structure applied.

**Per sq ft at qty 20:** $35 ÷ 2.564 = **$13.65/sq ft**

**Profile Validation:** Within the Cut Vinyl Pricing Profile band ($13.67–$13.93/sq ft at qty 20). The $0.02/sq ft variance from the band's lower bound is a rounding artifact: the band's $13.67/sq ft lower bound was computed from P/N 1205720's 2-decimal sq ft figure (2.56); at 3-decimal precision (2.564, same dimensions), the same $35 price yields $13.65/sq ft. Functionally identical to the cut vinyl baseline. No band adjustment warranted.

**AI Validation:** Not separately run. Per PRICING_VALIDATION.md, multi-round validation is exempt for items dimensionally and materially identical to an already-quoted item — this item is a direct dimensional and material clone of an FA-accepted item. Profile band validation still applies and was performed.

**Override:** None. Engine output.

**Rule 14 deviation acknowledged:** P/N 1205720 is a Relationship Concession. This item prices within the concession-phase band by deliberate strategic choice, not oversight. If/when relationship pricing normalizes, recalibrate against the AI consensus range ($14.84–$16.41/sq ft).

sections.nesting:
### Laminator Pass Count

Both the 10.5" width and 4" height dimensions are well within the 13.5" polyester laminator. Either orientation yields a single lamination pass. No laminator complications.

- 4" across laminator: 9.5" clearance — single pass ✓
- 10.5" across laminator: 3.0" clearance — single pass ✓

### Print Layout and Nesting

Label: 10.5" × 4". Roland SG3-300 max print width: 28".

**Orientation A** (10.5" across the 28" bed, 4" in feed direction):
- Labels per row: 2 (2 × 10.5" = 21" ≤ 28")
- At qty 10: 5 rows × 2 labels = 10 labels in one print run ✓

**Orientation B** (4" across the 28" bed, 10.5" in feed direction):
- Labels per row: 7 (7 × 4" = 28" exactly)
- At qty 10: 2 rows (7 + 3) in one print run ✓
- At qty 30/month: 5 rows × 7 = 35 → fits ≥30 in one run ✓

Nesting is efficient at recurring volume. Multiple labels per row, single print run at any expected order quantity.

### Material Cost Breakdown per Label

> Material cost version date: 2026-06-01. Unit costs from `governance/PRODUCTION.md` (Orajet 3951 verified 2026-04-22; 1-mil polyester lam verified 2026-05-28; full bleed ink standard verified 2026-06-01).
>
> **Account-wide full bleed ink rule (PRICING_RULES.md §25, established 2026-06-01):** All printed/laminated items on this account are priced assuming full bleed / full coverage ink at all times. Rate: $0.50/sq ft × full label sq ft. The $0.25 placeholder previously used on this item is superseded by this rule.

| Component | Calculation | Per-Label Cost |
|-----------|-------------|---------------|
| Orajet 3951 cast vinyl | 0.292 sq ft × $1.21/sq ft | $0.353 |
| 1-mil polyester overlaminate | 0.292 sq ft × $0.2389/sq ft | $0.070 |
| Eco-solvent ink (full bleed standard) | 0.292 sq ft × $0.50/sq ft | $0.146 |
| **Calculated total** | | **$0.569** |
| Incidental buffer (conservative round-up) ¹ | judgment-applied | + $0.031 |
| **Canonical `material_cost_per_unit`** | | **$0.60** |

¹ **Incidental buffer convention (account-wide rule per PRICING_RULES.md §25):** The calculated material total is rounded conservatively upward to account for setup scrap, registration pulls, and minor production waste on every Elliott printed/laminated item. The buffer is judgment-applied, not a fixed number. For this item the calculated $0.569 rounds to $0.60 in the frontmatter `material_cost_per_unit` field. Same convention applies to every other Elliott printed/laminated item going forward.

sections.production:
1. File import/setup — open the Elliott-supplied production-ready DWG (registration marks already defined), send to RIP. ~5 min mechanical. NOT billable labor (see `PRICING_RULES.md` §22).
2. Print — Roland SG3-300, full-color eco-solvent on Orajet 3951. Safety Red flood coat header + white DANGER text + white icon + white field panels + black schematics/body text/border. High ink coverage — ANSI Safety Red must meet Z535.1 color specification.
3. Laminate — 1-mil clear polyester. ONE pass (both 4" and 10.5" dimensions fit the 13.5" laminator with substantial clearance).
4. Contour cut — Roland, square corners per account default.
5. Weed — remove excess material.
6. Inspect — print quality, laminate adhesion, cut accuracy, Safety Red color fidelity (ANSI Z535.1 compliance), white panel coverage uniformity, black element sharpness.
7. Package — flat, individually cut, stacked.

- **Lamination Passes:** 1
- **Cut Runs:** 1
- **Estimated Production Time (qty 10, initial order):** ~30-40 min total (5 min file import + 25-35 min print/lam/cut/weed/inspect/package at qty 10)
- **Constraints:** None. All dimensions well within equipment limits on all passes.

---
sections.pricing_derivation:
> **REVALIDATED 2026-06-01 — 4-WAVE ATOMIC AI VALIDATION COMPLETE.** 6 models × 4 waves = 24 total independent responses. Each model session was atomic — models received one self-contained prompt and had no visibility into other waves or responses. Claude Chat synthesized each wave before generating the next prompt.
>
> All prior pricing derivation content for this item is superseded by the record below. Final pricing locked above benchmark $/sq ft at every tier; material cost corrected to $0.60 under the account-wide full bleed ink rule established simultaneously in this session.

---

## Step 1 — Process Summary

**Validation method:** 4-wave atomic AI validation per `governance/VALIDATION_PROMPTS.md`. 6 top-tier models per wave, 24 total independent responses. Atomic session model: each prompt is self-contained; no model has visibility into other waves or other models' responses. Claude Chat synthesizes after each wave and generates the next prompt. Nick is sole decision-maker; the 24 responses are evidence, not votes.

**Item:** 1210810 LBL - DANGER FALLING JIB. 10.5" × 4" = 0.292 sq ft. Single label. Orajet 3951 + 1-mil polyester lam. Safety Red flood coat ANSI Z535.1/Z535.4 DANGER label. 1 production pass.

**Dimensional scope:** 0.292 sq ft is below the singles band scope floor (~0.5 sq ft). Excluded from singles band DATA POINTS on dimensional scope, NOT on pricing grounds. The validated $/sq ft is INTENTIONALLY ABOVE the singles band — the structurally required small-format premium per Wave 4 verdict.

---

## Step 2 — Wave 1: Build Round

**6 models, atomic prompts. 1 model disqualified for calculation error → 5 valid responses.**

- 4 of 5 valid responses landed **$4.51–$4.64 at qty 20**, clustering in the singles band ($15.43–$15.91/sq ft on a per-sq-ft basis).
- Central tendency: **~$4.58 at qty 20**.
- All models respected the band floor — none priced below $15.43/sq ft.
- 1 outlier at **$6.00 at qty 20**, arguing small-format premium ABOVE band on first-principles grounds.

**Wave 1 finding:** Independent build anchors clustered at band floor. The outlier argued the small-format premium explicitly. Claude Chat carried both signals into Wave 2.

---

## Step 3 — Wave 2: Destruction Round

**All 6 responses returned.**

| Attack Vector | All 6 Models |
|---------------|--------------|
| Buyer attack severity | **H** (6/6) |
| Competitor attack severity | **H** (6/6) |
| Strategic attack severity | **H** (6/6) |

**Primary finding (universal):** The 10-19 tier (as configured in the proposed table going into Wave 2) was priced BELOW benchmark $/sq ft at the same tier. A smaller label carrying lower $/sq ft than a larger label inverts the cost logic and is detectable by a data-literate buyer normalizing spend across the account. Structural inversion.

**Secondary finding (universal):** $0.55 material cost (as it stood going into Wave 2) understated the true cost for a Safety Red flood coat ANSI label. Ink at the high-coverage rate needs to be in the material build, not a placeholder.

**Verdict:** 5 of 6 — Yes with modifications. 1 of 6 — No.

**Wave 2 carry-forward:** The 10-19 inversion is non-negotiable — must be corrected before quote. Material cost must be revised to include realistic high-coverage ink.

---

## Step 4 — Wave 3: Buyer Simulation

**All 6 responses returned. All 6 models simulated Sean Finn (Elliott Equipment Employee-Owner, ~$140K/year program, $/sq ft mental model, deepening partnership phase).**

- **All 6 calculated $15.41/sq ft at qty 20** independently and recognized benchmark consistency vs the singles band.
- **$4.50 at qty 20-49 cleared without a fight in all 6 simulations.** No model triggered pushback at this rate.
- **PO Decision:**
  - 4 of 6: PO as-is.
  - 2 of 6: One low-friction structural question before PO (clarification on 10-19 vs 20-49 boundary).
- **Pushback threshold (20-49 tier):** Median ~**$4.95**. Range across models: $4.75–$5.25.
- **Instant approval (20-49 tier):** **$4.25–$4.50**.
- **Anchor line (unanimous):** "You will never be invoiced more for a smaller quantity" was the unanimous quote-language anchor — all 6 models flagged this as the friction-eliminator.
- **All 6 confirmed:** They are logging $15.41/sq ft as a **permanent account data point** for benchmarking every future Pro Label quote against.

**Wave 3 carry-forward:** $4.50 clears Sean's approval. The Wave 2 structural inversion (10-19 below benchmark $/sq ft) still has not been addressed in the proposed tier table. Wave 4 must do the binary check.

---

## Step 5 — Wave 4: Final Synthesis

**All 6 responses returned. Verdict: NO on the Wave 3 table — UNANIMOUS.**

**Primary finding (universal):** The Wave 3 table had **10-19 at $4.95 ($16.95/sq ft)**, which is **8.5% BELOW** the benchmark's same-tier $/sq ft ($18.52/sq ft for 1230820's 10-19 tier at $24.00/1.296 sq ft).

The rule: **a smaller label must carry equal or higher $/sq ft than a larger label at the same tier.** The Wave 3 table inverted this at 10-19. Structurally backwards and detectable by a data-literate buyer normalizing spend across the account.

**Fix range across the 6 models:** **$5.25–$5.95**. Center: **$5.40**.

**Wave 4 verdict — unanimous NO.** The 10-19 inversion must be corrected. Every model insisted.

---

## Step 6 — Nick's Final Decision

Nick reviewed the Wave 4 unanimous verdict and the 24-response evidence base. His decision:

**Raise the full table above benchmark $/sq ft at every tier** to ensure the small-format premium is correct and unambiguous. This is stronger than the Wave 4 minimum fix — it is the structurally clean answer that propagates the same principle to every tier, not just 10-19.

- **10-19:** Set to **$5.75** ($19.69/sq ft, +6.3% above benchmark $18.52). Inside the Wave 4 fix range ($5.25–$5.95) and above the Wave 4 center ($5.40).
- **20-49:** Moved from $4.50 to **$4.75** to maintain curve integrity with the raised 10-19 tier. $/sq ft: $16.27 (+5.4% above benchmark $15.43). Wave 3 had cleared $4.50, but maintaining the curve required a corresponding lift.
- **50-99:** Set to **$4.00** ($13.70/sq ft, +4.4% above benchmark $13.12).
- **100-199:** Set to **$3.50** ($11.99/sq ft, +11.0% above benchmark $10.80).
- **200+:** Set to **$2.75** ($9.42/sq ft, +11.0% above benchmark $8.49).
- **1-9:** Initially set to $55 flat under the (then-extant) MOQ 10 rule. **Superseded 2026-06-05 in the MOQ purge** — see Step 7 for the new 1-9 tier at $7.25.

**Material cost simultaneously corrected** to $0.60 under the account-wide full bleed ink rule established in this same session: 0.292 × $0.50 = $0.146 ink (full bleed standard), plus Orajet ($0.353) + lam ($0.070) = calculated total $0.569 → buffered to $0.60 (conservative round-up per PRICING_RULES.md §25).

---

## Step 7 — 1-9 Tier Addition (2026-06-05, MOQ Purge)

The 1-9 tier was originally set to $55 flat under the printed/laminated MOQ 10 rule established 2026-06-01. On 2026-06-05 Nick removed all MOQ / $55 minimum order charge rules from the account (audit + decision: never communicated to Sean, never will be; sub-10 orders treated as loss-leader cost-of-doing-business on a $140K account).

This left 1210810 as the only catalog item without a real per-unit 1-9 tier. Nick set the new 1-9 tier at **$7.25**:

- **Derivation:** 1.5× the $4.75 anchor (20-49 tier) = $7.125 → snapped up to $7.25.
- **Pattern consistency:** 1.5× matches the singles-band 1-9/20-49 ratio used by 1230820 ($30/$20 = 1.50×) and the 5-label kit 1245130 ($75/$50 = 1.50×).
- **Premium consistency:** $/sq ft = $7.25 / 0.292 = $24.83 — +7.3% above the benchmark 1-9 $/sq ft ($23.15). Preserves the Wave 4 small-format premium principle (smaller labels carry equal or higher $/sq ft than larger labels at the same tier) at the new tier.
- **9/10 cliff resolved by §26 invoice protection.** 9 × $7.25 = $65.25 vs 10 × $5.75 = $57.50 — buyer is charged the lower of the two ($57.50). Same pattern that exists on every other multi-tier item on this account: 1205720 (9 × $45 = $405 vs 10 × $40 = $400 → buyer charged $400); 3010707 (9 × $28 = $252 vs 10 × $24 = $240 → buyer charged $240).

### Final Tier Table and $/Sq Ft vs Benchmark

| Tier | Final Price | Final $/Sq Ft | Benchmark $/Sq Ft (1230820) | Delta |
|------|------------|---------------|------------------------------|-------|
| 1-9 | $7.25 | $24.83 | $23.15 | **+7.3%** |
| 10-19 | $5.75 | $19.69 | $18.52 | **+6.3%** |
| 20-49 | $4.75 | $16.27 | $15.43 | **+5.4%** |
| 50-99 | $4.00 | $13.70 | $13.12 | **+4.4%** |
| 100-199 | $3.50 | $11.99 | $10.80 | **+11.0%** |
| 200+ | $2.75 | $9.42 | $8.49 | **+11.0%** |

**Small-format premium is consistent and defensible at every tier. No inversions.**

Benchmark $/sq ft derived from 1230820 (1.296 sq ft, root benchmark) tier table: $30/$24/$20/$17/$14/$11 = $23.15/$18.52/$15.43/$13.12/$10.80/$8.49 per sq ft at 1.296 sq ft.

---

## Step 8 — Invoice Protection at Tier Boundaries

Two adjacent-tier cliffs exist on this item; both are resolved automatically by §26 invoice protection (buyer charged whichever total is lower).

**9/10 boundary (1-9 → 10-19):**
- 9 × $7.25 = **$65.25** (maximum total at the top of the 1-9 tier)
- 10 × $5.75 = **$57.50** (minimum total at the bottom of the 10-19 tier)
- Cliff: **$7.75**. Buyer ordering 9 units is invoiced at $57.50, not $65.25.

**19/20 boundary (10-19 → 20-49):**
- 19 × $5.75 = **$109.25** (maximum total at the top of the 10-19 tier)
- 20 × $4.75 = **$95.00** (minimum total at the bottom of the 20-49 tier)
- Cliff: **$14.25**. Buyer ordering 19 units is invoiced at $95.00, not $109.25.

Per `PRICING_RULES.md` §26 — the buyer will never be invoiced more for a smaller quantity than they would pay for a larger quantity at the next tier. Where math creates a cliff, the buyer is charged whichever total is lower. This is the same pattern used on every other multi-tier item on this account.

---

## Step 9 — Band Positioning Summary

| Item | Sq Ft | Price (qty 20) | $/Sq Ft | Position |
|------|-------|----------------|---------|----------|
| 1230820 (Load Chart, D105) | 1.296 | $20.00 | $15.43 | Band root |
| 1082570 (Load Chart, I70) | 0.503 | $8.00 | $15.91 | Band ceiling |
| **1210810 (DANGER Falling Jib)** | **0.292** | **$4.75** | **$16.27** | **+5.4% above ceiling — small-format premium (intentional)** |

**Dimensional scope note (unchanged):** 1210810 at 0.292 sq ft remains below the ~0.5 sq ft singles band scope floor. It continues to be **excluded from band DATA POINTS** until production-volume acceptance is confirmed by Nick. The exclusion is dimensional, not pricing.

**Pricing note (changed under this revalidation):** The validated $/sq ft is now INTENTIONALLY ABOVE the singles band, reflecting the structurally required small-format premium per Wave 4 unanimous verdict. The prior derivation framed 1210810 as "essentially at the band floor" ($15.41/sq ft) — that framing is superseded. Smaller labels carry HIGHER fixed cost per sq ft (setup, registration, inspection); the 4-wave atomic validation enforced this with binary clarity.

**Do NOT use 1210810 as a benchmark** for any other item until production-volume acceptance is confirmed.

---

## Step 10 — Material Cost Under the Account-Wide Full Bleed Rule

The account-wide full bleed ink rule was established in this same session (2026-06-01) and codified as PRICING_RULES.md §25, MASTER_CONTEXT.md Core Rule 9, and PRODUCTION.md Account-Wide Ink Coverage Standard.

For 1210810, applying the rule:

```
Orajet 3951:           0.292 × $1.21    = $0.353
1-mil poly laminate:   0.292 × $0.2389  = $0.070
Ink (full bleed):      0.292 × $0.50    = $0.146
Calculated total:                          $0.569
Incidental buffer (conservative round-up): + $0.031
material_cost_per_unit (canonical):        $0.60
```

The prior $0.25 placeholder is fully superseded. There is no "unverified ink" condition on this item anymore — the account-wide rule sets a fixed coverage assumption ($0.50/sq ft full bleed) that applies regardless of actual flood-coat color or coverage measurement. The incidental buffer (judgment-applied conservative round-up) accounts for setup scrap, registration pulls, and minor production waste. The frontmatter `material_cost_per_unit: 0.60` reflects the buffered total.

This same convention applies to every Elliott printed/laminated item going forward — see `governance/PRODUCTION.md` Account-Wide Ink Coverage Standard for the canonical formula and `governance/PRICING_RULES.md` §25 for the rule itself.

---

## Step 11 — Account-Level Rule Codification (Cross-Reference)

The following account-level rules apply to this item:

- **PRICING_RULES.md §22-24** — File prep / artwork prep / pre-press labor are $0 on this account. Elliott supplies production-ready DWG.
- **PRICING_RULES.md §25** — Account-wide full bleed ink rule ($0.50/sq ft × full label sq ft + incidental buffer). Established 2026-06-01.
- **PRICING_RULES.md §26** — Invoice protection: buyer never invoiced more for a smaller quantity than a larger quantity at the next tier. Rewritten 2026-06-05 to be MOQ-independent. Resolves the 9/10 and 19/20 boundary cliffs on this item.
- **MASTER_CONTEXT.md Core Rule 8** — Full bleed ink ($0.50/sq ft) account-wide. Established 2026-06-01.
- **PRODUCTION.md Account-Wide Ink Coverage Standard** — Canonical material cost formula. Established 2026-06-01.

---
sections.margin_analysis:
> **Material cost: $0.60/label.** Locked under the account-wide full bleed ink rule established 2026-06-01 (PRICING_RULES.md §25). No "unverified ink" condition applies — the full bleed standard is a fixed coverage assumption that supersedes the prior $0.25 placeholder.

### Full Margin Table — All 6 Tiers at $0.60 Material

| Tier | Price | Material | $ Profit | Margin |
|------|-------|----------|----------|--------|
| 1-9 | $7.25 | $0.60 | $6.65 | **91.7%** |
| 10-19 | $5.75 | $0.60 | $5.15 | **89.6%** |
| 20-49 | $4.75 | $0.60 | $4.15 | **87.4%** |
| 50-99 | $4.00 | $0.60 | $3.40 | **85.0%** |
| 100-199 | $3.50 | $0.60 | $2.90 | **82.9%** |
| 200+ | $2.75 | $0.60 | $2.15 | **78.2%** |

All tiers comfortably above the internal P&L benchmark (~79.7%). The 200+ tier at $2.75/$0.60 = 78.2% sits ~1.5 pts below benchmark — acceptable for a structural-completeness tier that 30/month recurring volume never organically reaches.

### Internal P&L Benchmark: ~79.7%

| Tier | Margin | Vs. Benchmark |
|------|--------|---------------|
| 1-9 | 91.7% | **+12.0 pts above** |
| 10-19 | 89.6% | **+9.9 pts above** |
| **20-49 (recurring volume)** | **87.4%** | **+7.7 pts above** |
| 50-99 | 85.0% | +5.3 pts above |
| 100-199 | 82.9% | +3.2 pts above |
| 200+ | 78.2% | -1.5 pts (structural tier — acceptable) |

**The recurring 30/month volume tier (20-49) is 7.7 points above the internal P&L benchmark at the corrected $0.60 material cost.** Margin is durable.

### Per-Sq-Ft Margin Comparison (Internal Reference)

Per-sq-ft margin (price ÷ sq ft − material ÷ sq ft) at recurring tier:
- 20-49: $16.27/sq ft − $2.05/sq ft material = **$14.22/sq ft contribution**

Material cost per sq ft = $0.60 / 0.292 = $2.05/sq ft. Higher than the 1230820 reference (~$2.49/sq ft including ink at canonical full bleed math) at first glance — but this reflects the buffer convention applied to a small sq ft footprint where the fixed buffer is a larger fraction of total cost. This is structurally consistent with the small-format premium principle that drove the Wave 4 verdict.

> ## REVALIDATED 2026-06-01 — 4-WAVE ATOMIC AI VALIDATION COMPLETE
>
> This item was **revalidated** on 2026-06-01 under the new 4-wave atomic AI validation process (per `governance/VALIDATION_PROMPTS.md`). 6 models × 4 waves = **24 total independent responses**. Each model session was **atomic** — models received one self-contained prompt and had no visibility into other waves or other models' responses. Claude Chat synthesized each wave before generating the next prompt. Nick is sole decision-maker; the 24 responses are evidence, not votes.
>
> **All prior pricing structure for this item is superseded** by the validated final table locked in this session. References to the prior $4.50 / $4.75 tier structure with $0.67 material cost and the $0.25 placeholder ink scenario are obsolete.
>
> **Wave 4 unanimous verdict (the binding constraint):** A smaller label must carry **equal or higher** $/sq ft than a larger label at the same tier. The Wave 3 table inverted this at 10-19 (8.5% below benchmark $/sq ft at that tier). Structurally backwards and detectable by a data-literate buyer. Fixed by Nick by raising the full table above benchmark $/sq ft at every tier.
>
> **Nick's final decision:** Raise the full table above benchmark $/sq ft at every tier to ensure the small-format premium is correct and unambiguous. 20-49 moved from $4.50 to $4.75 to maintain curve integrity with the raised 10-19 tier. Small-format premium consistent and defensible at every tier. No inversions.

---

> ## ACCOUNT-WIDE FULL BLEED INK RULE (ESTABLISHED 2026-06-01)
>
> The account-wide full bleed / full coverage ink rule was established in this same session and codified at the governance layer:
> - **`governance/PRICING_RULES.md` §25** — the rule itself
> - **`.claude/MASTER_CONTEXT.md` Core Rule 9** — Core Rule entry
> - **`governance/PRODUCTION.md` Account-Wide Ink Coverage Standard** — material cost formula
>
> **Rule:** All printed/laminated items on this account are priced assuming **full bleed / full coverage ink at all times**. Rate: **$0.50/sq ft × full label sq ft**. No medium, low, or partial coverage assumptions are permitted on any Elliott printed/laminated item — past, present, or future.
>
> **Incidental buffer convention:** The calculated material total is rounded **conservatively upward** to account for setup scrap, registration pulls, and minor production waste. The buffer is judgment-applied, not a fixed number.
>
> **For this item (1210810 at 0.292 sq ft):**
> ```
> Orajet 3951:          0.292 × $1.21    = $0.353
> 1-mil poly laminate:  0.292 × $0.2389  = $0.070
> Ink (full bleed):     0.292 × $0.50    = $0.146
> Calculated total:                         $0.569
> Incidental buffer (conservative round-up): +$0.031
> material_cost_per_unit (canonical):       $0.60
> ```
>
> The prior $0.25 placeholder and the "ink unverified" condition on this item are **fully superseded**. There is no longer an action item to confirm ink after first production run for purposes of updating frontmatter — the rule sets a fixed coverage assumption regardless of actual measurement.

---

> ## INVOICE PROTECTION (§26) — TIER BOUNDARY CLIFFS RESOLVED AUTOMATICALLY
>
> Per `PRICING_RULES.md` §26 — the buyer will never be invoiced more for ordering a smaller quantity than they would pay for a larger quantity at the next tier. Where math creates a cliff, the buyer is charged whichever total is lower.
>
> **For this item, two cliffs exist and both are resolved automatically:**
>
> - **9/10 boundary:** 9 × $7.25 = $65.25 vs 10 × $5.75 = $57.50. Buyer ordering 9 units is invoiced at $57.50, not $65.25.
> - **19/20 boundary:** 19 × $5.75 = $109.25 vs 20 × $4.75 = $95.00. Buyer ordering 19 units is invoiced at $95.00, not $109.25.
>
> Both cliffs are the same pattern that exists on every other multi-tier item on this account (1205720, 3010707, etc.). The §26 principle handles them transparently — no quote-email language required.

---

> ## DIMENSIONAL SCOPE — BAND EXCLUSION (DIMENSIONAL, NOT PRICING)
>
> At 0.292 sq ft, 1210810 falls below the singles band scope (~0.5–2.0 sq ft). It is **excluded from the singles Pricing Profile band as a DATA POINT** until production-volume acceptance is confirmed by Nick.
>
> **This is a dimensional exclusion, NOT a pricing exception.** The validated $/sq ft rate at qty 20-49 ($16.27/sq ft) is INTENTIONALLY ABOVE the singles band ($15.43–$15.91/sq ft) — the structurally required small-format premium per the 4-wave Wave 4 unanimous verdict. Smaller labels carry HIGHER fixed cost per sq ft.
>
> **Do NOT:**
> - Use 1210810 as a benchmark for any other item until production-volume acceptance is confirmed
> - Add 1210810 to the singles Pricing Profile band data points without Nick's explicit direction
> - Use the validated above-band $/sq ft to argue for below-band pricing on any future item in this size range — the principle is that smaller carries higher, not the inverse

---

> ## INITIAL ORDER — QTY 10 — $57.50
>
> Initial order from Sean Finn: qty 10.
>
> - **10-19 tier applies** ($5.75/label, locked 2026-06-01 under 4-wave validation). Qty 10 falls in the 10-19 tier.
> - **Total: $57.50** ($5.75 × 10).
>
> Quote email anchor (validated Wave 3): **"Label measures 10.5" × 4" (0.292 sq ft) in cast vinyl with polyester overlaminate — priced at $4.75 at your projected monthly volume."**
> The anchor uses the 20-49 recurring rate ($4.75), not the initial 10-19 rate ($5.75), because the long-term economics of the item are defined by the recurring tier.
>
> Include label dimensions in the quote email — all 6 Wave 3 buyer simulations flagged dimensions as essential context.

---

> ## PROJECTED VOLUME AND RECURRING TIER
>
> Projected volume: ~30/month starting 2027. At 30/month, every recurring order lands in the **20-49 tier** at **$4.75/label**. The initial order (qty 10) is noise — the 20-49 tier defines the long-term economics of this item.
>
> **30/month recurring = $142.50/month = $1,710/year at qty 20-49 tier.** This item is worth pricing correctly for the long term.
>
> **200+ warehouse caution:** Nick will caution Sean against placing large warehouse orders (100+ labels). Label integrity degrades in non-climate-controlled storage. The 100-199 and 200+ tiers exist for structural completeness only — they are not expected to be used under normal ordering patterns.

---

> ## PRICING NORMALIZATION NOTE (INTERNAL ONLY — DO NOT SURFACE TO BUYER)
>
> The current account band ($15.43–$15.91/sq ft at qty 20) is a relationship concession, set during the displacement phase to remain competitive while establishing Pro Label as the technical expert vendor. This band sits below industry standard for premium cast vinyl + 1-mil polyester laminate ANSI safety labels (industry range: approximately $18–$22/sq ft at qty 20 at a typical commercial shop).
>
> **Nick's plan:** Gradually normalize pricing toward industry standard by January 1, 2027, leveraging the technical partnership status now established. This normalization plan is strategic context for future pricing sessions — it does NOT change any current price on any other item.
>
> **For 1210810 specifically:** the validated $16.27/sq ft at qty 20-49 is intentionally above the singles band — the structurally required small-format premium per Wave 4 verdict, NOT a normalization step. Future normalization on the broader account will raise other items' $/sq ft; the 1210810 small-format premium is independent of that trajectory and stays.
>
> **Do NOT surface this note, the industry standard comparison, or the normalization plan to the buyer in any form.** Internal only.

---

> ## ARTWORK PREP — $0
>
> Elliott Equipment Company provides production-ready DWG files for every item on this account, without exception. File prep, artwork preparation, design time, and pre-press labor are zero-cost, zero-time inputs on every Elliott job. Step 1 (file import/setup) is a ~5-minute mechanical operation. This is a permanent account-level rule. See `PRICING_RULES.md` §22-24.

---

> ## AI VALIDATION — COMPLETE (4-WAVE ATOMIC PROCESS)
>
> 4-wave, 6-model atomic AI validation per `governance/VALIDATION_PROMPTS.md` is complete (24 total independent responses). Full documentation in the Pricing Derivation section.
>
> **Key outcomes:**
> - Wave 1 (Build): 4 of 5 valid responses landed $4.51–$4.64 at qty 20; central tendency $4.58. 1 outlier at $6.00 argued small-format premium ABOVE band.
> - Wave 2 (Destruction): 6/6 H severity on Buyer, Competitor, and Strategic attacks. Primary finding: 10-19 inversion (smaller label at lower $/sq ft than benchmark same tier). Secondary: material cost understated at $0.55.
> - Wave 3 (Buyer Simulation): All 6 models calculated $15.41/sq ft and recognized benchmark consistency. $4.50 cleared without a fight. Anchor: "You will never be invoiced more for a smaller quantity."
> - Wave 4 (Final Synthesis): **UNANIMOUS NO** on the Wave 3 table — 10-19 inversion must be corrected. Fix range $5.25–$5.95, center $5.40.
> - Nick's final decision: Raised the full table above benchmark $/sq ft at every tier. Small-format premium consistent and defensible. Material cost corrected to $0.60 under account-wide full bleed ink rule (established same session).

sections.pricing_derivation:
## Method Summary

**Singles band data point — validated through the full 4-wave atomic AI process.** At 0.560 sq ft this item routes to the standard singles band (≥0.5 sq ft) per the category decision tree. $8.75 at qty 20 = **$15.63/sq ft** — inside the active singles band ($15.43–$15.91/sq ft at qty 20) and between both existing data points. This is the **third data point in the singles band** and its first interior point.

## Benchmark Comparison

| Item | Sq Ft | $/Label qty 20 | $/Sq Ft qty 20 | Status | Role |
|------|-------|----------------|----------------|--------|------|
| 1068270 | 0.503 | $8.00 | $15.91 | Quoted | **Primary benchmark — nearest neighbor by area** |
| **1267140 (this item)** | **0.560** | **$8.75** | **$15.63** | **Quoted** | **Third band data point (interior)** |
| 1230820 | 1.296 | $20.00 | $15.43 | FA Accepted | Root benchmark |

The band's $/sq ft gradient is monotonic by size: the smaller 0.503 sq ft items carry $15.91/sq ft, this 0.560 sq ft item carries $15.63/sq ft, and the 1.296 sq ft root benchmark carries $15.43/sq ft. Smaller labels carry equal-or-higher $/sq ft — consistent with the binding Wave 4 verdict from the 1210810 revalidation.

## Profile Validation (PRICING_RULES §15)

- **Category:** Printed + Laminated (Orajet 3951 Cast + Polyester Lam)
- **Profile band (singles, ≥0.5 sq ft, at qty 20):** $15.43–$15.91/sq ft
- **This item:** $8.75 / 0.560 = **$15.63/sq ft — within band**, between both existing data points

Rule 15 satisfied. No band adjustment required; the band gains an interior data point.

## §26 Invoice Protection Check

The final tier ladder is **strictly decreasing** per label ($13.00 > $10.50 > $8.75 > $7.50 > $7.25 > $6.50) — no per-unit tier inversion at any boundary. (The pre-correction table DID contain an inversion: 100-199 at $6.25 below 200+ at $6.50 — fixed by Nick's Wave 4 correction, see the validation record below.)

Tier-boundary cliff verification (buyer is charged the lesser of adjacent tier totals at billing time per `PRICING_RULES.md` §26):

| Boundary | Lower-tier max total | Upper-tier min total | Cliff? | Charged at billing |
|----------|----------------------|----------------------|--------|--------------------|
| 9 → 10 | 9 × $13.00 = $117.00 | 10 × $10.50 = $105.00 | Yes ($12.00 inversion) | $105.00 |
| 19 → 20 | 19 × $10.50 = $199.50 | 20 × $8.75 = $175.00 | Yes ($24.50 inversion) | $175.00 |
| 49 → 50 | 49 × $8.75 = $428.75 | 50 × $7.50 = $375.00 | Yes ($53.75 inversion) | $375.00 |
| 99 → 100 | 99 × $7.50 = $742.50 | 100 × $7.25 = $725.00 | Yes ($17.50 inversion) | $725.00 |
| 199 → 200 | 199 × $7.25 = $1,442.75 | 200 × $6.50 = $1,300.00 | Yes ($142.75 inversion) | $1,300.00 |

§26 covers every boundary cleanly. Same pattern as every other multi-tier item on this account.

## 4-Wave AI Validation Record

24 independent responses (6 models × 4 waves), atomic sessions per `governance/VALIDATION_PROMPTS.md`.

### Wave 1 — Build Round (6 models, no anchor exposure)

**Result:** 6/6 models independently proposed **$8.64–$8.80 at qty 20**. Central tendency **$8.75**. All six models anchored to the singles band ($15.43–$15.91/sq ft); no model went outside the band. **Engine consensus.**

### Wave 2 — Destruction Round (6 models, fresh chats)

- **Strategic attack — H (4/6):** $15.63/sq ft at qty 20 cements the band as a third data point, complicating the January 2027 normalization.
- **Primary structural finding:** the original 200+ tier at **$4.75 ($8.48/sq ft) sat 45% below the active band floor** and would have created an indefensible procurement anchor in Sean's file. **5/6 models flagged 200+ as the weakest tier.**
- **Buyer attack — M/H (split):** the qty-20 price passes Sean's model without friction; the curve breaks at 100-199 and 200+.
- **Proposed modification:** raise 200+ to $6.50.

### Wave 3 — Buyer Simulation (6 models, role-playing Sean Finn)

**Result:** **6/6 simulations issued the PO as-is at $8.75 for qty 20. Zero questions sent.**

- **Pushback threshold:** $9.24–$9.52 (safe zone confirmed).
- **Instant approval:** $8.40–$8.90.
- All 6 confirmed Sean will log **$15.43–$15.91/sq ft as his permanent procurement baseline**.
- **Quote email anchor line confirmed:** *"Pricing is based on the same cast vinyl/polyester overlaminate construction as your prior Pro Label orders."*

### Wave 4 — Final Synthesis (6 models, cold atomic sessions)

**Result:** **6/6 UNANIMOUS NO on the original table — specifically the 200+ tier.** 6/6 kept $8.75 at qty 20 unchanged. 5/6 recommended 200+ → $6.50. M1 also flagged the **100-199 cascade inversion** — 100-199 at $6.25 cannot sit below 200+ at $6.50 (§26 inversion).

### Nick's Decision

Accepted the Wave 4 consensus with the 100-199/200+ correction: **raised 100-199 to $7.25 and 200+ to $6.50** to fix the cascade and protect the band. $8.75 at qty 20 unchanged. No other modifications (the 1-9 / 10-19 / 20-49 / 50-99 tiers carried through unchanged). **No override type — engine consensus accepted.**

| Tier | Original (rejected) | Final (locked) | Change |
|------|---------------------|----------------|--------|
| 1-9 | $13.00 | $13.00 | — |
| 10-19 | $10.50 | $10.50 | — |
| 20-49 | $8.75 | $8.75 | — |
| 50-99 | $7.50 | $7.50 | — |
| 100-199 | $6.25 | **$7.25** | Raised — fixes the §26 cascade inversion vs 200+ |
| 200+ | $4.75 | **$6.50** | Raised — $4.75 ($8.48/sq ft) sat 45% below the band floor; indefensible procurement anchor |

### Per-Sq-Ft Position at Every Tier

| Tier | Price/Label | Per Sq Ft | Margin (material-only) |
|------|------------|-----------|------------------------|
| 1-9 | $13.00 | $23.21 | ~91.6% |
| 10-19 | $10.50 | $18.75 | ~89.6% |
| 20-49 | $8.75 | $15.63 | ~87.5% |
| 50-99 | $7.50 | $13.39 | ~85.5% |
| 100-199 | $7.25 | $12.95 | ~85.0% |
| 200+ | $6.50 | $11.61 | ~83.2% |

The raised tail (100-199/200+) keeps the deep-volume $/sq ft from collapsing far below the band — tier compression on this item is 50% ($13.00 → $6.50), intentionally flatter than the legacy singles curves (63–74%). This is band protection, not a volume-reward outlier.

sections.notes:
- **Description and model are BLANK on the drawing.** Documented as blank per Nick's explicit session instruction — never invented. The P/N (1267140) is the sole identifier. If a future drawing revision adds title-block text, update the frontmatter `description`/`model` and this note; do not backfill from artwork guesses.
- **Third singles-band data point.** $8.75 at qty 20 = $15.63/sq ft — the band's first interior point, between 1230820 ($15.43 at 1.296 sq ft) and 1082570/1068270 ($15.91 at 0.503 sq ft). The band $/sq ft gradient is now monotonic by size across three sq-ft positions.
- **Tier tail is band protection.** The 100-199 ($7.25) and 200+ ($6.50) tiers were raised from the original $6.25/$4.75 per Wave 2/4 findings: the original 200+ implied $8.48/sq ft, 45% below the band floor — an indefensible procurement anchor — and the original 100-199 sat below the corrected 200+ (§26 cascade inversion). Tier compression is 50% (flatter than the legacy 63–74% singles curves) by design. Do NOT "fix" this curve back toward the steeper legacy compression in a future session.
- **Forced lamination orientation.** 15.00" exceeds the 13.5" laminator; the 5.38" dimension feeds across (8.12" clearance). Single orientation group, 1 pass, 1 label per row at 15.00" across the 28" print bed. The constraint is Pro Label's to absorb — never surfaced to the buyer.
- **Quote email anchor line (Wave 3, confirmed by all 6):** *"Pricing is based on the same cast vinyl/polyester overlaminate construction as your prior Pro Label orders."*
- **§26 invoice protection** applies at every tier boundary (9/10, 19/20, 49/50, 99/100, 199/200) — buyer is always charged the lesser of adjacent tier totals at billing time. The locked per-label ladder itself is strictly decreasing (no per-unit inversion).
- **Artwork prep $0.** Elliott provides production-ready DWG files for every item on this account, without exception (PRICING_RULES.md §22-24).
- **Full bleed ink standard.** Material cost includes full bleed ink at $0.50/sq ft per the account-wide rule (PRICING_RULES.md §25): `(0.560 × $1.21) + (0.560 × $0.2389) + (0.560 × $0.50) = $1.0914 → $1.09 filed`. Ink cost is locked at the §25 rate regardless of artwork color.
- **No MOQ.** No minimum order quantity on this account. Permanent account-level rule.
- **First article:** not requested, not offered.

## Internal Only — Do NOT Surface to Buyer

> The note below is internal-only strategic context. Never mentioned in any quote, email, invoice, or conversation with Sean or any Elliott contact.

- **INTERNAL ONLY — January 2027 normalization anchor:** Normalizing from the $8.75 ($15.63/sq ft) anchor requires approximately **$1.33–$3.57/label of uplift** to reach the $18–$22/sq ft industry-standard target ($18 × 0.560 = $10.08; $22 × 0.560 = $12.32). The normalization argument **must be anchored to relationship-phase transition language — NOT per-label rate arguments.** Sean now has **three accepted/quoted data points at $15.43–$15.91/sq ft** and will use them as his procurement baseline (Wave 3: all 6 buyer simulations confirmed he logs the band permanently).

sections.production:
> **No file prep / artwork prep / design time / pre-press in this process.** Elliott supplies a production-ready DWG. Step 1 is a ~5-minute mechanical file import, not labor. See `PRICING_RULES.md` §22.

## Process Steps (~25 minutes total job time for the entire 20-label program)

1. **File import/setup** — open the Elliott-supplied production-ready DWG, send to RIP. ~5 min mechanical for the whole nested job (one click per file; the 4 designs are already production-ready).
2. **Print** — Roland SG3-300, full-color eco-solvent on Orajet 3951. All 4 designs nested in a single small print area; one print pass.
3. **Laminate** — 1-mil clear polyester overlay, single pass through the 13.5" laminator (the entire sheet of 20 labels easily fits).
4. **Kiss cut** — Roland contour cut, face stock only, liner intact. Slit liner across center per drawing spec.
5. **Inspect** — print quality, laminate adhesion, kiss-cut depth, text legibility (small format — readability check is critical).
6. **Package** — flat, on liner, ready for hand application on the control panel.

Steps 2-6 total ~20 minutes for the entire 20-label job. Combined with the 5-minute file import, total job time is ~25 minutes.

## Production Notes

- **Lamination passes:** 1 (entire program fits in a single pass)
- **Cut runs:** 1
- **Equipment:** Roland SG3-300 + 13.5" polyester laminator (same equipment as all Orajet 3951 work)

sections.pricing_derivation:
> **⚠ ONE-OFF PROGRAM. NOT A CATALOG PRICE. NOT A BENCHMARK.** Sean Finn verbatim: *"this will be a one off for a custom build we are doing."* The $2.75/label number is a job-economics floor for a 20-label minimum-run program — it must NEVER be used as a comparable, anchor, or benchmark for any future Elliott item, including future one-off programs. Future one-offs price from their own job economics (material + production time), not from this number. The implied ~$343/sq ft is a minimum-run artifact, not a per-sq-ft rate.

**Methodology:** Job economics for a one-off minimum-run program. Built from material cost + production time, anchored to the account's one-off job-economics floor (1230820 FA-anchored).

**Job inputs:**

| Component | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Material cost (full 20-label job) | $4.85 (3.125 sq ft Orajet 3951 + 2.8125 sq ft laminate + ~$0.40 ink — laminate at $0.2389/sq ft, updated 2026-05-28) |
| File prep / artwork prep / design time / pre-press | **$0** — Elliott supplies production-ready DWG (account-level rule, see `PRICING_RULES.md` §22) |
| Production time (file import + print/lam/cut/inspect/package) | ~25 min total (5 min file import + 20 min run) |
| Lamination passes | 1 |
| Cut runs | 1 |

**Why $55 program total:** $55 is the lowest first article price on the account (P/N 1230820 FA at $55) and is treated here as a program-level one-off job-economics floor (1230820 FA-anchored) — the lowest number Pro Label will run a custom one-off for. It is the floor, NOT a per-label comparable. Anchoring at this floor:

- Program revenue: $55
- Material cost: ~$6.94
- Production time: ~25 min
- Contribution margin: ~$48 to cover the ~25 minutes of machine + operator time + overhead
- That is the worthwhile-charge threshold for a 4-design one-off at this scale; below it the job is not worth running

**Why the singles sq ft band is inapplicable:** At 0.008 sq ft, the singles band ($15.43/sq ft at qty 20) yields $0.12/label — nonsensical, does not cover the run.

**Why this is NOT a benchmark:**

- It is a one-off, not a catalog item. Sean stated non-recurring intent.
- The per-sq-ft implied rate (~$343/sq ft) is structurally meaningless — it is the arithmetic byproduct of dividing a minimum-run floor by a tiny label area.
- Using this item to anchor a future tiny-label quote would either (a) under-price if the future job has a different design count / footprint / production routing, or (b) over-price if the future job nests inside an existing run.
- Future tiny-label one-offs must rebuild job economics from scratch (material + production time on their actual footprint), then anchor to whichever account floor is appropriate at the time. Do NOT shortcut through this item.

**Tier structure:** All six tiers flattened to $2.75. There is no volume tier for a one-off — Sean ordered qty 5 and will not reorder. The flat tier table is a structural placeholder; the operational price is the program total.

**Profile Validation:** Outside the Printed + Laminated singles Pricing Profile band by design. Founding data point for the sub-0.1 sq ft size class is preserved on this item ONLY in the sense that this item documents the one-off pricing approach — the **price itself is NOT a Profile data point** and must NOT be fed into the singles or any other band.

**AI Validation:** Full 4-round, 6-model validation completed (24 total model runs) per `governance/PRICING_VALIDATION.md`. Verdict: unanimous **send $55 program total as shown**.

| Round | Purpose | Result |
|-------|---------|--------|
| 1 — Build | Establish baseline from scratch | **Unanimous $55** across all 6 models. Each independently anchored to the account FA floor (P/N 1230820 FA at $55), recognized the singles sq ft band as inapplicable at 0.008 sq ft, and converged on minimum-program-charge logic. |
| 2 — Destruction | Attack from hostile angles | **5 of 6 Yes / Yes with modifications.** One outlier model (1 of 6) recommended $38 program total on a pure cost-plus basis. Outlier rejected — see "Rejected $38 alternative" below. Surviving modifications were quote-language refinements only, not price changes. |
| 3 — Buyer Simulation | Role-play Sean's reaction | **All 6 models approved without questions.** All 6 placed the instant-approval threshold at **$75 or under** for a 20-label one-off program. The $55 quote lands comfortably inside that threshold. No buyer pushback predicted. No per-label math expected to be triggered if the quote is presented as a single program line. |
| 4 — Final Synthesis | Validate the final decision | **All 6 unanimous: Yes, send as shown.** No tier-level concerns. No long-term precedent concerns (item is explicitly excluded from being a benchmark by category file and item-level warnings). No discomfort flags. |

**Rejected $38 alternative.** One Round-2 model proposed $38 program total on cost-plus reasoning: material ($6.94) + ~25 min production × ~$60/hr loaded labor (~$25) + overhead (~$6) ≈ $38. Rejected because:

- Cost-plus logic ignores the existing account floor. Pro Label has already established $55 as the lowest worthwhile charge on this account (P/N 1230820 FA). Quoting a one-off custom program below the catalog FA floor would undercut the account's own price structure.
- Cost-plus logic ignores relationship context. The customer is actively displacing the incumbent and has confirmed non-recurring intent — there is no reorder runway to amortize a thin one-off margin against.
- The $17 spread between $38 and $55 is below any buyer's sensitivity threshold for a 20-label program (Round 3 confirmed instant-approval up to $75). Capturing the extra $17 has zero customer-relationship cost.
- $55 protects the floor for the next one-off; $38 sets a precedent that future tiny one-offs must beat to stay aligned.

**Validated quote language (send verbatim — Round 4 confirmed no changes needed):**

> Outrigger switch label program — 4 P/Ns (Horizontal Front, Vertical Front, Horizontal Rear, Vertical Rear), qty 5 each, 20 labels total. Cast 4-mil vinyl, black on white, 1-mil clear polyester overlay, kiss cut. Customer-supplied production-ready DWG files — no artwork or setup fees applied.
>
> One-time minimum program charge for custom build support: **$55.00 total**.
>
> This is a one-time program price for your custom build and is not a catalog or reorder rate. If any of these become repeat-production items, we'll requote at production quantities — expect pricing in the $0.35–$0.55/label range at qty 250+.

**Override:** None.

**Repricing history:**
1. Initial quote 2026-05-27 AM: $7/label = $140 program total. Based on a job-floor estimate.
2. Reprice 2026-05-27 PM: $15/label = $300 program total. Used "file prep × 4 unique designs" as a major cost driver (~$60-80 of labor).
3. **Correction 2026-05-27 evening (this version): $2.75/label = $55 program total.** Elliott supplies production-ready DWG; file prep is NOT a cost input on this account. The $15/label reprice was built on an incorrect assumption that has now been purged account-wide. With file prep at $0, true job inputs are ~$6.94 material + ~25 min production time. The worthwhile-charge floor is the account's lowest FA price ($55), not a reconstructed labor-heavy figure.

---

The internal P&L benchmark (~79.7%) is a recurring-production margin and is not directly comparable to a one-off; this $55 program does not have repeat-run amortization to lean on, and its margin is best evaluated as contribution per machine-minute rather than as a percentage.

sections.nesting:
Identical to program peer P/N 1277970. The 4 unique designs nest together on a single small print/lam/cut run; the entire 20-label program runs as one print pass and one lam pass. See P/N 1277970 for the full footprint breakdown.

## Production Footprint (Nick-verified)

| Resource | Footprint |
|----------|-----------|
| Print vinyl (Orajet 3951) | 15" × 30" = 3.125 sq ft |
| Laminate (1-mil polyester) | 13.5" × 30" = 2.8125 sq ft |
| Lamination passes | 1 |
| Labels produced | 20 (5 × 4 P/Ns) |

## Material Cost Summary

Costs reference `governance/PRODUCTION.md`.

| Component | Calculation | Cost |
|-----------|-------------|------|
| Vinyl (Orajet 3951) | 3.125 sq ft × $1.21/sq ft | $3.78 |
| Laminate (1-mil polyester) | 2.8125 sq ft × $0.2389/sq ft | $0.67 |
| Ink (eco-solvent, low coverage) | 20 × ~$0.02 | $0.40 |
| **Total — full 20-label job** | | **$4.85** |
| **Per label** | $4.85 ÷ 20 | **$0.24** |
| **Per P/N (qty 5)** | $4.85 ÷ 4 | **$1.21** |

## Label Geometry Reference

| Spec | Value |
|------|-------|
| Label shape | Circle, Ø1-3/16" |
| Bounding box | 1.1875" × 1.1875" |
| Circle area | π × (0.59375)² = 1.107 sq in = 0.00768 sq ft |
| Frontmatter sq ft | 0.008 (circle area, not bounding box) |

---
sections.production:
> **No file prep / artwork prep / design time / pre-press in this process.** Elliott supplies a production-ready DWG. Step 1 is a ~5-minute mechanical file import for the whole nested job, not labor. See `PRICING_RULES.md` §22.

Identical to program peer P/N 1277970.

1. **File import/setup** — open the Elliott-supplied production-ready DWG, send to RIP. ~5 min mechanical for the nested job.
2. **Print** — Roland SG3-300, full-color eco-solvent on Orajet 3951. Nested with the other 3 program designs in a single small print area.
3. **Laminate** — 1-mil clear polyester, single pass through the 13.5" laminator.
4. **Kiss cut** — Roland contour cut, face stock only, liner intact. Slit liner across center per drawing spec.
5. **Inspect** — print quality, laminate adhesion, kiss-cut depth, text legibility.
6. **Package** — flat, on liner, ready for hand application.

## Production Notes

- **Lamination passes:** 1 (entire program fits in a single pass)
- **Cut runs:** 1
- **Equipment:** Roland SG3-300 + 13.5" polyester laminator
- **Total job time across all 4 designs:** ~25 min (5 min file import + 20 min run)

---
sections.pricing_derivation:
> **⚠ ONE-OFF PROGRAM. NOT A CATALOG PRICE. NOT A BENCHMARK.** Sean Finn verbatim: *"this will be a one off for a custom build we are doing."* The $2.75/label number is a job-economics floor for a 20-label minimum-run program — it must NEVER be used as a comparable, anchor, or benchmark for any future Elliott item. The implied ~$343/sq ft is a minimum-run artifact, not a per-sq-ft rate.

**Methodology:** Program peer to P/N 1277970. Identical specs, identical pricing, same one-off program.

**Why pricing matches 1277970:** All four labels share identical dimensions, material, and process. The only variable is directional text content. Run-time and material consumption per label are identical. The 4 designs nest together in a single print/lam/cut job, and the entire program is priced as a single $55 minimum-worthwhile-charge job.

**Job inputs (full 20-label program):**

| Component | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Material cost | $4.85 (3.125 sq ft vinyl + 2.8125 sq ft laminate + ink — laminate at $0.2389/sq ft, updated 2026-05-28) |
| File prep / artwork prep / design time / pre-press | **$0** — Elliott supplies production-ready DWG (account-level rule, see `PRICING_RULES.md` §22) |
| Production time | ~25 min (5 min file import + 20 min print/lam/cut/inspect/package) |
| Program revenue at $55 total | $55 |
| Contribution margin | ~$48 to cover ~25 min of machine + operator time + overhead |

**Why $55 program total:** Anchors to the lowest first article price on the account (P/N 1230820 FA at $55), treated here as a program-level one-off job-economics floor (1230820 FA-anchored) — NOT as a per-label comparable. See P/N 1277970's Pricing Derivation for the full reasoning.

**Why this is NOT a benchmark:** See 1277970's Pricing Derivation. Future tiny-label one-offs must rebuild job economics from scratch — do NOT shortcut through this item.

**Profile Validation:** Outside the Printed + Laminated singles Pricing Profile band by design. Explicitly excluded from being a Profile data point.

**AI Validation:** Inherits the full 4-round, 6-model validation completed for the program (24 total runs) — see P/N 1277970 Pricing Derivation for the detailed round-by-round summary. Round 1 unanimous $55, Round 2 5-of-6 yes with one $38 outlier rejected, Round 3 all 6 buyer simulations approved without questions (instant-approval threshold confirmed at $75 or under), Round 4 unanimous "send as shown." The validated quote language (verbatim) is captured in 1277970's Pricing Derivation and applies to the whole 4-P/N program.

**Override:** None.

**Repricing history:**
1. $7/label = $140 program total (2026-05-27 AM, job-floor estimate)
2. $15/label = $300 program total (2026-05-27 PM, used file-prep labor as cost driver)
3. **$2.75/label = $55 program total (2026-05-27 evening, this version, after account-wide artwork-prep purge).** Elliott supplies production-ready DWG; file prep is NOT a cost input on this account. The $15/label reprice was built on an incorrect assumption that has now been purged account-wide.

---

| Metric | Value (full program) |
|--------|----------------------|
| Program revenue (20 labels × $2.75) | $55.00 |
| Material cost | $4.85 |
| Production time | ~25 min (no file prep) |
| Contribution per minute of production time | ~$2.01/min ≈ $121/hr |

**Interpretation:** Healthy contribution-per-machine-minute for a one-off minimum-run custom job. The internal P&L benchmark (~79.7%) is a recurring-production margin and is not directly comparable.

sections.notes:
> ## ⚠ ONE-OFF PROGRAM
>
> Sean Finn confirmed in writing: *"one off for a custom build we are doing."* No reorder expected or implied. These four items (1277970, 1277980, 1277990, 1278000) exist as a single 20-label minimum-run program for a custom 18T crane build. The $55 program total is the only operationally relevant number.

> ## ⚠ DO NOT BENCHMARK
>
> $2.75/label is a minimum-run artifact. **Never** use as anchor, comparable, or catalog rate for any future Elliott item. The implied ~$343/sq ft is meaningless as a per-sq-ft reference. If any future session reaches for this item as a benchmark, STOP and re-read this section.

> ## ⚠ DO NOT SURFACE PER-LABEL MATH
>
> The quote was validated (4 rounds, 6 models, 24 runs) as a single program line: "**one-time minimum program charge for custom build support: $55.00 total**." The $2.75/label per-label number must **never** appear in a customer-facing document. If asked to break it out, decline and reframe as a program charge.

> ## ⚠ REORDER PRICING
>
> If any of these P/Ns are ever reordered, **reprice from scratch at production quantities.** Expected production-quantity range: **$0.35–$0.55/label at qty 250+** (per the validated quote language sent to Sean).

> ## ⚠ ACCOUNT FLOOR
>
> $55 is the validated minimum program charge for one-off custom builds on this account where Elliott supplies production-ready DWG files. **Do not go below this on future similar jobs.** The Round-2 outlier $38 alternative was rejected on exactly this principle.

> ## ⚠ ARTWORK PREP
>
> Elliott supplies production-ready DWG files on **every** job. File prep, artwork prep, design time, and pre-press cost are **always zero** on this account. This is a permanent account-level truth, not a per-job assumption. See `.claude/MASTER_CONTEXT.md` Core Rule #7 and `governance/PRICING_RULES.md` §22-24.
