sections.item_overview:
- **Part Number:** 1278220
- **Drawing Title:** LBL-MAX JIB CAP 1500 TIP HZRD
- **Description:** LBL-MAX JIB CAP 1500 TIP HZRD
- **Type:** Printed/Laminated Label (single)
- **Content:** Max jib capacity 1500 lbs / tip-over hazard WARNING label. Per the parity declaration, same two-zone ANSI Z535.4 WARNING layout as 1279000 — capacity callout zone ("MAXIMUM JIB CAPACITY / 1500 LBS") plus orange WARNING header with alert symbol and tip-over hazard body text. (Artwork content differs from 1279000 — 1279000 covers max platform capacity 1200 lbs; 1278220 covers max jib capacity 1500 lbs.)
- **Dimensions:** 8.00" W × 1.75" H — **0.0972 sq ft** (8.00 × 1.75 / 144). Frontmatter rounds to 0.097 at 3 decimal places. Identical to 1279000.
- **Corners:** R0.25 (parity with 1279000).
- **Tolerances:** ±1/16" (account default).
- **DWG:** No PDF artifact attached this session — direct parity clone declared off the 1279000 spec (drawing dated 06-05-2026).
- **Material:** Orajet 3951 cast vinyl + 1-mil clear polyester overlaminate. Slit in backing for application.
- **Customer:** Elliott Equipment Company — Sean Finn.
- **First Article:** Not requested, not offered.
- **Status:** Quoted (2026-06-12).

---
sections.material_spec:
- **Substrate:** Orajet 3951 cast vinyl, white, adhesive back (~2 mil). Account-standard callout: "CAST VINYL (5-7 YEAR) ADHESIVE BACKED, WITH PEEL-OFF BACKING."
- **Adhesive:** Permanent pressure-sensitive.
- **Laminate:** 1-mil clear polyester overlaminate — gloss finish (Flexcon FLX000233). Account-standard mapping of "1 MIL CLEAR VINYL OVERLAY" (Mylar / "vinyl overlay" / polyester are used interchangeably across Elliott drawings).
- **Laminator compatibility:**
  - 1.75" across the 13.5" laminator: 11.75" clearance ✓
  - 8.00" across the 13.5" laminator: 5.5" clearance ✓
  - Either orientation yields a single lamination pass.
- **Application Tape:** N/A (printed/laminated item — contour cut, no transfer tape).
- **Backing:** Slit in backing for application (parity with 1279000).
- **Print:** Black, red, and orange per ANSI Z535.4 WARNING layout (parity with 1279000). Full bleed / full coverage per account standard (`PRICING_RULES.md` §25). Orange WARNING header and alert symbol must meet ANSI Z535.4 color specification.

Material specification is identical to P/N 1279000 in every component.

> **Ink cost (account-wide full bleed standard, established 2026-06-01):** $0.50/sq ft × 0.0972 sq ft = $0.0486/label. Account-wide rule per `PRICING_RULES.md` §25 — all printed/laminated items on this account are priced at full bleed / full coverage at all times. An incidental buffer is applied to the total material cost (judgment-applied conservative round-up; see Nesting and Material Cost below).

---
sections.nesting:
### Laminator Pass Count

Both the 8.00" width and 1.75" height dimensions are well within the 13.5" polyester laminator. Either orientation yields a single lamination pass. No laminator complications. Identical to 1279000.

- 1.75" across laminator: 11.75" clearance — single pass ✓
- 8.00" across laminator: 5.5" clearance — single pass ✓

### Print Layout and Nesting

Label: 8.00" × 1.75". Roland SG3-300 max print width: 28". Nesting identical to 1279000.

**Orientation A** (8.00" across the 28" bed, 1.75" in feed direction):
- Labels per row: 3 (3 × 8.00" = 24" ≤ 28")
- At qty 20: ~7 rows × 3 labels = 21 labels in one print run ✓

**Orientation B** (1.75" across the 28" bed, 8.00" in feed direction):
- Labels per row: 16 (16 × 1.75" = 28" exactly)
- At qty 20: 2 rows × 16 = 32 → fits 20 in one row + start a second ✓

Nesting is highly efficient at recurring volume. Multiple labels per row, single print run at any expected order quantity.

### Material Cost Breakdown per Label

> Material cost version date: 2026-06-05 (same cost data vintage as benchmark 1279000). Unit costs from `governance/PRODUCTION.md` (Orajet 3951 verified 2026-04-22; 1-mil polyester lam verified 2026-05-28; full bleed ink standard verified 2026-06-01).
>
> **Account-wide full bleed ink rule (PRICING_RULES.md §25):** All printed/laminated items on this account are priced assuming full bleed / full coverage ink at all times. Rate: $0.50/sq ft × full label sq ft.

| Component | Calculation | Per-Label Cost |
|-----------|-------------|---------------|
| Orajet 3951 cast vinyl | 0.0972 sq ft × $1.21/sq ft | $0.1176 |
| 1-mil polyester overlaminate | 0.0972 sq ft × $0.2389/sq ft | $0.0232 |
| Eco-solvent ink (full bleed standard) | 0.0972 sq ft × $0.50/sq ft | $0.0486 |
| **Calculated total** | | **$0.1894** |
| Incidental buffer (conservative round-up) ¹ | judgment-applied | + $0.0106 |
| **Canonical `material_cost_per_unit`** | | **$0.20** |

¹ **Incidental buffer convention (account-wide rule per PRICING_RULES.md §25):** The calculated material total is rounded conservatively upward to account for setup scrap, registration pulls, and minor production waste on every Elliott printed/laminated item. The buffer is judgment-applied, not a fixed number. For this item the calculated $0.1894 rounds to $0.20 in the frontmatter `material_cost_per_unit` field.

Material cost matches P/N 1279000 ($0.20/label) by direct parity — identical sq ft, identical material family, identical full bleed ink treatment under PRICING_RULES.md §25.

---
sections.production:
1. File import/setup — open the Elliott-supplied production-ready DWG (registration marks already defined), send to RIP. ~5 min mechanical. NOT billable labor (see `PRICING_RULES.md` §22).
2. Print — Roland SG3-300, full-color eco-solvent on Orajet 3951. Black + red + orange per ANSI Z535.4 WARNING layout (parity with 1279000). Full bleed standard. Orange WARNING header and alert symbol must meet ANSI Z535.4 color specification.
3. Laminate — 1-mil clear polyester. ONE pass (both 8.00" and 1.75" dimensions fit the 13.5" laminator with substantial clearance).
4. Contour cut — Roland, R0.25 rounded corners per parity spec. Slit in backing.
5. Weed — remove excess material.
6. Inspect — print quality, laminate adhesion, cut accuracy, ANSI Z535.4 color compliance (orange WARNING header, alert symbol, black body, red elements), backing slit verification.
7. Package — flat, stacked.

- **Lamination Passes:** 1
- **Cut Runs:** 1
- **Estimated Production Time (batch of 20):** ~20-25 min total (5 min file import + 15-20 min print/lam/cut/weed/inspect/package).
- **Constraints:** None. All dimensions well within equipment limits on all passes.

Production process is identical to P/N 1279000 in every step.

sections.pricing_derivation:
> **DIRECT PARITY EXEMPTION APPLIED — NO MULTI-ROUND AI VALIDATION REQUIRED.**
>
> Per `governance/PRICING_VALIDATION.md`: "When NOT to use it: Items dimensionally and materially identical to something already quoted. Match the existing structure and move on."
>
> P/N 1278220 is dimensionally and materially identical to P/N 1279000 (LBL-MAX PLTF CAP 1200 TIP HZRD — Micro-Format Band founding data point). The parity exemption applies. Rule 15 (validate against the category Pricing Profile band) is the only validation still in force, and it is satisfied — see Step 3 below.

---

## Step 1 — Parity Comparison to Benchmark

| Attribute | 1279000 (Benchmark) | 1278220 (This Item) | Match |
|-----------|---------------------|---------------------|-------|
| Width | 8.00" | 8.00" | ✓ Identical |
| Height | 1.75" | 1.75" | ✓ Identical |
| Sq Ft per label | 0.097 | 0.097 | ✓ Identical |
| Label count | 1 | 1 | ✓ Identical |
| Material family | Orajet 3951 Cast + Polyester Lam | Orajet 3951 Cast + Polyester Lam | ✓ Identical |
| Lamination passes | 1 | 1 | ✓ Identical |
| Cut runs | 1 | 1 | ✓ Identical |
| Process | Print/Lam/Cut (1 pass) | Print/Lam/Cut (1 pass) | ✓ Identical |
| Material cost / label | $0.20 | $0.20 | ✓ Identical |

The two items differ only in (a) part number and (b) description/artwork content (max jib capacity 1500 lbs vs max platform capacity 1200 lbs). Dimensions are identical including orientation. Neither difference affects material cost, nesting, production process, or per-label labor — artwork prep is a $0 input on this account regardless (PRICING_RULES.md §22).

**Direct parity exemption applies per PRICING_VALIDATION.md.** Tier table and material cost cloned exactly from 1279000 with no modification.

---

## Step 2 — Tier Table Clone

| Tier | Price/Label | Per Sq Ft | Margin | Source |
|------|------------|-----------|--------|--------|
| 1-9 | $4.50 | $46.30 | 95.6% | Cloned from 1279000 |
| 10-19 | $3.50 | $36.01 | 94.3% | Cloned from 1279000 |
| 20-49 | $3.00 | $30.86 | 93.3% | Cloned from 1279000 |
| 50-99 | $2.60 | $26.75 | 92.3% | Cloned from 1279000 |
| 100-199 | $2.30 | $23.66 | 91.3% | Cloned from 1279000 |
| 200+ | $2.10 | $21.60 | 90.5% | Cloned from 1279000 |

The 1279000 table was locked 2026-06-05 after 4-wave atomic AI validation (24 independent responses, 6 models × 4 waves) plus a final 6-model comprehensive review (6/6 send as shown). No override. That entire validation record is inherited by this clone — see Step 4.

---

## Step 3 — Pricing Profile Band Check (Rule 15 — still applies)

The category **Micro-Format Band** (sub-0.1 sq ft printed/laminated, production volume) anchor at qty 20 is **$30.86/sq ft** — founded by 1279000 (2.0× the singles benchmark $15.43/sq ft).

1278220 at qty 20: $3.00 / 0.0972 = **$30.86/sq ft** — exact match to the band anchor. (Computed against the 3-decimal frontmatter rounding, $3.00 / 0.097 = $30.93/sq ft — a presentation-rounding artifact, same as documented on 1279000.)

Rule 15 satisfied. No band adjustment required. This is the correct band for this item: sub-0.1 sq ft AND a production catalog item (not a one-off / field-service request, which would route to the $55 tiny floor instead).

---

## Step 4 — Why No Multi-Round AI Validation Was Run

Per PRICING_VALIDATION.md: "Items dimensionally and materially identical to something already quoted. Match the existing structure and move on. Note: this exempts multi-round AI validation only — Rule 15 (validate against category Pricing Profile band) still applies to every new item regardless."

1278220 meets every dimensional and material parity criterion against 1279000 — exactly, including orientation. There is no new pricing question to validate, no new size class, no new material, no new process, and no new tier structure. Cloning the validated 1279000 tier table is the procedurally correct action.

The 1279000 tier table was itself the product of a full 4-wave atomic AI validation (6 models × 4 waves = 24 independent responses) plus a final 6-model comprehensive review (6/6 send as shown) — that validation is inherited by every direct parity clone of 1279000 going forward, including 1278220. Same exemption pattern as 1068270 (clone of 1082570).

**Reconciliation with the band's boundary-validation caution:** The Micro-Format Band documentation states that items at the boundary (~0.08–0.12 sq ft) "still require 4-wave AI validation — do not parity-quote from 1279000 without running the validation process." That caution governs dimensionally NEW items near the founding anchor's sq ft — items whose size differs from 0.097 sq ft and would therefore extend or stress the band. It does not apply to an exact-dimension, same-material, same-process clone, which is governed by the direct parity exemption in `governance/PRICING_VALIDATION.md`. 1278220 introduces no new sq ft data point: it sits at exactly 0.0972 sq ft and exactly $30.86/sq ft — the existing anchor, restated.

---

## Step 5 — Permanent Lockstep Link and Band Role

**The parity link is permanent.** If any dimension, material cost, process, or band parameter changes on 1279000 in a future session, 1278220 must be updated in lockstep — the link is a structural connection, not a one-time copy. (Same convention as the 1068270 ↔ 1082570 link.)

**1278220 is NOT a Micro-Format Band founding data point.** That status belongs to 1279000 alone. 1278220 inherits the validated pricing via the parity exemption; it does not independently anchor, tighten, or extend the band. The band's "Data points" count is unchanged by this item.

---

sections.notes:
> ## DIRECT PARITY CLONE OF 1279000
>
> P/N 1278220 is documented as a direct parity clone of P/N 1279000 (LBL-MAX PLTF CAP 1200 TIP HZRD — Micro-Format Band founding data point). All pricing, material cost, nesting math, production process, and tier structure are inherited unchanged. The two items differ only in part number and description/artwork content (max jib capacity 1500 lbs vs max platform capacity 1200 lbs). **No independent AI validation was run** — the direct parity exemption per `governance/PRICING_VALIDATION.md` applies, and 1279000's 4-wave validation (24 independent responses) plus final 6-model comprehensive review are inherited.
>

>
> The Micro-Format Band founding data point status belongs to **1279000 only**. 1278220 inherits the validated pricing via the parity exemption; it does not independently anchor, tighten, or extend the band, and it does not change the band's data point count. Future sub-0.1 sq ft production items validate against the band as founded by 1279000 — not against this clone.
>

---

> ## NO MULTI-ROUND AI VALIDATION
>
> Per PRICING_VALIDATION.md direct parity exemption. The 4-wave, 6-model atomic AI validation that established the 1279000 tier table (24 independent responses + final 6-model comprehensive review, 6/6 send as shown) is inherited by this item.

>

>
> Elliott Equipment Company provides production-ready DWG files for every item on this account, without exception. File prep, artwork preparation, design time, and pre-press labor are zero-cost, zero-time inputs on every Elliott job. Step 1 (file import/setup) is a ~5-minute mechanical operation. This is a permanent account-level rule. See `PRICING_RULES.md` §22-24.

> ## FULL BLEED INK STANDARD
>
> Material cost includes full bleed ink coverage at $0.50/sq ft per the account-wide rule established 2026-06-01 (PRICING_RULES.md §25). No partial, medium, or low coverage assumptions are permitted on any Elliott printed/laminated item. The $0.20/label material cost reflects the buffered total under the canonical formula:

> Ink cost is locked at the §25 full bleed rate regardless of artwork color — no recosting is triggered by any color decision on this item or its benchmark (1279000).

---

> ## NO MOQ / NO MINIMUM ORDER CHARGE / NO MINIMUM ORDER LANGUAGE
>
> Consistent with every other item on this account. No MOQ. No minimum order charge. No required quote language.

---

> ## INVOICE PROTECTION (§26) — TIER BOUNDARY VERIFICATION
>
> Tier table is identical to 1279000, so the boundary cliffs and their §26 resolutions are identical:

> - **9/10:** 9 × $4.50 = $40.50 vs 10 × $3.50 = $35.00 — cliff $5.50, resolved by §26 (buyer charged $35.00 for qty 9).
> - **19/20:** 19 × $3.50 = $66.50 vs 20 × $3.00 = $60.00 — cliff $6.50, resolved by §26 (buyer charged $60.00 for qty 19).

> All cliffs are resolved automatically at billing time by §26 invoice protection — no quote-email language required. Same pattern as every other multi-tier item on this account.

frontmatter.description: 2-Label Lifting Capacity Chart Kit, E160 V3

sections.item_overview:
- **Part Number:** 1278890
- **Description:** 2-Label Lifting Capacity Chart Kit, E160 V3
- **Type:** Printed/Laminated Kit (2 labels: LABEL-A, LABEL-B)
- **Content:**
  - **LABEL-A:** LABEL-LIFTING CAPACITY CHART / MODEL E160 V3 — rated capacity data table for the E160 V3 crane configuration.
  - **LABEL-B:** LABEL-LIFTING CAPACITY DIAGRAM / MODEL E160 V3 — range/boom diagram with logo positioned at top of chart (per ECO# 28830, 06/08/26).
  - Supplied as a matched two-label set.
- **Dimensions:** Both labels 7.88" W × 11.13" H (0.609 sq ft/label, 1.218 sq ft total/kit)
- **Corners:** 4× R.25 (rounded, confirmed from drawing 4X R .25 callout on both labels)
- **Tolerances:** ±1/16" (assumed account default)
- **DWG Date:** LABEL-A 03/04/26 Rev A (no further revisions); LABEL-B 03/04/26 Rev A, revised 06/08/26 ECO# 28830 (logo to top of charts, DJS)
- **Engineer:** DAW (revision by DJS)
- **Status:** Quoted (2026-06-09)

---
sections.material_spec:
- **Substrate:** Orajet 3951 cast vinyl, white, adhesive back (~2 mil) — drawing callout "Cast 2-2mil vinyl, adhesive back, peel-off backing slit at middle"
- **Adhesive:** Permanent pressure-sensitive
- **Laminate:** 1-mil clear polyester (Mylar) overlaminate — gloss finish. Drawing callout "1-mil clear Mylar overlay" maps to the standard 1-mil polyester per account precedent (Mylar is a polyester trade name; same convention used on 1245130, 1278930, 3017584).
- **Laminate Max Width:** 13.5" on current equipment — both labels fit single-orientation in one pass (7.88" across 13.5", 5.62" clearance).
- **Application Tape:** N/A
- **Ink:** Eco-solvent (Roland TR2), black on white, full bleed per account-wide ink coverage rule (PRICING_RULES.md §25 — established 2026-06-01).

---
sections.nesting:
- **Print Layout:** Both labels print in one run on the Roland SG3-300 (28" max print width). Labels arranged head-to-toe with a 90° rotation between them — overall layout footprint approximately 25" wide on the 28" print bed (3" clearance). Single print run, registration marks shared.
- **Laminator Feed:** 7.88" feeds across the 13.5" laminator with 5.62" clearance. Both labels share a single orientation group at lamination time — **1 lamination pass.** Most efficient 2-label configuration on current equipment, same single-pass geometry pattern as 1278930.

**Material Cost Breakdown (per kit, full bleed standard per PRICING_RULES.md §25):**

| Component | Calculation | Cost |
|-----------|-------------|------|
| Orajet 3951 cast vinyl | 1.218 sq ft × $1.21/sq ft | $1.474 |
| 1-mil polyester overlaminate | 1.218 sq ft × $0.2389/sq ft | $0.291 |
| Eco-solvent ink (full bleed) | 1.218 sq ft × $0.50/sq ft | $0.609 |
| Calculated total | | $2.374 |
| Incidental buffer (conservative round-up) | | + $0.026 |
| **Total material cost per kit** | | **$2.40** |

- **Per-label material cost:** ~$1.20 (kit-level $2.40 ÷ 2 labels — per the kit canonical convention in `PRODUCTION.md` Material Cost Quick Reference).

---
sections.production:
1. File import/setup — open the Elliott-supplied production-ready DWG (LABEL-A + LABEL-B on a tiled layout with registration marks already defined), send to RIP. ~5 min mechanical. NOT billable labor (see `PRICING_RULES.md` §22).
2. Print — Roland SG3-300, full-color eco-solvent on Orajet 3951. **Single run.** Layout uses a 90° rotation head-to-toe arrangement so both labels fit on the 28" print bed at approximately 25" wide overall footprint (3" clearance).
3. Laminate — 1-mil polyester overlaminate. **ONE pass.** Both labels feed with 7.88" across the 13.5" laminator (5.62" clearance), single orientation group.
4. Contour cut — **ONE run** following registration marks. R.25 corners.
5. Weed — remove excess around both labels.
6. Inspect — print quality, laminate adhesion, cut accuracy, label-to-label color fidelity.
7. Package — collate LABEL-A + LABEL-B as matched set.

- **Lamination Passes:** 1
- **Cut Runs:** 1
- **Constraints:** None — single-pass efficient. The 90° rotation head-to-toe print layout is what permits a single shared orientation group through the laminator. Same single-pass efficiency as 1278930 (3-label, 1 pass). 1245130 (5-label, 2 passes) is the comparable kit at the next step up in production complexity.

---
sections.pricing_derivation:
## Method Summary

**Per-label parity with P/N 1278930 (FA Accepted) — validated via the full 4-wave atomic AI process.** Same dimensions, same material, same process; the kit requires only 1 lamination pass — well within the ≤2 lam pass parity boundary. Per-label parity applies per `categories/printed-laminated-orajet.md` and `governance/PRICING_RULES.md` Parity Rules §1–§2. The $10.00/label at qty 20 lands exactly on the kit band anchor of $16.42/sq ft.

## Benchmark Comparison

| Item | Labels | Dimensions/Label | Sq Ft/Kit | Lam Passes | $/Kit qty 20 | $/Label qty 20 | $/Sq Ft (kit) | Status |
|------|--------|------------------|-----------|------------|--------------|----------------|----------------|--------|
| 1278930 | 3 | 11.13" × 7.88" | 1.827 | 1 | $30 | $10.00 | $16.42 | FA Accepted |
| **1278890 (this item)** | **2** | **7.88" × 11.13"** | **1.218** | **1** | **$20** | **$10.00** | **$16.42** | **Quoted** |
| 1245130 | 5 | 11.13" × 7.88" | 3.045 | 2 | $50 | $10.00 | $16.42 | Quoted |

Per-label parity holds at $10.00/label across all three kit-family members at qty 20. Per-sq-ft kit anchor of $16.42 holds exactly across all three. The 2-label kit slots between the 3-label and 5-label members at scale; tier table is the 2/3 scaling of 1278930's table (rounded to clean dollar amounts at each tier).

## Profile Validation (PRICING_RULES §15)

- **Category:** Printed + Laminated (Orajet 3951 Cast + Polyester Lam)
- **Profile band (kits, same-dimension, ≤2 lam passes):** $10.00/label at qty 20; $16.42/sq ft per-label at qty 20
- **This item:** $10.00/label at qty 20 = $16.42/sq ft — **lands exactly on the band anchor at every metric**

## §26 Invoice Protection Check

Tier-boundary cliff verification at every boundary (buyer is charged the lesser of adjacent tier totals at billing time per `PRICING_RULES.md` §26):

| Boundary | Lower-tier max total | Upper-tier min total | Cliff? | Charged at billing |
|----------|----------------------|----------------------|--------|--------------------|
| 9 → 10 | 9 × $30 = $270 | 10 × $24 = $240 | Yes ($30 inversion) | $240 |
| 19 → 20 | 19 × $24 = $456 | 20 × $20 = $400 | Yes ($56 inversion) | $400 |
| 49 → 50 | 49 × $20 = $980 | 50 × $17 = $850 | Yes ($130 inversion) | $850 |
| 99 → 100 | 99 × $17 = $1683 | 100 × $14 = $1400 | Yes ($283 inversion) | $1400 |
| 199 → 200 | 199 × $14 = $2786 | 200 × $12 = $2400 | Yes ($386 inversion) | $2400 |

§26 covers every boundary cleanly — buyer is always charged the lesser of adjacent totals at billing time. Same pattern as 1278930 and 1245130.

## 4-Wave AI Validation Record

24 independent runs (6 top-tier models × 4 waves). Calculator brief was built first; the proposed price was withheld from Wave 1.

### Wave 1 — Build Round (6 models, no anchor exposure)

**Purpose:** Each model independently builds a tier table from material specs, geometry, kit-band context, and benchmark items (1278930 FA Accepted, 1245130 Quoted) — proposed price NOT shown.

**Result:** **6/6 models independently built the identical tier table** ($30 / $24 / $20 / $17 / $14 / $12). Central finding across all 6 models: $10.00/label at qty 20 lands exactly on the $16.42/sq ft kit band anchor; invoice protection holds at every tier boundary with no inversions; the kit slots naturally between the 3-label (1278930) and 5-label (1245130) members at every tier.

### Wave 2 — Destruction Round (6 models, fresh chats)

**Purpose:** Attack the proposed tier table from Buyer/Procurement, Competitor, Cost Auditor, and Strategic angles.

**Severity:** 6/6 High on all four attack vectors.

**Primary findings:**
1. **200+ tier at $12/kit operates at near-breakeven fully-loaded.** At $85/hr shop rate and realistic 7.5–8.5 min/kit cycle times, fully-loaded margin compresses sharply. **Accepted as an inherited structural condition** shared with 1278930 ($18 at 200+) and 1245130 ($30 at 200+) — not a new problem introduced by this quote. Breaking the per-label parity here would corrupt the three-way triangulation that is Pro Label's most valuable strategic asset entering the January 2027 normalization.
2. **$17 clean-dollar at 50-99 confirmed correct** over the $17.34 strict 2/3 parity number. Prevents the buyer from doing cross-reference math on the kit family.
3. **Strategic cage confirmed.** Three data points at $10.00/label permanently establishes the per-label rate for this material and size on this account. The January 2027 normalization argument must be anchored to material cost increases or process complexity changes — NOT per-label rate arguments.

**Verdict:** 5/6 Yes with modifications (all modifications targeting the 200+ tier); 1/6 No (same set of modifications targeting 200+). Wave 3 buyer simulation resolved every proposed modification in favor of sending as shown.

### Wave 3 — Buyer Simulation (6 models, role-playing Sean Finn)

**Purpose:** Simulate Sean's response to the quote as written.

**Result:** **6/6 Sean simulations sent the PO as-is, without question.**

- **Instant approval number:** $20/kit at qty 20 — rubber stamp across all 6 simulations.
- **1-9 tier at $30:** Universally dismissed by all 6 — Sean relies on §26 invoice protection and will never pay $270 for 9 kits.
- **$17 vs $17.34 at 50-99:** Sean notices the $0.17/label delta but does NOT push back — clean dollar is correct.
- **200+ at $12:** Did not trigger Sean today — he interpreted the floor as the tier where Pro Label approaches Pro Sign's historical pricing range; read it as a reason to *consolidate volume toward Pro Label*, NOT as a renegotiation lever.
- **Mental-model log (all 6):** Sean permanently locks $10.00/label as the account standard for this material and size.
- **Pushback threshold:** Above $10.50/label at qty 20.
- **Quote email anchor line (confirmed by all 6):** *"Same material, same process, same per-label rate at qty 20 as the rest of the kit family."*

### Wave 4 — Final Synthesis (6 models, cold atomic sessions with full dossier)

**Purpose:** Final independent verdict on the locked tier table after Waves 1–3.

**Result:** **6/6 UNANIMOUS YES — send as shown. No tier modifications adopted.**

Every model independently concluded that the 200+ margin problem is **structural and inherited** from 1278930 and 1245130 — fixing it by breaking parity *here* would corrupt the three-way triangulation that is Pro Label's most valuable strategic asset entering the January 2027 normalization.

### Nick's Decision

Accepted Wave 4 unanimous consensus. No modifications. **No override type — engine consensus accepted.**

---

---
sections.notes:
- **Per-label parity is the foundation.** $10.00/label at qty 20 matches 1278930 (FA Accepted) and 1245130 (Quoted) exactly. Same material, same dimensions, ≤2 lam passes → per-label parity per account rule. Frame as *"separately finished safety labels supplied as a controlled matched set"* — never as kit-cost math or multiplier logic.
- **Single-pass efficiency.** Most efficient 2-label kit configuration on current equipment — same single-pass geometry pattern as 1278930. 90° rotation head-to-toe print layout at ~25" wide on the 28" Roland (3" clearance); single orientation group laminates at 7.88" across 13.5" (5.62" clearance).
- **LABEL-B revision (06/08/26, ECO# 28830).** "UPDATED: LOGO ON TOP OF CHARTS" — logo position moved to top of chart area on LABEL-B only. Artwork-only change. **No impact on dimensions, material, corners, lamination, cut, packaging, or pricing.**
- **Parity boundary.** Per-label parity applies at ≤2 lamination passes, same dimensions, same material. Beyond 2 passes or mixed dimensions → cost-build from scratch.
- **No MOQ.** Elliott is a strategic account — no minimum order quantity, no minimum order charge. §26 invoice protection applies at every tier boundary at billing time.
- **§25 full bleed standard applies.** Material cost includes the canonical full-coverage ink rate ($0.50/sq ft × 1.218 sq ft = $0.609) plus the incidental buffer to the canonical $2.40 frontmatter value.
- **File prep $0.** Elliott supplies production-ready DWG with both labels tiled and registration marks defined. ~5 min mechanical file-import operation; NOT a billable labor input. Account rule §22.
- **First article.** Not requested or offered. The first production run on this kit functions as a de facto internal QA check on the LABEL-B logo-position revision (ECO# 28830).
- **Defense language for the quote email (Wave 3 unanimous):** *"Same material, same process, same per-label rate at qty 20 as the rest of the kit family."*

## Internal Only — Do NOT Surface to Buyer

> The two notes below are internal-only strategic context. Never mentioned in any quote, email, invoice, or conversation with Sean or any Elliott contact.

sections.nesting:
### Laminator Pass Count

Both the 8.00" width and 1.75" height dimensions are well within the 13.5" polyester laminator. Either orientation yields a single lamination pass. No laminator complications.

- 1.75" across laminator: 11.75" clearance — single pass ✓
- 8.00" across laminator: 5.5" clearance — single pass ✓

### Print Layout and Nesting

Label: 8.00" × 1.75". Roland SG3-300 max print width: 28".

**Orientation A** (8.00" across the 28" bed, 1.75" in feed direction):
- Labels per row: 3 (3 × 8.00" = 24" ≤ 28")
- At qty 20: ~7 rows × 3 labels = 21 labels in one print run ✓

**Orientation B** (1.75" across the 28" bed, 8.00" in feed direction):
- Labels per row: 16 (16 × 1.75" = 28" exactly)
- At qty 20: 2 rows × 16 = 32 → fits 20 in one row + start a second ✓

Nesting is highly efficient at recurring volume. Multiple labels per row, single print run at any expected order quantity.

### Material Cost Breakdown per Label

> Material cost version date: 2026-06-05. Unit costs from `governance/PRODUCTION.md` (Orajet 3951 verified 2026-04-22; 1-mil polyester lam verified 2026-05-28; full bleed ink standard verified 2026-06-01).
>
> **Account-wide full bleed ink rule (PRICING_RULES.md §25):** All printed/laminated items on this account are priced assuming full bleed / full coverage ink at all times. Rate: $0.50/sq ft × full label sq ft.

| Component | Calculation | Per-Label Cost |
|-----------|-------------|---------------|
| Orajet 3951 cast vinyl | 0.0972 sq ft × $1.21/sq ft | $0.1176 |
| 1-mil polyester overlaminate | 0.0972 sq ft × $0.2389/sq ft | $0.0232 |
| Eco-solvent ink (full bleed standard) | 0.0972 sq ft × $0.50/sq ft | $0.0486 |
| **Calculated total** | | **$0.1894** |
| Incidental buffer (conservative round-up) ¹ | judgment-applied | + $0.0106 |
| **Canonical `material_cost_per_unit`** | | **$0.20** |

¹ **Incidental buffer convention (account-wide rule per PRICING_RULES.md §25):** The calculated material total is rounded conservatively upward to account for setup scrap, registration pulls, and minor production waste on every Elliott printed/laminated item. The buffer is judgment-applied, not a fixed number. For this item the calculated $0.1894 rounds to $0.20 in the frontmatter `material_cost_per_unit` field.

sections.pricing_derivation:
> **VALIDATED 2026-06-05 — 4-WAVE ATOMIC AI VALIDATION COMPLETE + FINAL 6-MODEL COMPREHENSIVE REVIEW.** 6 models × 4 waves = 24 independent responses, plus a separate post-Wave-4 comprehensive review by all 6 models. All 6 confirmed send as shown. Nick locked the price.
>
> This item is the **FOUNDING DATA POINT** for the sub-0.1 sq ft printed/laminated band. Same structural role as 3010707 for cut vinyl Band C and 3010704 for cut vinyl Band B. The band is INDEPENDENT — separate from the singles band ($15.43–$15.91/sq ft, calibrated at 0.5–1.3 sq ft) and the sub-scope handling (0.1–0.5 sq ft).

---

## Step 1 — Process Summary

**Validation method:** 4-wave atomic AI validation per `governance/VALIDATION_PROMPTS.md`. 6 top-tier models per wave, 24 total independent responses. Atomic session model: each prompt self-contained; no model has visibility into other waves or other models' responses. Claude Chat synthesized after each wave and generated the next prompt. After Wave 4, a separate final comprehensive 6-model review was run (all 6 confirmed send as shown). Nick is sole decision-maker; the 24 + 6 responses are evidence, not votes.

**Item:** 1279000 LBL-MAX PLTF CAP 1200 TIP HZRD. 8.00" × 1.75" = 0.0972 sq ft. Single label. Orajet 3951 + 1-mil polyester lam. ANSI Z535.4 WARNING (orange header + alert symbol, "TIP-OVER HAZARD!"). 1 production pass.

**Dimensional scope:** 0.0972 sq ft is BELOW the singles band scope floor (~0.5 sq ft) AND BELOW the sub-scope range (0.1–0.5 sq ft). This is a fundamentally new size class on the account — sub-0.1 sq ft production-volume printed/laminated. The 4 outrigger one-offs (1277970–1278000) and the 2 standalone tiny one-offs (3017583, 3017584) sit in this size class but are ONE-OFFS priced from job economics — they do NOT establish a production band. 1279000 is the first production-volume item below 0.1 sq ft on the account, and therefore establishes the band.

---

## Step 2 — Wave 1: Build Round

**6 models, atomic prompts. All 6 responses returned.**

Three clusters emerged across the 6 independent build responses at qty 20:

- **Low cluster ($3.00–$3.50):** Proportional sq ft scaling from singles band — 0.097 × $15.43 = $1.50 floor, anchored upward to account for fixed-labor dominance at sub-0.1 sq ft. 2 of 6 responses.
- **Mid cluster ($4.50):** Power-law scaling acknowledging fixed-labor amortization but holding the curve below the high cluster. 1 of 6 responses.
- **High cluster ($7.50–$9.00):** Absolute labor recovery — assumed per-label fixed labor at $5–$7 plus material plus margin. 3 of 6 responses.

All 6 confirmed material cost build ($0.20) and full bleed ink standard.

**Wave 1 carry-forward:** Three distinct cluster centers — engine has a spread of nearly 3× from low to high.

---

## Step 3 — Wave 2: Destruction Round

**All 6 responses returned.**

| Attack Vector | All 6 Models |
|---------------|--------------|
| Buyer attack severity (High cluster) | **H** (6/6) — Sean pattern-matches against 1230820 ($20/qty 20, 13.3× sq ft, ~2-3× price) and reads gouging |
| Competitor attack severity (High cluster) | **H** (6/6) — Pro Sign estimated $2.50–$3.25 at qty 20 for similar ANSI safety labels |
| Cost Auditor attack severity | Mixed — high cluster has unsupported labor assumptions on a 25-min batch-of-20 job |
| Strategic attack severity (High cluster) | **H** (6/6) — High cluster creates unsustainable precedent for future sub-0.1 production items |

**Primary finding (universal):** The High cluster ($7.50–$9.00) was UNANIMOUSLY KILLED. Sean would push back hard, and the absolute labor recovery assumption breaks down on a batch-of-20 job that takes ~25 minutes total.

**Secondary finding (universal):** The Low cluster ($3.00–$3.50) was VALIDATED as competitive with Pro Sign and consistent with the principle that material is negligible relative to labor on sub-0.1 sq ft labels. The Mid cluster ($4.50) was held as a viable upper-end alternative for the 1-9 tier.

**Convergence:** $3.00–$3.50 at qty 20, with $4.50 surviving as the candidate 1-9 tier.

**Verdict:** 0 of 6 — Send the High cluster. 6 of 6 — Send something in the Low cluster, with a possible Mid-cluster 1-9 to recover small-batch fixed costs.

---

## Step 4 — Wave 3: Buyer Simulation

**All 6 responses returned. All 6 models simulated Sean Finn (Elliott Equipment Employee-Owner, ~$140K/year program, $/sq ft mental model, deepening partnership phase).**

Two options surfaced from Wave 2 were presented side by side:
- **Option A:** $3.00 at qty 20 (Low cluster anchor — competitive with Pro Sign, 2.0× benchmark $/sq ft)
- **Option B:** $4.50 at qty 20 (Mid cluster anchor — premium 3.0× benchmark $/sq ft)

**Results:**
- **5 of 6 approved Option A ($3.00 at qty 20) immediately.** No pushback. One simulated Sean noted: "small label fixed cost premium is reasonable at 2× benchmark."
- **0 of 6 approved Option B ($4.50 at qty 20) as the recurring rate.** All 6 simulated Sean models flagged 3.0× benchmark as the trigger for a renegotiation conversation.
- **Pushback threshold (20-49 tier):** ~$3.25–$3.75. Range across models: $3.50 median.
- **Instant approval (20-49 tier):** $3.00 (unanimous).
- **All 6 confirmed:** They would log $30.86/sq ft as the "small safety label rate" — a permanent account data point.

**Wave 3 carry-forward:** $3.00 at qty 20 is the recurring anchor. $4.50 may work at the 1-9 tier (small-batch fixed cost recovery, Sean rarely orders 1-9).

---

## Step 5 — Wave 4: Final Synthesis

**All 6 responses returned. Verdict: SEND AS SHOWN on the proposed table at $4.50 / $3.50 / $3.00 / $2.60 / $2.30 / $2.10.**

- **5 of 6: YES, send as shown.** Full tier table approved. Margin healthy. Pricing defensible. Founding data point logic sound.
- **1 of 6: YES with a tier nudge** — wanted 20-49 at $3.25 instead of $3.00 (small-format premium argument). Not adopted.

**Tier compression check:** 53.3% (1-9 → 200+). All 6 confirmed this is correctly flatter than the singles band — material is negligible ($0.20 / 6.7% of price at qty 20), fixed labor dominates, labor doesn't compress as steeply at volume on tiny labels.

**Long-term precedent:** All 6 confirmed the table is sustainable. Future sub-0.1 sq ft printed/laminated items validate against this band.

**Wave 4 verdict — YES SEND.**

---

## Step 6 — Final Comprehensive 6-Model Review (Post-Wave 4)

After Wave 4, a separate final 6-model comprehensive review was run on the locked tier table. All 6 models received the full Wave 1–4 record plus the locked tier table and were asked: **send as shown?**

**All 6 confirmed: SEND AS SHOWN.**

- Margin healthy at every tier (90.5%–95.6%).
- Tier compression appropriate for fixed-labor-dominant sub-0.1 sq ft labels.
- Competitive with Pro Sign incumbent estimate ($2.50–$3.25 at qty 20).
- Pricing defensible.
- 1279000 establishes the sub-0.1 sq ft micro-format band as INDEPENDENT from the singles band — no contamination, no interaction.

---

## Step 7 — Nick's Final Decision

Nick reviewed the 24-wave evidence + 6-model comprehensive review and locked the full tier table as proposed:

| Tier | Final Price | Final $/Sq Ft | × Benchmark ($15.43/sq ft) |
|------|-------------|---------------|------------------------------|
| 1-9 | $4.50 | $46.30 | 3.00× |
| 10-19 | $3.50 | $36.01 | 2.33× |
| 20-49 | $3.00 | $30.86 | 2.00× |
| 50-99 | $2.60 | $26.75 | 1.73× |
| 100-199 | $2.30 | $23.66 | 1.53× |
| 200+ | $2.10 | $21.60 | 1.40× |

**No override type.** Engine consensus + final comprehensive review both unanimous.

**Band anchor: $30.86/sq ft at qty 20.** 2.0× benchmark. INDEPENDENT band — separate from singles ($15.43–$15.91/sq ft at 0.5–1.3 sq ft) and sub-scope (0.1–0.5 sq ft band-consistent handling).

**Band tier ratios (derived from this validated table):**
- 1-9: 1.50× anchor
- 10-19: 1.167× anchor
- 20-49: 1.00× anchor
- 50-99: 0.867× anchor
- 100-199: 0.767× anchor
- 200+: 0.70× anchor

These ratios become the band's tier-generation template for future sub-0.1 sq ft printed/laminated items.

---

## Step 8 — Band Positioning Summary

| Item | Sq Ft | Price (qty 20) | $/Sq Ft | Position |
|------|-------|----------------|---------|----------|
| 1230820 (Load Chart, D105) | 1.296 | $20.00 | $15.43 | Singles band root (root benchmark) |
| 1082570 (Load Chart, I70) | 0.503 | $8.00 | $15.91 | Singles band ceiling |
| 1210810 (DANGER Falling Jib) | 0.292 | $4.75 | $16.27 | Sub-scope (0.1–0.5), +5.4% above singles band — small-format premium (intentional) |
| **1279000 (Max Platform Cap WARNING)** | **0.0972** | **$3.00** | **$30.86** | **Micro-format band founding data point — 2.0× benchmark, INDEPENDENT band** |

**Three INDEPENDENT printed/laminated size classes now defined:**
- **Singles band (≥0.5 sq ft):** $15.43–$15.91/sq ft at qty 20. Calibrated 0.5–1.3 sq ft.
- **Sub-scope (0.1–0.5 sq ft):** band-consistent $/sq ft with small-format premium (1210810). Excluded from band data points until production-volume acceptance.
- **Micro-format band (< 0.1 sq ft):** $30.86/sq ft at qty 20. Founded by 1279000. 100% $/sq ft step-up from the singles band. Fixed-labor dominance justification.

The three size classes do NOT contaminate or interact. Future sub-0.1 sq ft production items validate against THIS band, not the singles band or the sub-scope handling.

The tiny route ($55 flat) remains the default for one-off / field-service requests at ≤ 0.1 sq ft — the micro-format band applies only to production catalog items.

---

## Step 9 — Per-Label Floor Caution

The $/sq ft anchor ($30.86) should **NOT be linearly extrapolated below ~0.06 sq ft.** At extreme smallness, a per-label floor (~$2.50–$3.00 at qty 20) overrides $/sq ft scaling because fixed labor per label has a minimum regardless of how small the label gets.

**Operational rule:** Items at the boundary (~0.08–0.12 sq ft) still require 4-wave AI validation. Items below ~0.06 sq ft default to a per-label floor that requires fresh validation against job economics, not a linear $/sq ft formula.

---

> ## FOUNDING DATA POINT — SUB-0.1 SQ FT MICRO-FORMAT BAND
>
> This item establishes the **sub-0.1 sq ft printed/laminated micro-format band** at **$30.86/sq ft at qty 20** (2.0× the singles band benchmark $15.43/sq ft). Same structural role as 3010707 for cut vinyl Band C and 3010704 for cut vinyl Band B.
>
> **The band is INDEPENDENT.** It does not contaminate or interact with:
> - The singles band ($15.43–$15.91/sq ft, calibrated at 0.5–1.3 sq ft)
> - The sub-scope handling (0.1–0.5 sq ft band-consistent $/sq ft with small-format premium, as documented for 1210810)
> - The tiny route ($55 flat one-off job-economics floor for ≤0.1 sq ft one-off requests)
>
> Future sub-0.1 sq ft printed/laminated **production-volume** items validate against THIS band ($30.86/sq ft anchor). One-off / field-service requests at ≤0.1 sq ft continue to route to the tiny one-off floor.

---

> ## PER-LABEL FLOOR — DO NOT LINEARLY EXTRAPOLATE BELOW ~0.06 SQ FT
>
> The band $/sq ft anchor ($30.86) is **NOT** a linear scaler for arbitrarily small labels. Below ~0.06 sq ft, a per-label floor (~$2.50–$3.00 at qty 20) overrides $/sq ft because fixed labor per label has a minimum regardless of label size.
>
> Items at the boundary (~0.08–0.12 sq ft) still require **4-wave AI validation** — do not parity-quote from this item without running the validation process. Items below ~0.06 sq ft require **fresh job-economics validation** because the linear $/sq ft formula breaks down.

---

> ## PRO SIGN COMPETITIVE CONTEXT
>
> Pro Sign (incumbent — actively being displaced) estimated **$2.50–$3.25 at qty 20** for similar ANSI safety labels at this size class. Our $3.00 at qty 20 sits in the middle of that range — competitive without being a giveaway. Pushback threshold (per Wave 3 simulation): $3.25–$3.75.

---

> ## DRAWING DATED 06-05-2026 — BRAND-NEW SPEC
>
> Drawing dated **today (06-05-2026)** — this is a brand-new spec. There is no prior production run. **Treat the first production run as a de facto internal first article** for ANSI Z535.4 color verification:
> - Orange WARNING header color fidelity
> - Alert symbol contrast
> - Red elements ANSI Z535.4 compliance
> - Black body text sharpness
>
> No external first article was requested or offered. The first production run carries the equivalent of an FA-level inspection burden as an internal QC step.

---

> ## SEAN WILL LOG $30.86/SQ FT AS THE "SMALL SAFETY LABEL RATE"
>
> All 6 Wave 3 buyer-simulation models confirmed they would log $30.86/sq ft as a **permanent account data point** ("small safety label rate") for benchmarking every future Pro Label sub-0.1 sq ft quote against. This is the expected and structurally correct outcome — the band is established by Sean's mental model the moment the first invoice goes out.

---

> ## NO MOQ / NO MINIMUM ORDER CHARGE / NO MINIMUM ORDER LANGUAGE
>
> Consistent with every other item on this account. No MOQ. No minimum order charge. No required quote language. §26 invoice protection applies automatically at all tier boundaries (buyer never invoiced more for a smaller qty than a larger qty at the next tier).

---

> ## INVOICE PROTECTION (§26) — TIER BOUNDARY VERIFICATION
>
> Boundary check on the locked tier table:
>
> - **9/10:** 9 × $4.50 = $40.50 vs 10 × $3.50 = $35.00 — cliff $5.50, resolved by §26 (buyer charged $35.00 for qty 9).
> - **19/20:** 19 × $3.50 = $66.50 vs 20 × $3.00 = $60.00 — cliff $6.50, resolved by §26 (buyer charged $60.00 for qty 19).
> - **49/50:** 49 × $3.00 = $147.00 vs 50 × $2.60 = $130.00 — cliff $17.00, resolved by §26.
> - **99/100:** 99 × $2.60 = $257.40 vs 100 × $2.30 = $230.00 — cliff $27.40, resolved by §26.
> - **199/200:** 199 × $2.30 = $457.70 vs 200 × $2.10 = $420.00 — cliff $37.70, resolved by §26.
>
> All cliffs are resolved automatically at billing time by §26 invoice protection — no quote-email language required. This is the same pattern that exists on every other multi-tier item on this account (1230820, 1210810, 1205720, 3010707, etc.).

---

> ## ARTWORK PREP — $0
>
> Elliott Equipment Company provides production-ready DWG files for every item on this account, without exception. File prep, artwork preparation, design time, and pre-press labor are zero-cost, zero-time inputs on every Elliott job. Step 1 (file import/setup) is a ~5-minute mechanical operation. This is a permanent account-level rule. See `PRICING_RULES.md` §22-24.

---

> ## AI VALIDATION — COMPLETE (4-WAVE ATOMIC PROCESS + FINAL 6-MODEL COMPREHENSIVE REVIEW)
>
> 4-wave, 6-model atomic AI validation per `governance/VALIDATION_PROMPTS.md` is complete (24 total independent responses), PLUS a final 6-model comprehensive review post-Wave 4. Full documentation in the Pricing Derivation section.
>
> **Key outcomes:**
> - Wave 1 (Build): Three clusters — Low ($3.00–$3.50), Mid ($4.50), High ($7.50–$9.00). All 6 confirmed material build and full bleed standard.
> - Wave 2 (Destruction): High cluster UNANIMOUSLY KILLED on buyer/competitor/strategic attacks. Low cluster validated. Mid retained as 1-9 candidate.
> - Wave 3 (Buyer Simulation): 5/6 approved $3.00 at qty 20 immediately; 0/6 approved $4.50 as the recurring rate. Instant approval $3.00 unanimous. Pushback threshold $3.25–$3.75. All 6 logged $30.86/sq ft as permanent account data point.
> - Wave 4 (Final Synthesis): 5/6 YES send as shown ($4.50 / $3.50 / $3.00 / $2.60 / $2.30 / $2.10); 1/6 wanted 20-49 nudged to $3.25 (not adopted).
> - **Final 6-model comprehensive review:** 6/6 confirmed SEND AS SHOWN.
> - Nick locked the full tier table as proposed. No override.

sections.item_overview:
- **Part Number:** 3010701
- **Description:** LBL-ELLIOTT MED RD — medium-format "ELLIOTT" wordmark in Cardinal Red
- **Drawing:** 3010701.dwg
- **Revision:** Rev B
- **Drawing Date:** 02/26/07 (Engineer: AEB / DTR: DFL / Sheet 1 of 1, Size B)
- **Type:** Vinyl cut lettering (no printing)
- **Content:** "ELLIOTT" block letter wordmark at medium scale — middle member of the S/M/L brand wordmark family. Applied to crane body/boom for brand identification at intermediate scale between small-format model designations and the large-format LRG wordmark (3010704).
- **Dimensions:** 49.16" W × 9.38" H (**3.202 sq ft**)
- **Tolerances:** ±1/16" fractional (drawing Note 1)
- **Status:** Quoted 2026-06-09

---
sections.material_spec:
- **Substrate:** 3M Controltac 180mC-53 Cardinal Red
- **Drawing Callout (Note 3):** "2-MIL, 3M 180-53 CARDINAL RED, 5-7 YEAR VINYL, ADHESIVE BACK" + "VINYL CUT LETTERING, AND CARRIER FOR APPLICATION"
- **Film Type:** 2-mil cast vinyl
- **Adhesive:** Controltac repositionable pressure-sensitive
- **Rated Durability:** 10-year (exceeds drawing spec of 5-7 year)
- **Roll Size:** 24" × 50yd = $775.10/roll ($15.502/yd, $7.751/sq ft) — verified 2026-05-28 (see `materials/3m-180mc-cardinal-red.md`)
- **Application Tape:** TransferRite Ultra 582U, 24" × 100yd = $118.21/roll ($1.1821/yd, $0.5911/sq ft) — verified 2026-05-28 (see `materials/transferrite-582u.md`)
- **Laminate:** N/A — cut vinyl, no laminate

> **Note on roll selection:** This item uses the standard 24" × 50yd Cardinal Red roll (same roll used by the small-format Band A cluster items 1205720 and 1186310). The 9.38" label height rotated across the 24" roll enables 2-across nesting (2 × 9.38" = 18.76", 5.24" clearance). The 24" TransferRite 582U tape matches the vinyl roll width exactly — no overhang, no waste strip. The 15" Cardinal Red roll used by Band B (3010704) is wrong for this item — at 9.38" height with 2-across nesting, 15" stock would force 1-across only, doubling vinyl cost per label.

---
sections.nesting:
## 90-Degree Rotation Geometry

The label is **49.16" wide × 9.38" tall** when installed. During production the label is **rotated 90 degrees on the roll** so that:

- **9.38" (label height) feeds ACROSS the 24" roll width** — enables 2-across nesting
- **49.16" (label width) feeds ALONG the roll length** — becomes the cut length per row

This is the same length-based, length-feeds-along-roll convention used by every other length-based cut vinyl item on this account (1205720, 3010704, 3010707).

## Roll Specs

| Component | Roll | Cost | Cost/Yard | Cost/Sq Ft |
|-----------|------|------|-----------|------------|
| 3M Controltac 180mC-53 Cardinal Red | 24" × 50yd | $775.10 | $15.502 | $7.751 |
| TransferRite Ultra 582U | 24" × 100yd | $118.21 | $1.1821 | $0.5911 |

## Vinyl Nesting on 24" Roll

| Dimension | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Label height (across roll, rotated) | 9.38" |
| Labels across 24" roll | **2** (2 × 9.38" = 18.76", **5.24" clearance**) |
| Label length (along roll, rotated) | 49.16" |
| Spacing allocation per row | 3" lead-in + 3" trail-out = 6" |
| Feed length per row of 2 | 49.16" + 6" = **55.16" = 1.532 yd** |
| Vinyl cost per row of 2 | 1.532 yd × $15.502/yd = $23.749 |
| Vinyl cost per label | $23.749 ÷ 2 = **$11.87** |

## Application Tape Nesting on 24" Roll

| Dimension | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Tape width | 24" (matches vinyl roll width exactly) |
| Feed length per row of 2 | 1.532 yd (same as vinyl) |
| Tape cost per row of 2 | 1.532 yd × $1.1821/yd = $1.811 |
| Tape cost per label | $1.811 ÷ 2 = **$0.91** |

## Total Material Cost per Label

| Component | Cost |
|-----------|------|
| Vinyl (3M 180mC-53 Cardinal Red, 24" × 50yd) | $11.87 |
| Application tape (TransferRite 582U, 24" × 100yd) | $0.91 |
| **Total material cost per label** | **$12.78** |

**Material cost per sq ft:** $12.78 ÷ 3.202 = **$3.99/sq ft**.

## Labels per Roll

- Rows per 50yd roll (1800" usable): floor(1800 / 55.16) = **32 complete rows**
- Labels per roll: 32 × 2 = **64 labels per roll**

---
sections.production:
## Process Steps

1. **File import/setup** — open the Elliott-supplied production-ready DWG, send to cutter. ~5 min mechanical. NOT billable labor (see `PRICING_RULES.md` §22).
2. **Cut** — Roland SG3-300 on 24" Cardinal Red stock. 2 labels across (2 × 9.38" = 18.76" across 24" roll, 5.24" clearance). Single continuous cut path of the "ELLIOTT" wordmark, repeated 2-up per row.
3. **Weed** — remove excess vinyl around the 7 block characters (E ×3, L ×2, I ×1, O ×1, T ×2 — wait, "ELLIOTT" = E-L-L-I-O-T-T, so E ×1, L ×2, I ×1, O ×1, T ×2). Open counters on E and O make weeding efficient per character; the 49.16" weed span across the row of 2 labels is the dominant time factor.
4. **Mask** — apply TransferRite 582U transfer tape over weeded characters. 24" tape matches 24" vinyl exactly.
5. **Inspect** — verify cut accuracy, no lifted edges, character legibility across the 49.16" width.
6. **Package** — flat, masked with transfer tape carrier, ready for hand-application on crane body/boom.

## Production Notes

- **Lamination passes:** 0 (cut vinyl, no print)
- **Cut runs:** 1
- **Equipment:** Roland SG3-300 (24" Cardinal Red stock; 28" max cut width — well within tolerance)
- **Weeding complexity:** Low–medium. 7 block characters at medium scale, open counters on E ×1 and O ×1. Geometrically similar to the LRG sibling (3010704), but at smaller scale; comparable to the small-format model designations (1205720 "E190", 1186310 "E160") in character count and shape complexity. The 49.16" weed/mask span is wider than the small-format cluster (~33.5" wide) but narrower than the LRG (~70.8" wide) — fractional width-handling premium of ~0.5% above proportional captures this.
- **Cut feed length:** 55.16" per row of 2 labels. On the 50yd roll (1800" usable), 32 complete rows × 2 labels = 64 labels per roll.

---
sections.pricing_derivation:
**Methodology:** Sq ft scaling within the Band A (Small-Format, 1.0–5.0 sq ft) Cut Vinyl Profile, anchored to the 2.51–2.56 sq ft small-format cluster ($35/qty 20, $13.67/sq ft) with a 0.5% fractional width-handling premium for the 49.16" weed/mask span. Validated through the full 4-wave AI validation process per `governance/VALIDATION_PROMPTS.md` (24 independent responses across 6 models × 4 waves) plus a revised Wave 2 with corrected geometry context. Wave 4 was UNANIMOUS YES (6/6) on the tier table as shown.

**Sq ft / $/sq ft comparison across the cut vinyl bands:**

| Band | Representative Item | Sq Ft | Price (qty 20) | $/sq ft | Position |
|------|---------------------|-------|----------------|---------|----------|
| Band B (Large-Format) | 3010704 (LRG sibling) | 7.069 | $78.00 | $11.03 | LRG anchor |
| Band A (Small-Format) | **3010701 (this item — MED, founding 3–5 sq ft data point)** | **3.202** | **$44.00** | **$13.74** | **First Band A item outside 2.51–2.56 sq ft cluster** |
| Band A (Small-Format) | 1205720 (small-format cluster anchor) | 2.56 | $35.00 | $13.67 | Cluster anchor |
| Band A (Small-Format) | 1186310 (small-format cluster, E160) | 2.564 | $35.00 | $13.65 | Cluster |
| Band A (Small-Format) | 3017435 (small-format cluster, ELLIOTT White) | 2.56 | $35.00 | $13.67 | Cluster |
| Band A (Small-Format) | 3018378 (small-format cluster, D115 Olympic Blue) | 2.512 | $35.00 | $13.94 | Cluster |

Monotonically increasing $/sq ft as sq ft drops within Band A; the MED item ($13.74/sq ft) sits inside the Band A range ($13.65–$13.94/sq ft).

**Proportional price scaling math:**

- Area ratio (MED / small-format anchor): 3.202 / 2.56 = **1.251**
- Price ratio (MED / small-format anchor): $44 / $35 = **1.257**
- Differential: 1.257 / 1.251 = **+0.5% premium above proportional** — fractional width-handling premium for the 49.16" weed/mask span. Proportional pricing with a small structural adjustment, not a band shift.

**Family position — ELLIOTT S/M/L wordmark family:**

| P/N | Family Role | Dimensions | Sq Ft | Price (qty 20) | $/sq ft |
|-----|-------------|------------|-------|----------------|---------|
| (not yet defined) | SML | TBD | TBD | TBD | TBD |
| **3010701 (this item)** | **MED** | 49.16" × 9.38" | 3.202 | $44.00 | $13.74 |
| 3010704 | LRG | 70.8125" × 14.375" | 7.069 | $78.00 | $11.03 |

3010701 is confirmed as the middle member of the ELLIOTT S/M/L brand wordmark family. SML is not yet defined; LRG is 3010704. The MED member sits inside Band A; LRG sits inside Band B. The $/sq ft curve is monotonic and structurally coherent.

**LRG inversion floor check (200+ tier):**

The 200+ tier ($39 at 3.202 sq ft = $12.18/sq ft) must stay above the LRG inversion floor — i.e., it must not drop below LRG $/sq ft scaled by the MED area:

- LRG inversion floor: 3.202 × $11.03 = **$35.32 per label**
- MED 200+ tier: **$39.00 per label**
- Headroom: $39.00 − $35.32 = **$3.68 per label** above the floor (10.4% above)

The MED 200+ price is structurally above the LRG band — no $/sq ft inversion at any quantity. The locked tier table preserves the band hierarchy at every tier.

## 4-Wave Validation Record

**Wave 1 — Build Round (6 models, independent, atomic prompts):**
- Six models built price from scratch without anchor exposure.
- Cluster: $42.50–$44.00 (4 models). Central tendency: $43.25.
- Outliers rejected: M1 at $33.50 (inverts family curve below LRG $11.03/sq ft); M5 at $46.00 (exceeds small-format band ceiling).
- **Key Wave 1 finding:** price-to-area ratio ($44/$35 = 1.257) nearly exactly matches area ratio (3.202/2.56 = 1.251) — proportional pricing with a 0.5% fractional width premium.

**Wave 2 — Destruction Round (6 models, fresh chat, hostile attack vectors — run twice):**
- First run partially contaminated by nesting geometry misread by some models. Second run with explicit geometry clarification produced clean results.
- **Surviving Wave 2 findings:**
  1. $44.00 landing inside the small-format band is structurally superior to $43.25 landing below it — **the single most important finding**.
  2. 200+ tier requires a visible step-down, not flat compression.
  3. Fully-burdened margin at $44 is approximately 44–53% (below the 57% warn floor) — accepted as a strategic account concession.
  4. January 2027 normalization requires ~9% uplift from $44 anchor — manageable.
- **Wave 2 verdict:** Yes with modifications — raise to $44.00 anchor; add real 200+ compression; revise 200+ from $38 to $39 after the second run.

**Wave 3 — Buyer Simulation (6 models as Sean Finn):**
- **All 6 models approved $44.00 at qty 20** with immediate PO same day. Zero pushback, zero question emails.
- Sean's mental normalization: $44 ÷ 3.202 = $13.74/sq ft — lands inside the small-format band, passes pattern-match in under 30 seconds.
- Pushback threshold: $47–$49 ($3–$5 of headroom above the anchor).
- 200+ at $38/$39 universally accepted — the visible $5–$6 step-down satisfies the volume reward expectation.
- Sean logs **$13.74/sq ft as a permanent Band A anchor** in the mental model; expects future 4.0 sq ft items at ~$54–$56.

**Wave 4 — Final Synthesis (6 models, binary verdicts):**
- **6/6 UNANIMOUS YES** on $57 / $50 / $44 / $42 / $40 / $38 tier table.
- After the revised Wave 2, the 200+ tier was raised to $39 based on the fully-burdened margin analysis at high volume.
- No other modifications adopted.
- All open items resolved: geometry confirmed, material cost locked at $12.78, band coherence confirmed (Band A range $13.65–$13.94/sq ft holds at 3.202 sq ft), buyer simulation unanimous, §26 infrastructure handles all cliffs.

**Nick's Final Decision:**
- Accepted Wave 4 unanimous consensus tier table.
- Raised 200+ from $38 to $39 based on revised Wave 2 fully-burdened margin analysis at high volume.
- No other modifications.
- **No override type — engine consensus accepted.** No relationship concession applied, no competitive defense applied, no owner judgment override applied.

**AI Validation Summary:** 24 independent responses across 6 models × 4 waves (with a corrected Wave 2 second run for geometry clarity). Wave 4 was UNANIMOUS YES (6/6) on the tier table. No override.

**Profile Implication:** This item is the **first Band A data point outside the 2.51–2.56 sq ft small-format cluster**. It establishes the curve between the cluster and Band B (LRG at $11.03/sq ft), validating that Band A holds proportional pricing in the 3–5 sq ft range. Future Band A items at 1.0 sq ft ≤ sq ft < 5.0 sq ft validate against the expanded Band A (now 5 data points). Items near the Band B boundary (~4.5–5.5 sq ft) still require 4-wave AI validation before pricing.

---
sections.margin_analysis:
| Qty Tier | Price | Material Cost | Gross Profit | Margin |
|----------|-------|---------------|-------------|--------|
| 1-9 | $57.00 | $12.78 | $44.22 | ~77.6% |
| 10-19 | $50.00 | $12.78 | $37.22 | ~74.4% |
| 20-49 | $44.00 | $12.78 | $31.22 | ~70.9% |
| 50-99 | $42.00 | $12.78 | $29.22 | ~69.6% |
| 100-199 | $40.00 | $12.78 | $27.22 | ~68.1% |
| 200+ | $39.00 | $12.78 | $26.22 | ~67.2% |

**Margin at qty 20: ~70.9%.** Slightly below the cut vinyl category margin band (~73–78% target) on a material-only basis. The structural softness at this tier was accepted as a strategic account concession during Wave 2 — fully-burdened margin (~44–53%) at $44 is below the 57% warn floor and was visible to the engine; the concession was deliberately taken to land $44 inside the Band A $/sq ft range and trigger Sean's instant-approval pattern-match.

**Margin floor at 200+: ~67.2%.** Above the ~57% category warn threshold and well above the 50% structural stop. Acceptable for a Band A item with a deliberate concession at the anchor.

**Tier compression (1-9 → 200+):** 31.6% discount ($57 → $39). **Tighter** than Band A's cluster compression (~51%) — the 200+ tier is intentionally less aggressive because the LRG inversion floor ($35.32) constrains how low 200+ can go, and the fully-burdened margin analysis at high volume calls for a visible-but-modest step-down rather than a deep cut. The compression also aligns with the family position — a too-deep 200+ discount on MED would invert against the LRG band at high quantities.

**Comparison to Band A cluster (material-only margins at qty 20):**

| Item | Sq Ft | Price (qty 20) | $/sq ft | Material Cost | Margin (qty 20) |
|------|-------|----------------|---------|---------------|-----------------|
| 1205720 (Cardinal Red, 24" roll) | 2.56 | $35 | $13.67 | $8.74 | ~75% |
| 1186310 (Cardinal Red, 24" roll) | 2.564 | $35 | $13.65 | $8.74 | ~75% |
| 3018378 (Olympic Blue, 24" roll) | 2.512 | $35 | $13.94 | $8.92 | ~75% |
| 3017435 (White, 24" roll) | 2.56 | $35 | $13.67 | $9.51 | ~73% |
| **3010701 (Cardinal Red, 24" roll — MED)** | **3.202** | **$44** | **$13.74** | **$12.78** | **~70.9%** |

The MED item sits just below the Band A material-only margin band at qty 20 (~71% vs cluster ~73–75%). The 3–4 percentage-point softness reflects the higher feed-length-driven material cost on a 49.16" cut (vs ~33.5" on the cluster) producing a higher material share of revenue at the same $/sq ft. The pricing-engine consensus held the band $/sq ft rather than chase material-cost-driven margin parity — the right call per Wave 4 unanimous verdict.

sections.item_overview:
- **Part Number:** 3010704
- **Description:** LBL-ELLIOTT LRG RED — large-format "ELLIOTT" wordmark in Cardinal Red
- **Drawing:** 3010704.dwg
- **Revision:** B
- **Drawing Date:** 02/26/07 (Engineer: AEB / Drafter: DFL)
- **Type:** Vinyl cut lettering (no printing)
- **Content:** Block letter "ELLIOTT" wordmark — 7 characters, open counters (E ×3, O ×1). Generic brand wordmark used across all Elliott crane models — applied to crane body/boom for brand identification.
- **Dimensions:** 70-13/16" W × 14-3/8" H (70.8125" × 14.375" — **7.069 sq ft**)
- **Tolerances:** ±1/16" fractional (drawing note 1)
- **Status:** Quoted 2026-06-05

---
sections.material_spec:
- **Substrate:** 3M Controltac 180mC-53 Cardinal Red
- **Drawing Callout:** "2-MILL, '3M 180-53 CARDINAL RED', 5-7 YEAR-VINYL, ADHESIVE BACK" (Elliott's "180-53" is shorthand for 180mC-53, consistent with all other Cardinal Red items on this account)
- **Film Type:** 2-mil cast vinyl
- **Adhesive:** Controltac repositionable pressure-sensitive
- **Rated Durability:** 10-year (exceeds drawing spec of 5-7 year)
- **Roll Size:** **15" × 50yd** = $433.77/roll ($8.6754/yd, $2.3134/sq ft) — verified 2026-06-05 (see `materials/3m-180mc-cardinal-red-15in.md`)
- **Application Tape:** TransferRite Ultra 582U, **30" × 100yd** = $141.86/roll ($1.4186/yd, $0.1891/sq ft) — verified 2026-06-05 (see `materials/transferrite-582u-30in.md`)
- **Laminate:** N/A — cut vinyl, no laminate

> **Note on roll selection:** This item uses the 15" × 50yd Cardinal Red roll (not the 24" × 50yd roll used for the 2.5 sq ft cluster). The 14.375" label height fits within the 15" roll width with 0.625" clearance, giving 1-across nesting. The 24" roll would waste 9.625" per row — operationally and economically wrong for this item. The 30" application tape similarly enables 2-up nesting (2 × 14.375" = 28.75" across 30" tape), which is required to amortize the tape feed across two labels.

---
sections.nesting:
## Roll Specs

| Component | Roll | Cost | Cost/Yard | Cost/Sq Ft |
|-----------|------|------|-----------|------------|
| 3M Controltac 180mC-53 Cardinal Red | 15" × 50yd | $433.77 | $8.6754 | $2.3134 |
| TransferRite Ultra 582U | 30" × 100yd | $141.86 | $1.4186 | $0.1891 |

## Vinyl Nesting on 15" Roll

| Dimension | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Label height (across roll) | 14.375" |
| Labels across 15" roll | **1** (0.625" clearance) |
| Label length (down roll) | 70.8125" |
| Spacing allocation per label | 3" lead-in + 3" trail-out = 6" |
| Feed length per label | 70.8125" + 6" = **76.8125" = 2.1337 yd** |
| Vinyl cost per label (length-based) | 2.1337 yd × $8.6754/yd = **$18.51** |

## Application Tape Nesting on 30" Roll

| Dimension | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Label height (across tape) | 14.375" |
| Labels across 30" tape | **2** (2 × 14.375" = 28.75", 1.25" waste strip) |
| Feed length per row of 2 | 76.8125" = 2.1337 yd (same as vinyl) |
| Tape cost per row of 2 | 2.1337 yd × $1.4186/yd = $3.03 |
| Tape cost per label | $3.03 ÷ 2 = **$1.51** |

## Total Material Cost per Label

| Component | Cost |
|-----------|------|
| Vinyl (3M 180mC-53 Cardinal Red, 15" × 50yd) | $18.51 |
| Application tape (TransferRite 582U, 30" × 100yd) | $1.51 |
| **Total material cost per label** | **$20.02** |

**Material cost per sq ft:** $20.02 ÷ 7.069 = $2.83/sq ft (vs $3.41/sq ft on the 2.5 sq ft benchmark — larger area amortizes the tape cost and improves per-sq-ft material economics).

---
sections.production:
## Process Steps

1. **File import/setup** — open the Elliott-supplied production-ready DWG, send to cutter. ~5 min mechanical. NOT billable labor (see `PRICING_RULES.md` §22).
2. **Cut** — Roland SG3-300 on 15" Cardinal Red stock. 1 label across (14.375" height across 15" roll, 0.625" clearance). Single continuous cut path of the "ELLIOTT" wordmark.
3. **Weed** — remove excess vinyl around the 7 block characters. Large simple letters with open counters (E ×3, O ×1) — straightforward weeding, no fine interior detail.
4. **Mask** — apply TransferRite 582U transfer tape over weeded characters. 30" tape allows 2 labels side-by-side per masking pass (2 × 14.375" = 28.75" across 30" tape, 1.25" waste strip).
5. **Inspect** — verify cut accuracy, no lifted edges, character legibility across the 70.8125" length.
6. **Package** — flat or rolled (depending on logistics), masked with transfer tape carrier, ready for hand-application on crane body/boom.

## Production Notes

- **Lamination passes:** 0 (cut vinyl, no print)
- **Cut runs:** 1
- **Equipment:** Roland SG3-300 (15" Cardinal Red stock; 28" max cut width — well within tolerance for the 70.8125" feed length)
- **Weeding complexity:** Low–medium. 7 large block characters at large scale, open counters. Simpler geometry per sq ft than the "E190" / "E160" / "D115" model designations (which are similar-scale characters at smaller overall size). The increased physical scale slows weeding linearly with area, but the open-counter geometry keeps the weeding rate per inch comparable.
- **Cut feed length:** ~76.8" per label (label + 6" spacing). On the 50yd roll (1800" usable), ~23 labels per roll (1800 ÷ 76.8 = 23.4 → 23 complete labels with end waste).

---
sections.pricing_derivation:
**Methodology:** Sq ft scaling from the 2.51–2.56 sq ft cluster benchmark (P/N 1205720) with a 19.3% $/sq ft step-down to reflect the structural advantages of a large-format size class. Validated through the full 4-wave AI validation process per `governance/VALIDATION_PROMPTS.md` (24 independent responses across 6 models × 4 waves).

**Sq ft comparison:**

| Item | Dimensions | Sq Ft | Price (qty 20) | $/sq ft |
|------|-----------|-------|----------------|---------|
| P/N 1205720 (small-format benchmark) | 33-9/16" × 11" | 2.56 | $35 | $13.67 |
| P/N 3017435 (small-format parity) | 43.91" × 8.38" | 2.56 | $35 | $13.67 |
| P/N 3018378 (small-format parity) | 32.88" × 11.00" | 2.512 | $35 | $13.94 |
| P/N 1186310 (small-format parity) | 33-9/16" × 11" | 2.564 | $35 | $13.65 |
| **P/N 3010704 (large-format founding data point)** | **70.8125" × 14.375"** | **7.069** | **$78** | **$11.03** |

**Per sq ft at qty 20:** $78 ÷ 7.069 = **$11.03/sq ft**

**Step-down from small-format band:** $13.67/sq ft → $11.03/sq ft = **19.3% step-down**. Justified by three structural advantages of the large-format size class:
1. **Fixed labor amortization** — file import, machine setup, inspection, packaging are roughly constant per label regardless of physical size. The fixed-labor share of cost per sq ft drops as label area grows.
2. **Simpler weeding geometry per sq ft** — large block letters with open counters weed faster per sq ft than dense small-format text. The 7-character "ELLIOTT" wordmark with E ×3 and O ×1 open counters is geometrically simpler than a comparable area of small-format model designations.
3. **Lower material cost per sq ft** — $2.83/sq ft on this item vs $3.41/sq ft on the 2.5 sq ft benchmark. The 30" application tape amortizes 2-up against the 15" single-label vinyl feed, reducing tape cost per sq ft by ~$0.08/sq ft.

**Profile Validation:** Establishes the **Large-Format Cut Vinyl band (5+ sq ft)** in the Cut Vinyl Pricing Profile at **$11.03/sq ft at qty 20**. This is a separate band from the existing 2.51–2.56 sq ft concession-phase band ($13.65–$13.94/sq ft). The two bands are independent — they do not contaminate or interact with each other. The January 2027 normalization plan applies to the 2.51–2.56 sq ft cluster only, not to this large-format class.

## 4-Wave Validation Record (3010704)

> **NOTE:** Validation was conducted at 13.5" height (6.639 sq ft). Nick corrected the operative height to 14.375" (7.069 sq ft) after validation. Material cost is feed-length-driven, not area-driven — the vinyl cost ($18.51) depends on the 76.8125" feed length, which is unchanged; the tape cost ($1.51) depends on the same feed length and still nests 2-up on the 30" tape (2 × 14.375" = 28.75" ≤ 30"). All prices and margins are unchanged. $/sq ft figures were recalculated with the larger area (denominator change only).

**Wave 1 — Build (6 models, independent, atomic prompts):**
- Price range at qty 20: $63–$91. Central cluster: $75–$91. Median: ~$83.
- Fundamental debate: $/sq ft step-down at 2.6× the calibrated size.
- 2 models held band at $13.71/sq ft ($91). 4 models applied step-downs ($63–$85).
- All 6 confirmed $20.02 material cost. All 6 confirmed $0 file prep.

**Wave 2 — Destruction (6 models, hostile attack vectors):**
- Buyer/Procurement: 6/6 HIGH — flat $/sq ft across size classes is the structural error.
- Competitor: 6/6 HIGH — regional competitor at $55–$68 qty 20, $44–$50 qty 200+.
- Cost Auditor: 4/6 MEDIUM, 2/6 HIGH — 6" spacing padding ($0.40–$0.65), margin is material-only.
- Strategic: 6/6 HIGH — must establish separate large-format size class before first invoice.
- Verdicts: 6/6 rejected original table ($91 at qty 20).

**Wave 3 — Buyer Simulation (6 models as Sean Finn):**
- All 6 pushed back. None approved as-is at $85.
- Pushback threshold range: $75–$85. Central cluster: $80–$82.
- Instant approval range: $65–$78. Central cluster: $78 (4 of 6).
- Incumbent estimate: $55–$70. All 6 referenced Pro Sign.
- PO decision: All 6 held PO. All 6 would send question/counteroffer email.
- All 6 confirmed this becomes a permanent data point in the buyer's pricing model.

**Wave 4 — Final Synthesis (6 models, binary verdicts):**
- Verdict: **6/6 NO on $85 at qty 20.**
- All 6 recommended **$78 at qty 20. UNANIMOUS.**
- All 6 required separate large-format size class established before first invoice.

**Nick's Final Decision:**
- Accepted $78 at qty 20 (unanimous Wave 4 recommendation).
- Set T1 at $105, T2 at $92 (operational tiers — Sean orders across T1–T3).
- Set T4–T6 at $68/$60/$52 for structural completeness.
- No override type — pricing validated through full 4-wave process.

**AI Validation Summary:** 24 independent responses across 6 models × 4 waves. Unanimous on $78 at qty 20 in Wave 4. Unanimous on the requirement to establish a separate large-format size class before first invoice.

**Override:** None. Engine consensus accepted.

**Profile Implication:** This item establishes the founding data point for the Large-Format Cut Vinyl band (5+ sq ft) at $11.03/sq ft at qty 20. Future cut vinyl items at ≥ 5.0 sq ft validate against this band, NOT the 2.51–2.56 sq ft concession band.

---
sections.margin_analysis:
| Qty Tier | Price | Material Cost | Gross Profit | Margin |
|----------|-------|---------------|-------------|--------|
| 1-9 | $105.00 | $20.02 | $84.98 | ~80.9% |
| 10-19 | $92.00 | $20.02 | $71.98 | ~78.2% |
| 20-49 | $78.00 | $20.02 | $57.98 | ~74.3% |
| 50-99 | $68.00 | $20.02 | $47.98 | ~70.6% |
| 100-199 | $60.00 | $20.02 | $39.98 | ~66.6% |
| 200+ | $52.00 | $20.02 | $31.98 | ~61.5% |

**Margin at qty 20: ~74.3%.** Within the cut vinyl category margin band (~73–78% target). Material-only margin per Pro Label P&L convention.

**Margin floor at 200+: ~61.5%.** Above the ~57% category warn threshold and well above the 50% stop threshold. Acceptable for a low-complexity cut/weed/mask process at large-format scale.

**Tier compression (1-9 → 200+):** ~50.5% discount ($105 → $52). Consistent with the cut vinyl category tier compression of ~51%.

**Comparison to category:**

| Item | Sq Ft | Price (qty 20) | $/sq ft | Material Cost | Margin (qty 20) |
|------|-------|----------------|---------|---------------|-----------------|
| P/N 1205720 (Cardinal Red, 24" roll) | 2.56 | $35 | $13.67 | $8.74 | ~75% |
| P/N 1186310 (Cardinal Red, 24" roll) | 2.564 | $35 | $13.65 | $8.74 | ~75% |
| P/N 3018378 (Olympic Blue, 24" roll) | 2.512 | $35 | $13.94 | $8.92 | ~75% |
| P/N 3017435 (White, 24" roll) | 2.56 | $35 | $13.67 | $9.51 | ~73% |
| P/N 3017435 (White, 48" roll) | 2.56 | $35 | $13.67 | $7.79 | ~78% |
| **P/N 3010704 (Cardinal Red, 15" roll)** | **7.069** | **$78** | **$11.03** | **$20.02** | **~74.3%** |

3010704 sits squarely in the cut vinyl margin band at qty 20 (~74.3%, between the ~73% and ~78% endpoints of the existing cluster). The $/sq ft is structurally lower (large-format size class), but the absolute margin percentage is consistent with the rest of the category — the step-down is in $/sq ft, not in profitability.

---
sections.notes:
- **FOUNDING DATA POINT — Large-Format Cut Vinyl size class (5+ sq ft).** This is the first item on the Elliott account at the large-format scale. Establishes the Large-Format Cut Vinyl band in the Cut Vinyl Pricing Profile at **$11.03/sq ft at qty 20**.
- **Separate from the 2.51–2.56 sq ft cluster.** The large-format band is an INDEPENDENT band — it does not contaminate or interact with the existing 2.51–2.56 sq ft concession-phase band ($13.65–$13.94/sq ft). The two bands are independent. The January 2027 normalization plan applies to the 2.51–2.56 sq ft cluster only, not to this large-format class.
- **4-wave AI validation (24 passes).** Pricing validated through the full 4-wave atomic AI validation process per `governance/VALIDATION_PROMPTS.md`. Wave 4 was UNANIMOUS on $78 at qty 20 across all 6 models. No override type — engine consensus accepted.
- **Validation height vs operative height.** Validation was conducted using an initial height of 13.5" (6.639 sq ft). Nick subsequently corrected the operative height to 14.375" (7.069 sq ft). All prices and margins are unchanged because material cost is feed-length-driven, not area-driven. The vinyl cost ($18.51) depends on the 76.8125" feed length, which is unchanged. The tape cost ($1.51) depends on the same feed length and still nests 2-up on the 30" tape (2 × 14.375" = 28.75" ≤ 30"). The only figures that changed are sq ft (6.639 → 7.069), $/sq ft at each tier (recalculated with larger denominator), and the benchmark step-down percentage (14% → 19.3%). The $78 at qty 20 was validated unanimously by all 6 Wave 4 models.
- **Roll selection — 15" Cardinal Red and 30" TransferRite.** This item uses the **NEW** 15" × 50yd Cardinal Red roll (materials/3m-180mc-cardinal-red-15in.md) at $433.77/roll and the **NEW** 30" × 100yd TransferRite 582U (materials/transferrite-582u-30in.md) at $141.86/roll. The 24" Cardinal Red roll used by the 2.5 sq ft cluster is wrong for this item — it would waste 9.625" per row. The 24" TransferRite 582U used by the 2.5 sq ft cluster is also wrong — it only fits 1 label per row at this height, doubling the tape cost per label. The 30" tape enables 2-up nesting, which is required to amortize the tape cost.
- **Material cost build is feed-length-driven.** Both vinyl and tape cost are calculated from feed length (76.8125" = 2.1337 yd per label), not from label area. This is the same length-based method used for the small-format cluster, but the larger feed length produces a larger absolute material cost ($20.02 vs $8.74).
- **No first article requested or offered.** Sean did not request FA, and Pro Label did not offer one. The item ships directly at production-tier pricing on whatever quantity is ordered.
- **No MOQ on any item on this account.**
- **Incumbent context (Wave 3 finding).** All 6 Wave 3 buyer-simulation models referenced Pro Sign as the likely incumbent estimate at $55–$70 at qty 20. The $78 quote is above incumbent but justified by Pro Label's track record on this account (15 prior items quoted, FA-accepted baseline, zero-drama fulfillment). The "instant approval" cluster was at $78 (4 of 6), which is what we sent.
- **Mental model risk (Wave 3 finding).** All 6 Wave 3 models confirmed that this price becomes a permanent data point in Sean's pricing model. He will normalize $/sq ft across cut vinyl items going forward and apply this band ($11.03/sq ft at qty 20) to any future large-format requests. The structural separation between the 2.5 sq ft band and this large-format band must be explicit in the quote email if Sean asks.
- **Drawing context.** Drawing dated 02/26/07, Revision B. This is a long-lived production item — Elliott has been making this generic ELLIOTT brand wordmark for ~19 years before Pro Label took over the program. Pro Sign (incumbent) made it for them previously.

---
