sections.production:
## Process Steps

1. **File import/setup** — open the Elliott-supplied production-ready DWG, send to cutter. ~5 min mechanical. NOT billable labor (see `PRICING_RULES.md` §22).
2. **Cut** — Roland SG3-300 on 24" Cardinal Red stock. 5 labels across (5 × 4" = 20" across 24" roll, 4" clearance). Single continuous cut path for the "ElliottEquip.com" wordmark, repeated 5-up across the roll.
3. **Weed** — remove excess vinyl around the 16 mixed-case characters. Weeding considerations: 4 enclosed counters (e ×2, o ×1, p ×1), 2 isolated dots (period between "ElliottEquip" and "com", dot over lowercase "i"), tight descender channel on "q" adjacent to "u" stem. Each label requires careful weeding despite the small overall area.
4. **Mask** — apply TransferRite 582U transfer tape over weeded characters. 24" tape matches 24" vinyl exactly.
5. **Inspect** — verify cut accuracy, no lifted edges, character legibility across the 34.887" length, all enclosed counters cleanly removed, dots intact.
6. **Package** — flat, masked with transfer tape carrier, ready for hand-application.

## Production Notes

- **Lamination passes:** 0 (cut vinyl, no print)
- **Cut runs:** 1
- **Equipment:** Roland SG3-300 (24" Cardinal Red stock; 28" max cut width)
- **Weeding complexity:** Elevated relative to Band A model-designation items. 16 mixed-case characters at small scale, 4 enclosed counters, 2 isolated dots, tight q-u channel. Per-label weeding time is comparable to a Band A "E190" weeding pass despite the smaller sq ft, because the character count and enclosed-counter count are higher.
- **Cut feed length:** 40.887" per row of 5 labels. On the 50yd roll (1800" usable), 44 complete rows × 5 labels = 220 labels per roll.

---
sections.pricing_derivation:
**Methodology:** Founding data point for the **sub-1 sq ft cut vinyl band (Band C)** in the Cut Vinyl Pricing Profile. Validated through the full 4-wave AI validation process per `governance/VALIDATION_PROMPTS.md` (24 independent responses across 6 models × 4 waves) plus a final independent industry-validation audit (6 models). 4-wave verdict was UNANIMOUS YES on $20 at qty 20.

**Sq ft / $/sq ft comparison across the three cut vinyl bands:**

| Band | Representative Item | Sq Ft | Price (qty 20) | $/sq ft | Step from previous |
|------|---------------------|-------|----------------|---------|--------------------|
| Band B (Large-Format) | 3010704 | 7.069 | $78 | $11.03 | — |
| Band A (Small-Format) | 1205720 | 2.56 | $35 | $13.67 | +24% |
| **Band C (Sub-1 sq ft, new)** | **3010707 (this item)** | **0.969** | **$20** | **$20.64** | **+51%** |

Monotonically increasing $/sq ft as sq ft drops. No tier-level inversion at any quantity.

**Band C justification — 51% $/sq ft step-up from Band A:** Three structural drivers:

1. **Fixed-labor dominance on sub-1 sq ft items.** Setup, weeding, masking, inspection, and packaging take approximately the same time per label regardless of size. At 0.969 sq ft (2.64× smaller than Band A's 2.56 sq ft), these fixed costs amortize over much less area. Fixed-labor share of $/sq ft is structurally higher on sub-1 sq ft items.
2. **Elevated weeding complexity per sq ft.** "ElliottEquip.com" is 16 mixed-case characters with 4 enclosed counters (e ×2, o ×1, p ×1), 2 isolated dots, and a tight descender channel (q adjacent to u stem). Band A's "E190" / "E160" / "D115" / "ELLIOTT" designations are 3-7 uppercase block characters with open geometry — much faster weeding per sq ft.
3. **Higher material cost per sq ft.** $3.91/sq ft (Cardinal Red, worst-case) vs ~$3.41/sq ft on Band A. The 5-across nesting on 24" roll is efficient, but the smaller label area produces a higher $/sq ft material rate.

**Material cost build at Cardinal Red (worst-case anchor):**

| Component | Calculation | Cost |
|-----------|-------------|------|
| Vinyl (24" × 50yd Cardinal Red, $15.502/yd) | 1.1357 yd ÷ 5 labels/row × $15.502/yd | $3.52 |
| Application tape (24" × 100yd TransferRite 582U, $1.1821/yd) | 1.1357 yd ÷ 5 labels/row × $1.1821/yd | $0.27 |
| **Total per label** | | **$3.79** |

## 4-Wave Validation Record

**Wave 1 — Build (6 models, independent, atomic prompts):**
- Price range at qty 20: $16.50–$34.00. Three distinct clusters identified.
  - **Low cluster ($16.50–$19.00):** Proportional $/sq ft scaling from Band A. Models held the Band A $/sq ft rate and scaled by area only.
  - **Mid cluster ($22.00):** Power-law $/sq ft + complexity premium. Single model used a fitted curve from Bands A and B.
  - **High cluster ($31.00–$34.00):** Absolute labor recovery. Models built up from fixed minutes per label × shop rate, ignoring size relative to Band A.
- All 6 confirmed: $/sq ft must exceed Band A; fixed labor dominant at this size class; weeding complexity premium justified; monotonic $/sq ft curve required across all three bands; Cardinal Red worst-case anchor approach correct.
- All 6 confirmed $3.79 material cost (Cardinal Red worst case). All 6 confirmed $0 file prep.

**Wave 2 — Destruction (6 models, hostile attack vectors):**
- **Buyer/Procurement: H (6/6).** High cluster destroyed. $31–$34 triggers competitive bidding from Sean — he can pattern-match against the Band A $35/qty 20 (3.7× the sq ft, same price) and conclude Pro Label is gouging on small items.
- **Competitor: H (6/6).** Regional sign shop quotes for "ElliottEquip.com" at this geometry land at $13–$18 at qty 20 (incumbent Pro Sign reference). High cluster is uncompetitive.
- **Cost Auditor: M (6/6).** True material+labor cost ~$6–$9/label including weeding labor at this complexity. Low cluster margins defensible at ~60-65% true margin. Mid cluster healthy. High cluster has "air" beyond shop-rate justification.
- **Strategic: H (6/6).** High cluster creates an unsustainable precedent at the January 2027 Band A normalization. If Band A moves from $13.67/sq ft to $15–$16/sq ft, sub-1 sq ft items priced at $32/sq ft compress the band ratios in a way that becomes hard to defend.
- **Verdict: 5 No / 1 Yes-with-mods.** Convergence to $18–$22 at qty 20. Central tendency: $20.

**Wave 3 — Buyer Simulation (6 models as Sean Finn, Employee-Owner, $140K/year program, $/sq ft mental model):**
- All 6 approved the PO at $20/qty 20.
  - 5 of 6: as-is, no question.
  - 1 of 6: with a non-blocking parallel question ("how does this scale if we wanted to do this in larger formats?" — informational, not pricing).
- Pushback threshold: $22–$24/sq ft. Below that, Sean clears it without thinking.
- Instant approval: $18/sq ft (unanimous). $20.64/sq ft is just above the instant-approval line but well below the pushback line.
- All 6 logged **$20.64/sq ft as a permanent sub-1 sq ft anchor in their mental model.** Future quotes above this level will be challenged.

**Wave 4 — Final Synthesis (6 models, binary verdicts):**
- **6/6 YES on the full tier table $28 / $24 / $20 / $16.50 / $13.50 / $11.50.** UNANIMOUS.
- 4 of 6: send as-is, no modifications.
- 1 of 6: suggested raising 50-99 from $16.50 → $17.50 (not adopted — small change, no defense need).
- 1 of 6: suggested raising 200+ from $11.50 → $12.50 (not adopted — Sean's projected volumes don't reach 200+ frequently; no margin concern).
- No model rejected any tier. No model called for a $/sq ft change at qty 20.
- All 6 confirmed: $20.64/sq ft survives the January 2027 Band A normalization. When Band A moves to $15–$16/sq ft, the Band C premium compresses from 51% to 26–39% — **structurally better**, not worse.

## Final Industry Validation (6 models, independent audit)

After the 4-wave AI validation, an additional 6-model independent industry-validation audit was run against the locked tier table.

- **6/6 APPROVE, confidence HIGH.**
- $20.64/sq ft at qty 20 placed at the upper-middle of the specialty shop range for sub-1 sq ft cut vinyl ($17–$25/sq ft).
- Tier structure sound: 59% compression top-to-bottom, 14–18% step-downs at each tier. Within standard cut vinyl tier compression conventions.
- $/sq ft curve validated: 51% step-up Band A → Band C falls within expected 45–60% range for a 2.6× sq ft compression at this character/complexity profile.
- Margins appropriate for a specialty 2-person shop: 67–86.5% across tiers. Above the 50% structural floor at every tier including 200+.
- Multi-color identical pricing (3010707/3010708/3010709 all at the same tier table) confirmed as universal industry standard for color-variant runs of identical geometry.
- No structural risk to the $140K Elliott account from this price point.

**Nick's Final Decision:**
- Accepted the Wave 2/3/4 consensus tier table: **$28 / $24 / $20 / $16.50 / $13.50 / $11.50.**
- No modifications adopted from the two Wave 4 suggestions (both were soft "could" suggestions, not "must" findings).
- All three colors (3010707 Cardinal Red, 3010708 Black, 3010709 White) carry the identical tier table. Material cost differs by color; margin differs by color; price does NOT differ by color. Standard industry practice for multi-color variants of identical geometry. Cardinal Red is the worst-case material anchor; pricing decisions use the Cardinal Red figure.
- **No override type — pricing validated through full 4-wave process and Nick accepted the consensus.**

**AI Validation Summary:** 24 independent responses across 6 models × 4 waves. Wave 4 was UNANIMOUS YES on the tier table. Final industry validation: 6/6 APPROVE, confidence HIGH. Pricing locked.

**Profile Implication:** This item, together with 3010708 (Black) and 3010709 (White), establishes the **founding data point cluster for the sub-1 sq ft cut vinyl band (Band C)** at **$20.64/sq ft at qty 20**. Band C is INDEPENDENT of Bands A and B — the three bands do not contaminate or interact. The January 2027 normalization plan applies to Band A only, not Band C.

| Dimension | Value |
|-----------|-------|

| Label length (down roll) | 34.887" |

| Dimension | Value |
|-----------|-------|

sections.production:
## Process Steps

1. **File import/setup** — open the Elliott-supplied production-ready DWG, send to cutter. ~5 min mechanical. NOT billable labor (see `PRICING_RULES.md` §22).
2. **Cut** — Roland SG3-300 on 24" Black stock. 5 labels across (5 × 4" = 20" across 24" roll, 4" clearance). Single continuous cut path for the "ElliottEquip.com" wordmark, repeated 5-up across the roll.
3. **Weed** — remove excess vinyl around the 16 mixed-case characters. Weeding considerations: 4 enclosed counters (e ×2, o ×1, p ×1), 2 isolated dots (period between "ElliottEquip" and "com", dot over lowercase "i"), tight descender channel on "q" adjacent to "u" stem. Each label requires careful weeding despite the small overall area.
4. **Mask** — apply TransferRite 582U transfer tape over weeded characters. 24" tape matches 24" vinyl exactly.
5. **Inspect** — verify cut accuracy, no lifted edges, character legibility across the 34.887" length, all enclosed counters cleanly removed, dots intact.
6. **Package** — flat, masked with transfer tape carrier, ready for hand-application.

## Production Notes

- **Lamination passes:** 0 (cut vinyl, no print)
- **Cut runs:** 1
- **Equipment:** Roland SG3-300 (24" Black stock; 28" max cut width)
- **Weeding complexity:** Elevated relative to Band A model-designation items. 16 mixed-case characters at small scale, 4 enclosed counters, 2 isolated dots, tight q-u channel. Per-label weeding time is comparable to a Band A "E190" weeding pass despite the smaller sq ft, because the character count and enclosed-counter count are higher.
- **Cut feed length:** 40.887" per row of 5 labels. On the 50yd roll (1800" usable), 44 complete rows × 5 labels = 220 labels per roll.

---
sections.pricing_derivation:
**Methodology:** Color-parity pricing with the Band C founding data point (P/N 3010707, Cardinal Red). Identical dimensions, identical process, identical tier table. Color is the only variable; price does not differ by color. Standard industry practice for multi-color variants of identical geometry.

**Benchmark item:** P/N 3010707 (Cardinal Red) — the canonical pricing anchor for Band C. The 4-wave AI validation record and final industry validation are documented in full in `items/3010707.md`. P/N 3010707 carries the worst-case material cost ($3.79/label) and is the basis for all margin reasoning in this band.

**Why identical pricing across colors:** All three P/Ns (3010707 Cardinal Red, 3010708 Black, 3010709 White) cover the same drawing (D/3010707), the same dimensions (34.887" × 4" = 0.969 sq ft), the same cut/weed/mask process, the same weeding complexity (16 mixed-case characters, 4 enclosed counters, 2 dots, tight q-u channel), and the same nesting layout (5-up on 24" × 50yd roll). Material cost varies by color, but the labor profile, the nesting profile, and the buyer's $/sq ft mental model do not. Differential pricing by color on identical geometry would create a procurement-side problem and would not survive the Wave 3 buyer-simulation test (Sean expects identical prices for color-only differences on the same drawing).

**4-Wave AI Validation Summary** (full record in `items/3010707.md`):

| Wave | Outcome |
|------|---------|
| Wave 1 (Build) | $16.50–$34.00 at qty 20. Three clusters (Low/Mid/High). All 6 confirmed $/sq ft must exceed Band A; fixed labor dominant; weeding complexity premium justified; Cardinal Red worst-case anchor correct. |
| Wave 2 (Destruction) | High cluster destroyed by Buyer/Competitor/Strategic attacks. Cost Auditor moderate concerns on low cluster margins. **Convergence to $18–$22 at qty 20, central $20.** Verdict: 5 No / 1 Yes-with-mods on original tables. |
| Wave 3 (Buyer Sim) | All 6 approved PO at $20/qty 20 (5 as-is, 1 with non-blocking question). Pushback threshold $22–$24/sq ft; instant approval $18/sq ft. All 6 logged $20.64/sq ft as permanent sub-1 sq ft anchor. |
| Wave 4 (Final Synthesis) | **6/6 UNANIMOUS YES on the full tier table $28 / $24 / $20 / $16.50 / $13.50 / $11.50.** No tier rejected. All 6 confirmed $20.64/sq ft survives Jan 2027 Band A normalization (premium compresses 51% → 26–39%, structurally better). |

**Final industry validation:** 6/6 APPROVE, confidence HIGH. Multi-color identical pricing (3010707/3010708/3010709) confirmed as universal industry standard for color-variant runs of identical geometry.

**Material cost on this item (Black):**

| Component | Calculation | Cost |
|-----------|-------------|------|
| Vinyl (24" × 50yd Black, $13.1852/yd) | 1.1357 yd ÷ 5 labels/row × $13.1852/yd | $2.99 |
| Application tape (24" × 100yd TransferRite 582U, $1.1821/yd) | 1.1357 yd ÷ 5 labels/row × $1.1821/yd | $0.27 |
| **Total per label** | | **$3.26** |

**Margin on this item is structurally better than the Band C anchor (Cardinal Red).** Black material at $3.26/label vs Cardinal Red at $3.79/label produces ~3 points higher margin at every tier.

**Nick's Final Decision:** Accepted color-parity pricing — identical tier table to P/N 3010707. No override type — pricing validated through full 4-wave process on the band's anchor item.

**Profile Implication:** Founding data point cluster for the **sub-1 sq ft cut vinyl band (Band C)** at **$20.64/sq ft at qty 20**, together with 3010707 and 3010709. Band C is INDEPENDENT of Bands A and B.

| Spacing allocation per row | 3" lead-in + 3" trail-out = 6" |
| Feed length per row of 5 | 34.887" + 6" = **40.887" = 1.1357 yd** |
| Vinyl cost per row of 5 | 1.1357 yd × $12.3842/yd = $14.063 |
| Vinyl cost per label | $14.063 ÷ 5 = **$2.81** |

## Application Tape Nesting on 24" Roll

| Dimension | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Tape width | 24" (matches vinyl roll width exactly) |
| Feed length per row of 5 | 1.1357 yd (same as vinyl) |
| Tape cost per row of 5 | 1.1357 yd × $1.1821/yd = $1.342 |
| Tape cost per label | $1.342 ÷ 5 = **$0.27** |

## Total Material Cost per Label

| Component | Cost |
|-----------|------|
| Vinyl (3M 180mC-10 White, 24" × 50yd) | $2.81 |
| Application tape (TransferRite 582U, 24" × 100yd) | $0.27 |
| **Total material cost per label** | **$3.08** |

**Material cost per sq ft:** $3.08 ÷ 0.969 = $3.18/sq ft.

## Labels per Roll

- Rows per 50yd roll: floor(50 / 1.1357) = **44 complete rows**
- Labels per roll: 44 × 5 = **220 labels per roll**

---
sections.production:
## Process Steps

1. **File import/setup** — open the Elliott-supplied production-ready DWG, send to cutter. ~5 min mechanical. NOT billable labor (see `PRICING_RULES.md` §22).
2. **Cut** — Roland SG3-300 on 24" White stock. 5 labels across (5 × 4" = 20" across 24" roll, 4" clearance). Single continuous cut path for the "ElliottEquip.com" wordmark, repeated 5-up across the roll.
3. **Weed** — remove excess vinyl around the 16 mixed-case characters. Weeding considerations: 4 enclosed counters (e ×2, o ×1, p ×1), 2 isolated dots (period between "ElliottEquip" and "com", dot over lowercase "i"), tight descender channel on "q" adjacent to "u" stem. Each label requires careful weeding despite the small overall area.
4. **Mask** — apply TransferRite 582U transfer tape over weeded characters. 24" tape matches 24" vinyl exactly.
5. **Inspect** — verify cut accuracy, no lifted edges, character legibility across the 34.887" length, all enclosed counters cleanly removed, dots intact.
6. **Package** — flat, masked with transfer tape carrier, ready for hand-application.

## Production Notes

- **Lamination passes:** 0 (cut vinyl, no print)
- **Cut runs:** 1
- **Equipment:** Roland SG3-300 (24" White stock; 28" max cut width)
- **Weeding complexity:** Elevated relative to Band A model-designation items. 16 mixed-case characters at small scale, 4 enclosed counters, 2 isolated dots, tight q-u channel. Per-label weeding time is comparable to a Band A "E190" weeding pass despite the smaller sq ft, because the character count and enclosed-counter count are higher.
- **Cut feed length:** 40.887" per row of 5 labels. On the 50yd roll (1800" usable), 44 complete rows × 5 labels = 220 labels per roll.

---
sections.pricing_derivation:
**Methodology:** Color-parity pricing with the Band C founding data point (P/N 3010707, Cardinal Red). Identical dimensions, identical process, identical tier table. Color is the only variable; price does not differ by color. Standard industry practice for multi-color variants of identical geometry.

**Benchmark item:** P/N 3010707 (Cardinal Red) — the canonical pricing anchor for Band C. The 4-wave AI validation record and final industry validation are documented in full in `items/3010707.md`. P/N 3010707 carries the worst-case material cost ($3.79/label) and is the basis for all margin reasoning in this band.

**Why identical pricing across colors:** All three P/Ns (3010707 Cardinal Red, 3010708 Black, 3010709 White) cover the same drawing (D/3010707), the same dimensions (34.887" × 4" = 0.969 sq ft), the same cut/weed/mask process, the same weeding complexity (16 mixed-case characters, 4 enclosed counters, 2 dots, tight q-u channel), and the same nesting layout (5-up on 24" × 50yd roll). Material cost varies by color, but the labor profile, the nesting profile, and the buyer's $/sq ft mental model do not. Differential pricing by color on identical geometry would create a procurement-side problem and would not survive the Wave 3 buyer-simulation test (Sean expects identical prices for color-only differences on the same drawing).

**4-Wave AI Validation Summary** (full record in `items/3010707.md`):

| Wave | Outcome |
|------|---------|
| Wave 1 (Build) | $16.50–$34.00 at qty 20. Three clusters (Low/Mid/High). All 6 confirmed $/sq ft must exceed Band A; fixed labor dominant; weeding complexity premium justified; Cardinal Red worst-case anchor correct. |
| Wave 2 (Destruction) | High cluster destroyed by Buyer/Competitor/Strategic attacks. Cost Auditor moderate concerns on low cluster margins. **Convergence to $18–$22 at qty 20, central $20.** Verdict: 5 No / 1 Yes-with-mods on original tables. |
| Wave 3 (Buyer Sim) | All 6 approved PO at $20/qty 20 (5 as-is, 1 with non-blocking question). Pushback threshold $22–$24/sq ft; instant approval $18/sq ft. All 6 logged $20.64/sq ft as permanent sub-1 sq ft anchor. |
| Wave 4 (Final Synthesis) | **6/6 UNANIMOUS YES on the full tier table $28 / $24 / $20 / $16.50 / $13.50 / $11.50.** No tier rejected. All 6 confirmed $20.64/sq ft survives Jan 2027 Band A normalization (premium compresses 51% → 26–39%, structurally better). |

**Final industry validation:** 6/6 APPROVE, confidence HIGH. Multi-color identical pricing (3010707/3010708/3010709) confirmed as universal industry standard for color-variant runs of identical geometry.

**Material cost on this item (White):**

| Component | Calculation | Cost |
|-----------|-------------|------|
| Vinyl (24" × 50yd White, $12.3842/yd) | 1.1357 yd ÷ 5 labels/row × $12.3842/yd | $2.81 |
| Application tape (24" × 100yd TransferRite 582U, $1.1821/yd) | 1.1357 yd ÷ 5 labels/row × $1.1821/yd | $0.27 |
| **Total per label** | | **$3.08** |

**Margin on this item is structurally the best in the Band C cluster.** White material at $3.08/label vs Cardinal Red at $3.79/label produces ~3.5 points higher margin at every tier. White is the lowest-cost color in the band.

**Nick's Final Decision:** Accepted color-parity pricing — identical tier table to P/N 3010707. No override type — pricing validated through full 4-wave process on the band's anchor item.

**Profile Implication:** Founding data point cluster for the **sub-1 sq ft cut vinyl band (Band C)** at **$20.64/sq ft at qty 20**, together with 3010707 and 3010708. Band C is INDEPENDENT of Bands A and B.

sections.pricing_derivation:
> **⚠ ONE-OFF ORDER. NOT A CATALOG PRICE. NOT A BENCHMARK.** Qty 6 for a unit in the field. The $9.17/label number is a job-economics floor sliced out of the $55 account minimum-worthwhile-charge program total — it must NEVER be used as a comparable, anchor, or benchmark for any future Elliott item, including future tiny labels, mid-size labels, or one-off requests. Future one-offs price from their own job economics (material + production time), not from this number.

**Methodology:** Job economics for a one-off small-batch run. Built from material cost + production time, anchored to the account's one-off job-economics floor (1230820 FA-anchored).

**Job inputs:**

| Component | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Material cost (full 6-label job) | ~$1.33 (0.5 sq ft Orajet 3951 + 0.5 sq ft laminate at $0.2389/sq ft + ~$0.60 ink for 3-color ANSI Z535 — laminate updated 2026-05-28) |
| File prep / artwork prep / design time / pre-press | **$0** — Elliott supplies production-ready DWG (account-level rule, see `PRICING_RULES.md` §22) |
| Production time (file import + print/lam/cut/inspect/package) | ~20-25 min total (5 min file import + 15-20 min run) |
| Lamination passes | 1 |
| Cut runs | 1 |

**Why the sq ft band is inapplicable.** At 0.026 sq ft, this label is well outside the ~0.5–2.0 sq ft singles band scope. Applying the singles band ($15.43/sq ft at qty 20) would yield $0.40/label — does not cover the run setup and inspection time. The band applies to mid-size production singles, not one-off small-batch jobs of this size.

**Why pricing anchors to the $55 account floor.** $55 is the lowest first article price on the account (P/N 1230820 FA) and serves as a program-level one-off job-economics floor (1230820 FA-anchored) — the lowest number Pro Label will run a one-off custom job for, regardless of how small. Anchoring at this floor:

- Program revenue: $55
- Material cost: ~$1.70
- Production time: ~20-25 min
- Contribution margin: ~$53 to cover ~20-25 minutes of machine + operator time + overhead
- That is the worthwhile-charge threshold for a 6-label one-off at this scale; below it the job is not worth running

**Why this is a one-off, not a catalog item.** Qty 6 for a single unit in the field. No recurring production runway. The tier table is flat at $9.17 for frontmatter completeness only — there is no volume curve when there is no expected volume.

**Why this is NOT a benchmark:**

- One-off, not catalog. No reorder runway.
- The per-sq-ft implied rate (~$352/sq ft) is the arithmetic byproduct of dividing a one-off job-economics floor (1230820 FA-anchored) by a small label area — structurally meaningless as a per-sq-ft reference.
- The per-label rate ($9.17) is the arithmetic byproduct of dividing $55 by 6 — it scales inversely with order quantity and is not a property of the label itself.
- Using this item to anchor a future quote would either over-price (if the future job has more labels to amortize $55 across) or under-price (if the future job has fewer labels or different setup).
- Future one-off small-batch jobs must rebuild job economics from scratch on their actual footprint and order quantity, then anchor to whichever account floor is appropriate at the time. Do NOT shortcut through this item.

**Tier structure:** All six tiers flattened to $9.17. There is no volume tier for a one-off — qty 6, no reorder. The flat tier table is a structural placeholder; the operational price is the program total of $55.

**Profile Validation:** Outside the Printed + Laminated singles Pricing Profile band (~0.5–2.0 sq ft scope) by design. The **price itself is NOT a Profile data point** and must NOT be fed into the singles band, the kit band, or any other band.

**AI Validation:** None. Per session brief — straightforward one-off job-economics call with no precedent-setting risk to existing bands. The four-round, six-model validation methodology is reserved for items that set precedent or could affect the existing pricing structure; this item is explicitly excluded from being a benchmark.

**Override:** None.

| Qty Tier | Price | Material Cost | Gross Profit | Margin |
|----------|-------|---------------|-------------|--------|
| 1-9 | $9.17 | $0.22 | $8.95 | ~98% |
| 10-19 | $9.17 | $0.22 | $8.95 | ~98% |

| Job-Level Metric | Value |
|------------------|-------|

| Gross margin (revenue − material) | ~$53.67 ≈ 98% of revenue |

**Interpretation:** Margin % is structurally inflated because the price floor is set by the worthwhile-charge floor, not by a catalog rate. Contribution per production-minute (~$145/hr) is healthy for a one-off small-batch custom job. The internal P&L benchmark (~79.7%) is a recurring-production margin and is not directly comparable to a one-off — this $55 program has no repeat-run amortization to lean on, and its economics are best evaluated as contribution per machine-minute rather than as a percentage.

sections.nesting:
## Production Footprint (estimated for qty 6 of a 0.5" × 0.5" label)

A 6-label run of a single 0.5" × 0.5" design nests as a tiny print/lam/cut strip on the Roland SG3-300. The labels themselves cover only 1.5 sq in (~0.01 sq ft); the operational footprint is driven by margins, registration marks, and the minimum manageable strip width through the laminator.

| Resource | Footprint |
|----------|-----------|
| Label area (6 × 0.5" × 0.5") | 1.5 sq in = 0.010 sq ft |
| Print vinyl (Orajet 3951) with bleeds, margins, registration | ~0.20 sq ft |
| Laminate (1-mil polyester) | ~0.20 sq ft |
| Lamination passes | 1 (single pass through 13.5" laminator — all 6 labels in one nest) |
| Print passes | 1 |
| Labels produced | 6 |

## Material Cost — Entire Job

Costs reference `governance/PRODUCTION.md` (verified 2026-04-22).

| Component | Calculation | Cost |
|-----------|-------------|------|
| Vinyl (Orajet 3951) | 0.20 sq ft × $1.21/sq ft | **$0.24** |
| Laminate (1-mil polyester) | 0.20 sq ft × $0.2389/sq ft | **$0.05** |
| Ink (eco-solvent, low coverage — single line of 0.1" Helvetica Bold) | 6 labels × ~$0.01/label | **$0.06** |
| **Total material cost for the full 6-label job** | | **~$0.35** |

## Material Cost per Unit

| Unit | Calculation | Cost |
|------|-------------|------|
| Per label | $0.35 ÷ 6 | **~$0.06** |
| Per sq ft of label area (informational only) | $0.35 ÷ 0.010 sq ft | ~$35.00/sq ft |
| Per sq ft of production footprint (informational only) | $0.35 ÷ 0.20 sq ft | ~$1.75/sq ft |

> Frontmatter `material_cost_per_unit` carries the per-label figure ($0.06). At this size the material cost is effectively zero relative to the $55 job floor — it is documented for completeness and audit, not as a price driver. Laminate updated 2026-05-28 to $0.2389/sq ft (Flexcon FLX000233 invoice verified).

---
sections.production:
> **No file prep / artwork prep / design time / pre-press in this process.** Elliott supplies a production-ready DWG. Step 1 is a ~5-minute mechanical file import, not labor. See `PRICING_RULES.md` §22.

## Process Steps (~20-25 minutes total job time for the 6-label run)

1. **File import/setup** — open the Elliott-supplied production-ready DWG, send to RIP. ~5 min mechanical.
2. **Print** — Roland SG3-300, eco-solvent on Orajet 3951. 6 labels nested in a single tiny print strip; black text only.
3. **Laminate** — 1-mil clear polyester overlay, single pass through the 13.5" laminator.
4. **Contour cut** — Roland contour cut with R1/8 rounded corners per drawing spec; backing slit at middle.
5. **Inspect** — print quality, laminate adhesion, cut accuracy, text legibility on a 0.5" × 0.5" footprint.
6. **Package** — flat, individually cut, ready for hand application on equipment. Backing slit for application.

Steps 2-6 total ~15-20 minutes for the 6-label job. Combined with the 5-minute file import, total job time is ~20-25 minutes.

## Production Notes

- **Lamination passes:** 1
- **Cut runs:** 1
- **Equipment:** Roland SG3-300 + 13.5" polyester laminator (same equipment as all Orajet 3951 work)
- **Tolerance note:** At 0.5" × 0.5", ±1/16" tolerance is meaningful relative to label size. Inspect contour cut closely.

sections.pricing_derivation:
> **⚠ ONE-OFF ORDER. NOT A CATALOG PRICE. NOT A BENCHMARK.** Qty 6 for a field service request. The $9.17/label number is a job-economics floor sliced out of the $55 account minimum-worthwhile-charge program total — it must NEVER be used as a comparable, anchor, or benchmark for any future Elliott item, including future tiny labels, mid-size labels, or one-off requests. Future one-offs price from their own job economics (material + production time), not from this number.

**Methodology:** Job economics for a one-off small-batch run. Built from material cost + production time, anchored to the account's one-off job-economics floor (1230820 FA-anchored).

**Job inputs:**

| Component | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Material cost (full 6-label job) | ~$0.35 (~0.20 sq ft Orajet 3951 + ~0.20 sq ft laminate at $0.2389/sq ft + ~$0.06 ink — laminate updated 2026-05-28) |
| File prep / artwork prep / design time / pre-press | **$0** — Elliott supplies production-ready DWG (account-level rule, see `PRICING_RULES.md` §22) |
| Production time (file import + print/lam/cut/inspect/package) | ~20-25 min total (5 min file import + 15-20 min run) |
| Lamination passes | 1 |
| Cut runs | 1 |

**(a) Why the sq ft band is inapplicable.** At 0.00174 sq ft, this label is the smallest item on the account and is far outside the ~0.5–2.0 sq ft singles band scope. Applying the singles band ($15.43/sq ft at qty 20) would yield $0.027/label — does not cover even the file import, let alone print/lam/cut/inspect/package. The band applies to mid-size production singles, not one-off small-batch jobs of this size.

**(b) Why pricing anchors to the $55 account floor.** $55 is the lowest first article price on the account (P/N 1230820 FA) and serves as a program-level one-off job-economics floor (1230820 FA-anchored) — the lowest number Pro Label will run a one-off custom job for, regardless of how small. Same logic applied to the outrigger switch program (1277970/80/90/1278000) and to P/N 3017583. Anchoring at this floor:

- Program revenue: $55
- Material cost: ~$0.50
- Production time: ~20-25 min
- Contribution margin: ~$54.50 to cover ~20-25 minutes of machine + operator time + overhead
- That is the worthwhile-charge threshold for a 6-label one-off at this scale; below it the job is not worth running

**(c) Why this is a one-off, not a catalog item.** Qty 6 for a field service request, arrived alongside P/N 3017583 as part of the same field service request (next-day rush). No recurring production runway. The tier table is flat at $9.17 for frontmatter completeness only — there is no volume curve when there is no expected volume.

**(d) File prep is $0 per account rule.** Elliott supplies a production-ready DWG. File prep, artwork prep, design time, and pre-press are not cost inputs on this account. See `PRICING_RULES.md` §22.

**(e) Mylar = polyester terminology note.** Drawing callout "1 mil clear Mylar overlay" — Mylar is a polyester-film trade name; this maps to the standard 1-mil polyester laminate used across the Orajet 3951 family on this account. No material change; documentation alignment only.

**Field-service context (relationship to 3017583).** P/N 3017583 (LBL - PTO Engage Process, 2.5" × 1.5") and P/N 3017584 (LBL - PTO Active, 0.5" × 0.5") arrived together as part of the same next-day-rush field service request, qty 6 each. They are **separate line items**, not a kit — different P/Ns, different sizes, different content, different drawings, applied at different locations on the equipment. Each is priced as its own one-off at the $55 account floor. Quoting them as two separate $55 programs (total $110) is correct; quoting as a single combined $55 program would be incorrect because each requires its own file import + print/lam/cut/inspect/package run.

**Why this is NOT a benchmark:**

- One-off, not catalog. No reorder runway.
- The per-sq-ft implied rate (~$5,270/sq ft) is the arithmetic byproduct of dividing a one-off job-economics floor (1230820 FA-anchored) by an extremely small label area — structurally meaningless as a per-sq-ft reference, and the most extreme such artifact on the account.
- The per-label rate ($9.17) is the arithmetic byproduct of dividing $55 by 6 — it scales inversely with order quantity and is not a property of the label itself.
- Using this item to anchor a future quote would either over-price (if the future job has more labels to amortize $55 across) or under-price (if the future job has fewer labels or different setup).
- Future one-off small-batch jobs must rebuild job economics from scratch on their actual footprint and order quantity, then anchor to whichever account floor is appropriate at the time. Do NOT shortcut through this item.

**Tier structure:** All six tiers flattened to $9.17. There is no volume tier for a one-off — qty 6, no reorder. The flat tier table is a structural placeholder; the operational price is the program total of $55.

**Profile Validation:** Outside the Printed + Laminated singles Pricing Profile band (~0.5–2.0 sq ft scope) by design. The **price itself is NOT a Profile data point** and must NOT be fed into the singles band, the kit band, or any other band.

**AI Validation:** None. Per session brief — straightforward one-off job-economics call with no precedent-setting risk to existing bands. The four-round, six-model validation methodology is reserved for items that set precedent or could affect the existing pricing structure; this item is explicitly excluded from being a benchmark.

**Override:** None.

sections.margin_analysis:
> **N/A in the conventional recurring-production sense — this is a one-off priced at a flat $55 total, not a catalog item.** See Pricing Derivation for full job-cost reasoning. The margin table below is structural; the operationally meaningful metric is contribution per production-minute.

| Qty Tier | Price | Material Cost | Gross Profit | Margin |
|----------|-------|---------------|-------------|--------|
| 1-9 | $9.17 | $0.06 | $9.11 | ~99% |
| 10-19 | $9.17 | $0.06 | $9.11 | ~99% |
| 20-49 | $9.17 | $0.06 | $9.11 | ~99% |
| 50-99 | $9.17 | $0.06 | $9.11 | ~99% |
| 100-199 | $9.17 | $0.06 | $9.11 | ~99% |
| 200+ | $9.17 | $0.06 | $9.11 | ~99% |

| Job-Level Metric | Value |
|------------------|-------|
| Program revenue (6 labels) | $55.00 |
| Material cost (full job) | ~$0.35 |
| Production time | ~20-25 min |
| File prep / artwork prep | $0 (Elliott provides production-ready DWG) |
| Gross margin (revenue − material) | ~$54.65 ≈ 99% of revenue |
| Contribution per minute of production time | ~$54.65 ÷ ~22 min ≈ $2.48/min ≈ $149/hr |

**Interpretation:** Margin % is structurally inflated because the price floor is set by the worthwhile-charge floor, not by a catalog rate, and because material is near-zero at this size. Contribution per production-minute (~$149/hr) is healthy for a one-off small-batch custom job. The internal P&L benchmark (~79.7%) is a recurring-production margin and is not directly comparable to a one-off — this $55 program has no repeat-run amortization to lean on, and its economics are best evaluated as contribution per machine-minute rather than as a percentage.

sections.nesting:
## Roll Specs

| Spec | Value |
|------|-------|
| Roll | 3M Controltac 180mC-57 Olympic Blue, 24" × 10yd |
| Roll cost | $162.78 |
| Roll sq in | 24" × 360" = 8,640 sq in |
| Cost per sq in | $162.78 ÷ 8,640 = $0.018839/sq in |
| Cost per sq ft | $162.78 ÷ 60 sq ft = $2.713/sq ft |
| Cost per yard | $162.78 ÷ 10 yd = $16.278/yd |

## Nesting Layout on 24" Roll

| Dimension | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Label width (across roll) | 11.00" |
| Labels across 24" roll | 2 (2 × 11" = 22", leaves 2" waste strip) |
| Label length (down roll) | 32.88" = 0.9133 yd |
| Positions along 360" roll | 10 (360 ÷ 32.88 = 10.95 → 10 complete) |
| **Labels per roll** | **20 (2 across × 10 positions)** |
| Unused at roll end | 360" − (10 × 32.88") = 31.2" |
| Roll-end waste | 8.7% of roll length |
| Side waste | 2" strip (9.1% of roll width) |

> **Yield Note:** The session brief indicated "10 labels per roll (2 across × 5 positions)." Verified math: 360" ÷ 32.88" = 10.95 → 10 full positions. Correct yield is **20 labels per roll**. Material cost per label (calculated from $/sq in × allocated area) is unaffected by this discrepancy.

## Material Cost per Label

**Vinyl (length-based, includes blade path allocation):**

| Step | Value |
|------|-------|
| Roll width allocated per label | 24" ÷ 2 = 12" (2-across layout) |
| Label length | 32.88" = 0.9133 yd |
| Cost per row of 2 labels | 0.9133 yd × $16.278/yd = $14.864 |
| Vinyl cost per label | $14.864 ÷ 2 = **$7.43** |

**Application Tape (length-based):**

| Step | Value |
|------|-------|
| Feed length per row | 32.88" + 6" spacing = 38.88" = 1.0800 yd |
| Tape cost per row of 2 labels | 1.0800 yd × $1.1821/yd = $1.277 |
| Tape cost per label | $1.277 ÷ 2 = **$0.638** |

**Total Material Cost per Label: $7.433 + $0.638 = $8.071 → $8.07**

| Component | Cost |
|-----------|------|
| Vinyl (3M 180mC-57 Olympic Blue) | $7.433 |
| Application tape (TransferRite 582U) | $0.638 |
| **Total material cost per label** | **$8.071 → $8.07** |

> **Tape cost corrected 2026-06-09** from area method (pseudo-rate: 2.512 sq ft × $0.5911/sq ft = $1.485) to the length-based method for consistency with Band B, Band C, and 3010701. No sell price change.

> **No 48" roll option available for this color.** Olympic Blue is only stocked at 24" × 10yd. If higher volume warrants it, a 48" roll quote from 3M would reduce material cost — document as an operational improvement opportunity, not current pricing basis.

sections.production:
## Process Steps

1. **File import/setup** — open the Elliott-supplied production-ready DWG, send to cutter. ~5 min mechanical. NOT billable labor (see `PRICING_RULES.md` §22).
2. **Cut** — Roland SG3-300 on 24" Olympic Blue stock. 2 labels across, cut in rows. Estimated: 5-8 min per production run.
3. **Weed** — remove excess vinyl around "D115" block letters. 4 large characters, no fine detail, open geometry. Estimated: 10-15 min per unit at small quantities; 5-8 min/unit at production volume.
4. **Mask** — apply TransferRite 582U transfer tape over weeded characters. Squeegee for adhesion. Estimated: 3-5 min per label.
5. **Inspect** — verify cut accuracy, confirm no lifted edges, check character legibility.
6. **Package** — flat, masked with transfer tape carrier. Label is delivered ready for hand-application on equipment.

## Production Notes

- **Lamination passes:** 0 (cut vinyl, no print)
- **Cut runs:** 1
- **Equipment:** Roland SG3-300 (24" stock)
- **Weeding complexity:** Low. "D115" is 4 large block characters at generous scale. No serifs, no fine interior detail. Similar weeding profile to "E190" (P/N 1205720).
- **Weeding time:** Faster than the 7-character "ELLIOTT" brand lettering (P/N 3017435). Comparable to P/N 1205720.

## Estimated Production Time per Unit

| Step | Time (small qty) | Time (production vol) |
|------|-----------------|----------------------|
| File import/setup | ~5 min mechanical, NOT billable labor (see `PRICING_RULES.md` §22) | — |
| Cut | 8 min | 5 min |
| Weed | 15 min | 8 min |
| Mask (transfer tape) | 5 min | 3 min |
| Inspect + pack | 5 min | 3 min |
| **Per-unit billable total** | **~33 min** | **~19 min** |

**Profile Validation:** Within the Cut Vinyl Pricing Profile band (concession-phase band: ~$13.67/sq ft). AI consensus range for this category is $14.84-$16.41/sq ft — this item prices below that range, consistent with the relationship-concession phase pricing established by P/N 1205720.

**AI Validation:** Not separately run. Direct parity with an FA-accepted item at the same sq ft, same process, same material family. Per PRICING_VALIDATION.md, multi-round validation is exempt for items dimensionally and materially identical to an already-quoted item. Profile band validation still applies and was performed.

**Override:** None. Engine output.

Rule 14 deviation acknowledged: P/N 1205720 is a Relationship Concession. This item prices within the concession-phase band by deliberate strategic choice, not oversight. If/when relationship pricing normalizes, recalibrate against the AI consensus range ($14.84–$16.41/sq ft).

**Material cost difference from benchmark:** Olympic Blue ($162.78/roll for 10yd) vs Cardinal Red (24"×50yd $775.10) yields slightly higher material cost per label ($8.07 vs $7.88 — both length-based tape as of 2026-06-09). Both colors hit ~77% margin at qty 20 — no price change warranted. The tier structure has been validated by the market and the margin floor remains acceptable.

sections.notes:
- **PMS COLOR CAVEAT — must be disclosed in quote.** Drawing specifies PMS 2386 C. Olympic Blue (3M 180mC-57) is a visual approximation, not a certified Pantone match. Language for quote: *"Olympic Blue (3M 180mC-57) is the closest available stock color. This is a visual match, not a certified PMS 2386 C match. If an exact Pantone match is required, a custom color program or alternative approach would be needed — please advise."*
- **New material not previously stocked.** Olympic Blue (3M 180mC-57) has not been used on any prior Elliott item. If FA is ordered, ensure this roll is purchased before committing a ship date.
- **Material cost methodology.** This item uses the length-based vinyl cost calculation (0.9133 yd × $16.278/yd ÷ 2 = $7.43). The original 1205720 calculation used an area method with an incorrect $2.56/sq ft vinyl cost (that figure was the label area in sq ft, not a cost — a data entry error corrected in the 2026-05-22 audit). All cut vinyl items now use the length-based method. Future cut vinyl items must use the length-based approach. **Tape cost corrected 2026-06-09** from area method (pseudo-rate) to length-based method (feed length × $1.1821/yd ÷ labels per row) for consistency with Band B, Band C, and 3010701 — no sell price change.
- **Labels per roll: 20 (not 10).** Session brief contained a nesting arithmetic error (stated "5 positions" for 10.95 available). Verified: 20 labels per roll (2 across × 10 positions × 32.88" = 328.8" consumed, 31.2" waste at roll end).
- **No 48" roll available for Olympic Blue.** Cannot replicate the margin improvement seen on P/N 3017435 (White). Watch for volume patterns — if orders are frequent, request 48" roll pricing from 3M supplier.
- **Comparison to E190 (P/N 1205720).** Direct analog: same process, same dimensions (within <2% sq ft), same content type (4-character model designation). Sean should recognize this as consistent with existing pricing.
